<span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span></span></span><br /></span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_Parts_Ports_dialog1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></strong></p><p>The following procedure will guide you in working with the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you want to display required properties on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display Ports and Properties using the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog:</p><ul><li>When creating a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>, the dialog opens automatically.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li><li>When editing an already created <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br />- Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and choose <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></li><li><p>Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>If some <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>check boxes</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span> are inactive in the element list, </span></span></span>those elements are already <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>displayed</span></span></span> in the diagram.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the element tree<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the left side of the dialog:</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">-</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Manually select individual check boxes<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="927433d1-8a3b-4ca2-ad2e-d567bde15d2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All properties (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected recursively <span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span> from the element tree</span></span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested elements in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_nested_elements_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the context-sensitive panel<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on the right side of the dialog:</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> your own</span> stereotypes)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group - displays all Port types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including your own stereotypes)</span> that are owned by a selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.</span><br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.</span><br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree</span> located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a648a8-07dd-4136-9c82-c33cbd61bacb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All ports (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected <span>in <span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the element tree</span></span></span> by using the <span><strong>Select Ports</strong> <span>group</span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required properties."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_panel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: select the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.</li><li>Ports layout on Parts shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom.<br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link>, and hide all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>the </span><strong>Display</strong> button</span> and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the shortcut menu as</span></span> their functions are identical. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All those commands</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are interactive and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">only </span>the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">both the menu</span> shows<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> commands that can be applied</span> to the whole diagram.</span></span></p><p>These <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">comprised of </span></span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li>Metatypes.</li><li>Stereotypes.</li><li>Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li>Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Internal Structure of Workplace diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display Properties and/or Ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">Display Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b"> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span><span>After selecting the required command from the</span></span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span><span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_command_group_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide Ports</p><hr /><ul><li>Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the command, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">ports</span></span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span> ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></span></span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>Select<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> the required</span></span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">  From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>All <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span> Ports of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the selected shape</span> will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">   <ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports_SM_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> in the Composite Structure diagram</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> is by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> or Property elements. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span> properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures will show you:</span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Property shape and how to display all its Ports when dragging the Class element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">Ports</span> by dragging the Property element.</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a new Property by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Model Browser</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane</span>. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p>To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element</p><hr /><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane</span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol </strong>command.</span></span></span><br />The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span></span> </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d33f1ab5-07e0-42c6-8681-25e8e07df1d5">To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d407cdbc-1a05-485b-9d7d-bd7aec91f448"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command and </span></span>dragging (<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">long drag</span></span>) the Class element to <em>Internal Structure of Workplace</em> diagram pane, it </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">creates a new Property typed by a </span><em>Duplex Power Outlet</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Class and displays all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_class_on-diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane. If this property has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).</span></span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by dragging the Property element.</span></p><p>To display a Property by dragging a Property element</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Create Property Symbol </strong></span>command.</span><br />The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21aea531-8fce-41e3-a048-c759c72d59e2">To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.</span><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00be8f45-9eb5-4f84-b3c5-e89d3731db2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to</span><em> Internal Structure of Workplace</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Port</h3><p>If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.</p><p>To enable Part compartments on the Port shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port shape whose type has properties.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li>Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> Port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cec12941-2515-4aab-8522-c5a866c3eec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567711109</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link>  </li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page are:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Composite structure diagram </strong>sample model. To open this sample do one of the following: download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> </em>or f</span>ind in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li><li>The <strong>Vehicle parts under ports</strong> sample model. To open this sample d<span class="confluence-link"><em>ownload <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674883 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00408: Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

- page_id: `169674883`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674883/Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:51:56.697+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581472387

581472391

581472390

Before using this feature, make sure:

- The diagram with aspect is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram aspects' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How aspects match with parts and ports' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical *Data Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the **All By Aspect** button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected *control Panel* part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581472386

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model, which you can find:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcd8e89b-1f9f-408a-b6f6-affd9427300f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472387</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df8ea34-f6f7-4cf8-a5ad-f360b21222b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee8920-b70e-421a-aeb8-05dcb0da208a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before using this feature, make sure:</p><ul><li>The diagram with aspect is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The main workflow of using aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="How aspects match with parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Matching rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Data Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">All By Aspect</strong> button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">control Panel</em> part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="all_by_aspect.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a59f1e42-587f-49d8-9347-83280f66505c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model, which you can find:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674578 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: Displaying paths

- page_id: `169674578`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674578/Displaying+paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:39:23.191+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1107335505

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1107335508

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1107335507

This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MD2024xR1']

displayedTo display paths between selected element shapes

1. On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display In-Between Paths .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display In-Between Paths .

- 
  - On the main menu, click the **Analyze** > **Display In-Between Paths**. The **Display In-Between Paths** command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.

The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane. 
Paths to self also will not be displayed.

not_displayedTo display all existing paths of the selected shape

1. On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.
2. Do one of the following: All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.
  1. 
    - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display All Paths .
    - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display All Paths .
  - 
    - On the main menu, click the Analyze > Display All Paths .

#### TIP: Example of the Display All Paths command

Example of the Display All Paths command

This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.

The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the *Room infrastructure model* Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to visualize how the *Workplace* Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the**Display All Paths** command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After selecting the**Display All Paths** command, all paths between the *Workplace* Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the *Room* consists of between one and an arbitrary number of *Workplaces*. The *Workplace* consists of *Electric Extension* and *Works Station*. Also, the *Workplace* can contain *Duplex Power Outlet*, *External HDD2*, *Digital Camera*, and *External_HDD*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Options' space='MD2024xR1']**Environment Options** dialog , find the Display Paths on Element Drop option and set it's value to true .
3. Click OK .
4. Select the elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] and drag them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1']. The shapes and paths between them are displayed. WarningThe paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.

1107335503

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='MD2024xR1']

**Related page in Developer Guide**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='MD2024xR1']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37dd8375-c197-4b6f-bf42-a27572384477"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="176da447-7b42-4d75-bab2-b4002a3a64ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d1c89-9923-40a6-a69a-5d355d870cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying paths only between selected element shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="not_displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying all existing paths among the shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a5c1a99-dc96-41e3-bbf0-54960c3255f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display paths between selected element shapes</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.</p></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df015cf7-dece-418b-bf0b-e0e34099a919"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong> command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13128f02-7f4d-4518-a6cf-efd94ab17c80"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.<br />Paths to self also will not be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5110f86-ce4d-44a9-86c7-8b422d41d784"><ac:parameter ac:name="">not_displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display all existing paths of the selected shape</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul>All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1f11cf-477d-4351-82cb-af2526300618"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of the Display All Paths command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the <em>Room infrastructure model</em> Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relations_node_and_class_shapes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">If you want to visualize how the <em>Workplace</em> Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">After selecting the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command, all paths between the <em>Workplace</em> Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the <em>Room</em> consists of between one and an arbitrary number of <em>Workplaces</em>. The <em>Workplace</em> consists of <em>Electric Extension</em> and <em>Works Station</em>. Also, the <em>Workplace</em> can contain <em>Duplex Power Outlet</em>, <em>External HDD2</em>, <em>Digital Camera</em>, and <em>External_HDD</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:18.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Environment Options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, find the <strong>Display Paths on Element Drop</strong> option and set it's value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> and drag them on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The shapes and paths between them are displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99aac734-b4b5-4ee4-afa4-790fc249f3f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="977a11dd-971c-41cd-8a46-e2cd416913fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with paths and relationships" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related page in Developer Guide</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Paths" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Paths]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Composite structure diagram</span></strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></span></span></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\diagrams</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">composite structure diagram</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675769 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00410: Displaying rake icon

- page_id: `169675769`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675769/Displaying+rake+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-12-29T14:04:13.339+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Displaying icon or image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.

The rake icon is shown on:

- Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.
- Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.
- Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.
- Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.
- Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter.
2. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use <ac:image ac:title="Rake" ac:alt="Rake"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.</p><p>The rake icon is shown on:</p><ul><li>Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.</li><li>Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.</li><li>Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.</li><li>Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.</li><li>Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Rake icon on symbol" ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674571 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00411: Displaying related elements

- page_id: `169674571`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674571/Displaying+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:12:01.191+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 3

567913020

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 3

567913022

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 3

567913021

When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.

After selecting the **Display Related Elements** command, the **Display Related Elements** dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):

- Element box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.
- Relations area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.
- Expand Relations area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.
- Scope area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.
- Depth area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.
- Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.
- Layout related elements option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following procedure will guide you through using the **Display Related Elements** command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.

To display related elements

1. Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.
2. Open the **Display Related Elements** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Related Elements .
  - On the diagram toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements.png'] , and select Display Related Elements .
  - On the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Display Related Elements**. ImportantThe **Display Related Elements** dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements. Example 1A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named *Shipment*. It is showing the need to display related elements of the *Shipment* Class in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened dialog, specify:
  - In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.
  - In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships: - Incoming - in which the element participates as client. - Outgoing - in which the element participates as supplier. - Both- in which the element participates as both client and supplier.
  - In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found: - Whole Project - search in the whole model. - Package - search in the package under which the selected element is created.
  - In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships: - Indefinite - search for all possible relationships. - Definite - search for a specified level of relationships.
4. Select the check box of the Always create new symbols option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.
5. Select the check box of the **Layout related elements**option to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024xR1'] new symbols by using the default diagram [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024xR1']. NoteThe position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the **Layout related elements** check box. Example 2For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the *Shipment* Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing. 
- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project. 
- The search depth of the relationship is one level.If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**. 
According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane. Example 3After opening the **Display Related Elements** dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (*VendorShipment* and *CustomerShipment*) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the *Product Shipment object diagram* pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567913019

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements in the diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] (Open API)

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Inventory Control System** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Inventory Control System.mdzip']
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd0de44d-5c52-4f80-86ba-5a1f0ff0f08d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3219c483-b7a5-487a-a500-a3747eab5873"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaf9f520-5faf-4bec-8e92-1e996989277e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913021</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After selecting the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command, the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog opens.
````
