The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.</p><p> </p><p>To display related elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select<strong> Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and select <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8506d135-0ccf-4672-bb3e-67a2276a84cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ae0b19-4229-488a-b7c0-35a3f7dd9599"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named <em>Shipment</em>. It is showing the need to display related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="shortcut_menu_display_related_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>In the opened dialog, specify:<br /><ul><li>In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.</li><li>In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships:<br />- <strong>Incoming</strong> - in which the element participates as client.<br />- <strong>Outgoing</strong> - in which the element participates as supplier.<br />- <strong>Both-</strong>in which the element participates as both client and supplier.</li><li>In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found:<br />-<strong> Whole Project</strong> - search in the whole model.<br />- <strong>Package </strong>- search in the package under which the selected element is created.</li><li>In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships:<br />-<strong> Indefinite</strong> - search for all possible relationships.<br />- <strong>Definite</strong> - search for a specified level of relationships.</li></ul></li><li>Select the check box of the <strong>Always create new symbols</strong> option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Select the check box of the <strong>Layout related elements </strong>option to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> new symbols by using the default diagram <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca55d086-99f0-4658-b091-e71aff3855e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the <strong>Layout related elements</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d05eaf9-951d-4f91-a2e3-71340aa6934f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:</p><p>- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing.<br />- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project.<br />- The search depth of the relationship is one level.</p><p>If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_of_class_shipment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d1e5e9e-6684-4fd6-b528-046e6f11e36c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After opening the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (<em>VendorShipment</em> and <em>CustomerShipment</em>) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em> pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane." ac:alt="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="related_elements_of_the_shipment_class_displayed_on_the_diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f98c1d05-b3bb-46e6-ba17-e4ac3025074d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Layout" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements in the diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying related elements in the diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (Open API)</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Inventory Control System</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inventory Control System.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Inventory Control System.mdzip.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> </em></li><li>Find in modeling tool<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674345 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00412: Displaying row elements hierarchically

- page_id: `169674345`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674345/Displaying+row+elements+hierarchically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T14:51:47.369+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Hierarchy in tables
- labels: ['nested-instances', 'display-nested-elements', 'display-nested-instances', 'display-mode', 'complete-tree', 'comapct-tree', 'specify-query', 'hierarchy-in-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hierarchical tables are very useful for managing and reviewing data. Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.

**Important**

The hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024xR1'] is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.

##### Displaying elements hierarchically in a tableDisplaying elements hierarchically

To display elements hierarchically

1. In the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. Under the Display Mode option choose: The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree.
  - **Complete tree** to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Compact tree**to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying elements in custom hierarchyDisplaying row elements in a custom hierarchy

To display elements in a custom hierarchy

1. In the Criteria area, next to the Scope box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='scope_query.png'] icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_query_button.png']
2. Specify the expression to collect table elements:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

If you have selected Custom, please [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying expression in the Query dialog

##### Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table

To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] / [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the name column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674342 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00413: Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row

- page_id: `169674342`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674342/Displaying+hiding+element+numbers+in+a+row
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-29T08:06:50.336+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.

To display/hide element numbers in the table

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. Set the **Show Element Number** property value to *true* to display element numbers, or *false* to hide them*.*The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.</p><p>To display/hide element numbers in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the table.</li><li><p>Set the <strong>Show Element Number</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to display element numbers, or <em>false</em> to hide them<em>.<br /></em>The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table." ac:alt="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table."><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_number_in_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679230 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00414: Distributing custom diagrams

- page_id: `169679230`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679230/Distributing+custom+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:30.304+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1523698130

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1523698132

1523698127

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1523698131

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space='MD2024xR1'] can be exchanged with other usersin a *.zip* file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024xR1']. Alternatively, you can export/import the custom diagram descriptor (the .*xml*file) using the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Resource Manager

You need to build a*.zip*file containing a diagram descriptor file (e.g., *descriptor.xml*) and a Resource Manager descriptor file (e.g., *MDR_Custom Diagram_MagicDraw_74842_descriptor.xm**l*.)to be able to share your own custom diagram. For this, you need to use the **Development Tools** plugin. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the **Resource Manager**for future use and accessibility.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can build a *.zip* file for custom diagram distribution only after you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='MD2024xR1']created a newdiagram type or edited an existing standard diagram.

To create a *.zip* file using the **Development Tools** plugin

1. On your local file system, create the following folders structure: ...\<Custom Diagram Resource>\data\defaults\data\diagrams . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Launch the modeling tool.
3. Do one of the following:
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to **Help** > **About**.
    2. Next to Configuration Files , click the file location path. The configuration files folder opens.
    3. Under <configuration files>\data\diagrams, locate your custom diagram folder containing a descriptor .
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to**Diagrams** >**Customize**.
    2. In the **Customize Diagrams** dialog, select the custom diagram whose descriptor file you want to export and click the**Export** button. WarningBefore the export operation, create a new folder on your local file system and give it the name of your custom diagram, such as *Custom Diagram*. The exported descriptor file needs to be renamed to *descriptor.xml*and saved in this newly-created folder.
4. Copy and paste the custom diagram folder together with the descriptor file into the folders structure you created in thefirststep. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Distributing ProfilesIf you have specified Profiles when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space='MD2024xR1'], it is highly recommended that you distribute them together with your custom diagram. For this, create the *Profiles* folder under the custom diagram resource root folder and place all required Profiles in it before generating a *.zip*archive.You need to mark each Profile distributed together with a custom resource as**Standard/System Profile** (**File** >**Project Properties** > **More** >**Standard/System Profile**) to store it locally. Profiles stored on the server cannot be used for a custom diagram.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Download and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024xR1'] the Development Tools plugin.
6. Restart the modeling tool.
7. In the main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Custom Resource .
8. In the Custom Resource Building Wizard , set the custom diagram folder as the Resource Folder .
9. Click Next to skip the other steps and then click Finish . The resource ( . zip file) is built. It can now be distributed and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### INFO: Info

Info

A created custom diagram resource (.*zip*) can be bundled into the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) using the **Development Tools** plugin for distribution alongside other resources. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building a resource distribution file' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Customize Diagrams dialog

You can export the custom diagram descriptor (.*xml*) file directly into the**Customize Diagrams** dialog. The exported file can then be shared with others and imported into the modeling tool by another user via the same **Customize Diagrams** dialog using the **Import**command.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can export an .*xml*file for custom diagram distribution only after you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='MD2024xR1']or edited an existing standard diagram.

To export a custom diagram descriptor

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Select a custom diagram from the list and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_diagram_descriptor.png']

The .*xml* file is exported. You can now distribute it.

To import a custom diagram descriptor into the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Click the **Import** button and point to a descriptor (.*xml*)**on your local file system. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *.xml* file is imported, and a new diagram type appears under **Custom** **Diagrams**in the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b277f74b-e3a3-427b-a2c3-cacc84dd1cd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698130</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5065f0a-667b-4025-a1ec-446a822b81e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698132</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ab2bcd-1e4b-44c3-b64e-a0452d78669c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698127</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0c159d6-10b5-4b2a-a6db-feb87db6c1ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97826136-737d-4280-9c80-6a6996e8c1b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698131</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="885cb35b-65e1-4587-be5b-555938d081bc">can be exchanged with other users </ac:inline-comment-marker>in a <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.zip</em> file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /></ac:link>. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the <strong>Resource Manager </strong>for future use and accessibility. </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf5b8bcc-78e7-416b-a481-a4cb9bb09c9e"><ac:parameter using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ad3836a-5451-4c2d-9781-c716582b08f0">On your local file system,</ac:inline-comment-marker> create the following folders structure: <em>...\&lt;Custom Diagram Resource&gt;\data\default<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">click the file location path. The configuration files folder opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Under <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;configuration files&gt;\data\diagrams,</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> locate your custom diagram folder containing a descriptor</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the main menu, go to<strong> Diagrams</strong> &gt;<strong> Customize</strong>.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog, select the custom diagram whose descriptor file you want to export and click the<strong> Export</strong> button. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04cb718a-579a-47d8-98e3-1635280cb9e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before the export operation, create a new folder on your local file system and give it the name of your custom diagram, such as <em>Custom Diagram</em>. The exported descriptor file needs to be renamed to <em>descriptor.xml </em>and saved in this newly-created folder.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">Copy and paste the custom diagram folder together with the descriptor file into the folders structure you created in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">first</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"> step.</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_folder_with_a_descriptor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ed1c88c-36c9-4093-ba1b-89777c42636b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Distributing Profiles</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have specified P<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="63b4a73c-08ff-4f59-8a60-db89fba517a6">rofiles w</ac:inline-comment-marker>hen <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customizing a diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it is highly recommended that you distribute them together with your custom diagram. For this, create the <em>Profiles</em> folder under the custom diagram resource root folder and place all required Profiles in it before generating a <em>.zip </em>archive.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="520947aa-aa71-4079-9342-e66b046aa82b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to mark each Profile distributed together with a custom resource as<strong> Standard/System Profile</strong> (<strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Project Properties</strong> &gt; <strong>More</strong> &gt;<strong> Standard/System Profile</strong>) to store it locally. Profiles stored on the server cannot be used for a custom diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="profiles_folder.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Download and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024xR1" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. </li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Development Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Build Custom Resource</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Custom Resource Building Wizard</strong>, set the custom diagram<em> </em>folder as the <strong>Resource </strong><strong>Folder</strong>. </li><li>Click<strong> Next</strong> to skip the other steps and then click <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7e5bbb4-c15b-4440-bf69-d213b65a12ea">Finish</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7e5bbb4-c15b-4440-bf69-d213b65a12ea">.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">The resource (</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></em><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7"> file) is built.</ac:inline-comment-marker> It can now be distributed and <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing resource using Resource Manager"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[imported into the modeling tool using the Resource Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66a4e43d-0041-4421-82f5-7b4164fa33cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Info</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A created custom diagram resource (.<em>zip</em>) can be bundled into the resource distribution file (.<em>rdzip</em>) using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin for distribution alongside other resources. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Building a resource distribution file" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about building a resource distribution file>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Distributing custom diagrams using Customize Diagrams dialog</h3><p>You can export the custom diagram descriptor (.<em>xml</em>) file directly into the<strong> Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog. The exported file can then be shared with others and imported into the modeling tool by another user via the same <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog using the <strong>Import </strong>command. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ad1e09-7273-4ba2-9f50-9333cfe89384"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can export an .<em>xml </em>file for custom diagram distribution only after <span>you have </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating new diagram type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created a new diagram type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> or edited an existing standard diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To export a custom diagram descriptor </p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Select a custom diagram from the list and click the <strong>Export </strong>button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_diagram_descriptor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>The .<em>xml</em> file is exported. You can now distribute it.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import a custom diagram descriptor into the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button and point to a descriptor (.<em>xml</em>)<em> </em>on your local file system.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_descriptor_file.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>The <em>.xml</em> file is imported, and a new diagram type appears under <strong>Custom</strong> <strong>Diagrams </strong>in the <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog.<strong> </strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679237 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00415: Distributing Profiles

- page_id: `169679237`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679237/Distributing+Profiles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:30.367+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1777779865

1777779876

1777779866

You can distribute profiles with included customization data in the following two ways:

- Exchanging Profiles via XMI
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024xR1']

###### Exchanging Profiles via XMI

Using UML XMI interchange mechanisms it is possible to interchange profiles between tools, together with models to which they have been applied. A profile must therefore be defined as an interchangeable UML model. In addition to exchanging profiles together with models between tools, profile application should also be definable “by reference”; that is, a profile does not need to be interchanged if it is already present in the importing tool.

1777779864

**Related pages**

- Resource Manager
- Distributing Resources

A profile must therefore be defined as an interchangeable UML model. In addition to exchanging profiles together with models between tools, profile application should also be definable “by reference”; that is, a profile does not need to be interchanged if it is already present in the importing tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9b39b1-01b7-482f-aeab-0c421c83f8d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1777779864</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Resource+Manager">Resource Manager</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Distributing+Resources">Distributing Resources</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679096 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00416: Do Not Suggest As Type

- page_id: `169679096`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679096/Do+Not+Suggest+As+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.210+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Excludes elements from being suggested as a type in the list dialogs.

To exclude a DSL element from the list dialogs

1. In the Customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the Customization Target property.
2. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selection in diagrams' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog appears.
3. Select a stereotype or a metaclass.
4. Click OK to close the dialog.
5. Set the Hide Metatype property to true .
6. Set the Do Not Suggest As Type property to true .
7. Click Close .
8. Reopen the project. Elements or metaclasses selected in the third step of this procedure are not listed in the list dialogs. For example, in the Property [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], click the Type property and then the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selection in diagrams' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog, the elements or metaclasses selected as **customization targets** will not be displayed.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

## PAGE 00417: Do Not Suggest Name Auto Completion

- page_id: `169679099`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679099/Do+Not+Suggest+Name+Auto+Completion
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.260+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The property is useful when you do not want to display the name auto completion list for DSL elements typically displayed only in one diagram, or if the names of the DSL elements are full sentences or fragments of sentences.

To hide the name auto completion list

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Target' space='MD2024xR1'] property and then select a customization target.
2. Set the Do Not Suggest Name Auto Completion property to true .
3. Reopen the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of name auto completion list that appears when typing a DSL element name.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678086 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00418: Document Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `169678086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678086/Document+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-04-09T04:34:10.043+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983358622

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983358634

985841017

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983358624

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.

#### Using the Document Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select Report Designer Diagrams > Document Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Document Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** to generate the template. NoteOnly symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Generated from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Text Box

Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Box for holding text to be typed in.

###### Report Data Variable

Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the **Report Data Variable** dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.

###### Element Box

Element Box is represented as **#foreach** directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.

###### Page Box

Page Box is represented as **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.

###### Property Box

Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., $class.name or $class.isActive .
- If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., #foreach($member in $class.member) .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Box for obtaining an element property.

###### Element Table

Element Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Table** wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Table wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Table** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

After creating the Element Table, you can open the **Element Table** wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Table** editing context menu.

###### Property Table

Property Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the **Property Table** wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is **#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow**. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Document Report Designer diagram' space='MD2024xR1']).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.

###### Include

Include is represented as **#include** and **#includeSection** directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Include/IncludeSection** dialog. After selecting the document, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****)** is created. There are two options to select an included document: **File** and **Attached File**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you enter a section name in the **Section** box, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****,****<section_name>****)** will be created instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Include for including content or a document to the template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.

###### Condition Box

Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Condition** dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press **CTRL** + **SPACE**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.
- If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.
- If you want to add an #else statement, select the Add #else option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Break for starting a new page.

#### Helper Tool

Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Helper Tool** dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the **Create this function in #foreach directive** option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.

983358620

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Designer' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Report Designer Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as desired.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>  and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bccdd6ce-5638-40b6-bb8e-811e1d2f858b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating the Template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.</p></li><li><p>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</p></li><li><p>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Generated from Added Template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.</p><h4>Text Box</h4><p>Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Text Box.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Text Box for holding text to be typed in.</h6><h4>Report Data Variable</h4><p>Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Box.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.</h6><h4>Page Box</h4><p>Page Box is represented as <strong>#forpage</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the <strong>Property Table</strong> wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is <strong>#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow</strong>. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in <ac:link ac:anchor="ElementTable"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ac:link>).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Table Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.</h6><h4>Include</h4><p>Include is represented as <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Include/IncludeSection</strong> dialog. After selecting the document, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>)</strong> is created. There are two options to select an included document: <strong>File</strong> and <strong>Attached File</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db36aa00-e1fd-4c3b-b6fb-ff8cbadd6fb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you enter a section name in the <strong>Section</strong> box, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>, </strong><strong>&lt;section_name&gt;</strong><strong>)</strong> will be created instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Include for including content or a document to the template.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include Section Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.</h6><h4>Condition Box</h4><p>Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Condition</strong> dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b279208-3639-46c7-b587-27a4774df765"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.</li><li>If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.</li><li>If you want to add an #else statement, select the <strong>Add #else</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Box.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.</h6><h4>Page Break</h4><p>You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Break.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Break for starting a new page.</h6><h2>Helper Tool</h2><p>Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Helper Tool</strong> dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8fb04c8-a037-4c3e-9f6a-415ac170ff82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the <strong>Create this function in #foreach directive</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Helper Tool.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93dbb6b7-1bbc-4b38-a4d0-ca645b2887bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Designer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673338 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00419: Documentation tab

- page_id: `169673338`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673338/Documentation+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:48.818+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Quick properties panel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425887055

1425887058

1425887056

INLINE

The **Documentation** tab shows information associated with a selected model element in the Model Browser or on a diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Documentation** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Documentation tab.
- On the **Window** menu, click **Documentation**. If there is no documentation for the selected element, the text area is empty.

To write documentation for the selected model element

- In the Documentation tab, click the text area and type the text.

To turn on the HTML text

- In the Documentation tab, click the HTML check box.

The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.

1425887054

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the text area and type the text.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To turn on the HTML text</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Documentation</strong> tab, click the <strong>HTML</strong> check box.</li></ul><p>The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf777f01-546d-4bf9-9d0c-a29da6af118f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425887054</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Model+Browser">Model Browser</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00420: DOCX template in the Chart tool

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - "Column"
  - "Line"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"
- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (by default)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Line
    - "Line" (by default)
    - "3-D Line"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (by default)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (by default)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (by default)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (by default)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (by default)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

A code example:

```text

```

##### Setting chart titles

To set the chart title, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

##### Setting chart data categories

To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.

##### Setting chart data series

To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Setting a data source for each data series

To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Printing a chart to a report

To print a chart to a report, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

ac:macro-id="a3c9e37a-a0cd-4736-9f49-860fac6876bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514375</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles</h3><p>To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14f18f14-ed8c-4418-bba3-da7e456ef1e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[create(chartType : String, chartStyle : String) : Chart]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li><strong>chartType</strong>: case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:<ul><li>&quot;Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;Line&quot;</li><li>&quot;Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Radar&quot;<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>chartStyle</strong>: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:</p><ul><li>Column<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Column&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Column&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Line<br /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Line&quot; (by default)</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>&quot;3-D Line&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Pie<br /><ul><li>&quot;Pie&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;3-D Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Doughnut&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Bar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Bar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Area<br /><ul><li>&quot;Area&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Area&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Stacked Area&quot;</p></li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Area&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Surface<br /><ul><li>&quot;3-D Surface&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Wireframe 3-D Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Contour&quot;</li><li>&quot;Wireframe Contour&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Radar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Radar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Radar with Markers&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Filled Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ff663a8-5a8f-4aaa-95a4-909cf4aa491e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A code example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b71698-6a7c-43f4-9c13-da1795a0b1d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart titles</h3><p>To set the chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e05969-ca81-46b0-b3e9-82e38d1e887a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed7a520-5006-41c6-a511-1d11e7c6b007"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart data categories</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b64be4b-6660-487c-9bfe-246019575d19"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setCategories(categories : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b744b19c-7bee-444d-b0b3-f44cdc338507"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($categories = $array.createArray())
````html
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.</p><h3>Setting chart data series</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b6f0dd1-8866-40ee-93f4-9c71ce1b8768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.createSeries(seriesName : String) : DataSeries]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dc65b3-cf97-4b7f-938b-3103889f9435"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a data source for each data series</h3><p>To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7793b04-cb7a-4f69-916c-5e20fc74196e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setDataSources(dataSource : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32c06d6-7451-4bc7-821d-d8583c928d39"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Printing a chart to a report</h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b60c62be-6788-4dcc-b7c4-1e45b0c57845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7d5a03-9b62-48e1-a357-7a3cfc23609b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample Model.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample model used to extract values for creating a chart.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values from a Model Mapped.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag values from a model mapped into a matrix table using the Chart tool to create a chart.</h6><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f8c30bd-fb4f-4f64-9858-69d2081bfa1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")

#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)
#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
	#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
	#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
	#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Printed.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679074 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00421: Domain Specific Language customization

- page_id: `169679074`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679074/Domain+Specific+Language+customization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:28.965+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1679637636

1679637647

1679637637

DSL (Domain Specific Language) customization is a model-driven approach, based on UML profiling. Our modeling tool provides the ability to use DSL engine for adapting domain specific profiles to create your own custom specification windows, custom real-time semantic rules and others. In other words, you can create specialized domain specific tool and hide UML underneath.

The modeling tool's DSL customization engine is able to process user defined rules for DSL elements and reflect this in GUI and diagrams behavior.

The topics in this section show you how to create a new DSL element, how to customize its Specification window, shortcut menu, how to create customization rules for relationships drawing, how to customize possible owned elements, how to create numbering customization, how to extend metamodel with derived properties and others.

1679637635

**Related Pages**

- Creating Customization Data
- Using Customization Data

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Our modeling tool provides the ability to use DSL engine for adapting domain specific profiles to create your own custom specification windows, custom real-time semantic rules and others. In other words, you can create specialized domain specific tool and hide UML underneath. </span></p><p><span>The modeling tool's DSL customization engine is able to process user defined rules for DSL elements and reflect this in GUI and diagrams behavior. </span></p><p><span>The topics in this section show you how to create a new DSL element, how to customize its Specification window, shortcut menu, how to create customization rules for relationships drawing, how to customize possible owned elements, how to create numbering customization, how to extend metamodel with derived properties and others. </span></p><p><br /></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1cc433f-370a-46f8-b593-c5c0343d85cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1679637635</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+Customization+Data">Creating Customization Data</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Customization+Data">Using Customization Data</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00422: Doxygen-after-member documentation

- page_id: `169679522`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679522/Doxygen-after-member+documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-12T16:56:17.240+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Functionality

Functionality

This feature only works in import-code-only mode.

If a doxygen documentation after a member is reversed, the comment after the member should use the special doxygen tag used to mark a documentation after a member.

- '/**<'
- '///<'
- '//!<'

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c66397a-07b8-4750-89fe-4407a60da285"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>This feature only works in import-code-only mode.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>If a doxygen documentation after a member is reversed, the comment after the member should use the spe</span>cial doxygen tag used to mark a documentation after a member.</p><ul><li>'/**&lt;'</li><li>'///&lt;'</li><li>'//!&lt;'</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674546 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00423: Dragging objects

- page_id: `169674546`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674546/Dragging+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:47:27.993+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: ['dragging-in-diagram', 'dragging-in-activity-diagram', 'dragging-in-state-machine-diagram', 'dragging-in-sequence-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567604035

567604039

567604033

**On this page**

3

567604037

##### Dragging files and elements

The drag-and-drop capability allows you to:

- Drag files from your file system onto an element in the Model Browser or a symbol on a diagram. A hyperlink to the dragged file is automatically created.Dragged images are set as the **Image** property value of the element.If an image is dragged to a Stereotype, it is set as a Stereotype icon.
- Drag a project to the diagram pane to open the project.
- Drag source code files from your file system to the Code Engineering Set.
- Drag any elements to any properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. For example, you can drag a Class element from the Model Browser to the **Type** property in the **Operation** Specification window. That Class element is assigned as the Operation type in the Specification window.
- Drag any elements from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] onto:
  - An empty place on a diagram. In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.
  - A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.
  - An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.
- Drag a property to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1'] :
  - Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.
  - Drag a property from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] to a diagram pane. In both cases, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1']is drawn from the classifier[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1']to a property's type. The property itself is set as an association end. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.

If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be allowed to drag those model elements.

##### Dragging in diagrams

You can drag-and-drop relationships to a diagram.The path for the relationship with shapes at the end of the path and the path between the shapes (if the shapes at the end of the path are already represented on the diagram) is created.

You can also drag-and-drop model elements to a note or a text box symbol in a diagram to add a hyperlink to that element.

###### Dragging in Sequence diagrams

In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] you can:

- Drag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR1'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] in a Sequence diagram.The Message becomes a Call Messageonce the operation is assigned. The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MD2024xR1']type must have or inherit this Operation.
- Drag an Interaction to an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1']. The Interaction will be set as the Refers To element.
- Drag a Signal to a Message. The Message is converted into a Send Signal Message and the Signal is assigned to the Message.

###### Dragging in State Machine diagrams

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] you can d rag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024xR1'] element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR1'] element. A Trigger with this Event is created for the Transition element.
- You can drag behaviors from one state shape to another.

###### Dragging in Activity diagrams

In an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] you can:

- Drag an Activity, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR1'], or a State Machine onto an Activity diagram to create a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Call Behavior Action' space='MD2024xR1']Call Behavior Action.
- Drag a Signal onto an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MD2024xR1'].
- Drag a Signal onto a Send Signal Action to set or change the Signal.
- Drag a Signal onto an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MD2024xR1'] to set or change the Signal. Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, the Signal Event for the diagram and the Trigger for the action will be created automatically.
- Drag an Event onto an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag an Event onto an Accept Event Action to set the Event.
- Drag a Signal Event to an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag a Property, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024xR1'] to an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR1']. Elements will be set as representative elements.
- Drag a Property, Actor, Class or Instance Specification to an Activity diagram by using the right mouse button. From the shortcut menu, select to create a Swimlane, Central Buffer Node, or available Actions. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567640426

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']
