ac:name="id">567604035</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="547728ce-5644-479a-b2fd-9eabda3179c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567604039</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="916b8261-9524-48d5-96fc-ed6d0fbcef7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567604033</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abcce0ea-f8b3-4c6f-8486-c54448d86ab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2308200-880c-49b1-aff8-35264bfd39de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567604037</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Dragging files and e<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a8c8e46-e5fb-4e5d-bd57-4ffc924b9c04">lements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>The drag-and-drop capability allows you to:</p><ul><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0d87f94-8d89-4066-b20e-66075101f392">Drag files from your file system onto an element in the Model Browser or a symbol on a diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14eb5968-c32a-4a4c-a183-23ad5fe890b1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A hyperlink to the dragged file is automatically created.</li><li>Dragged images are set as the <strong>Image</strong> property value of the element.</li><li>If an image is dragged to a Stereotype, it is set as a Stereotype icon.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Drag a project to the diagram pane to open the project.</li><li>Drag source code files from your file system to the Code Engineering Set.</li><li><p>Drag any elements to any properties in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="dc58f90c-8b23-49db-aa1e-56d1c51624fd"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e096930-2535-4ff4-95a0-984b6daedde5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, you can drag a Class element from the Model Browser to the <strong>Type</strong> property in the <strong>Operation</strong> Specification window. That Class element is assigned as the Operation type in the Specification window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Drag any elements from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> onto:<ul><li>An empty place on a diagram. In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.</li><li>A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.</li><li>An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.</li></ul></li><li>Drag a property to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[association]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<br /><ul><li>Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.</li><li><p>Drag a property from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> to a diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="093a9b10-ed05-405c-82a3-0e69fac47181"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In both cases, an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[association ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>is drawn from the classifier<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to a property's type. The property itself is set as an association end. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fb5fa2f-e445-41f0-b781-5b11a7ee947d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be allowed to drag those model elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Dragging in diagrams</h3><p>You can d<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">rag-and-drop relationships to a diagram. </span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">The path for the relationship with shapes at the end of the path and t</span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">he path between the shapes (if the shapes at the end of the path are already represented on the diagram) <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bb715060-dbad-4658-9400-8890f717aec8">is created.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p>You can also drag-and-drop model elements to a note or a text box symbol in a diagram to add a hyperlink to that element.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="863cbc04-9e7f-4d42-82d5-d58fa95ea0bc"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h4><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="863cbc04-9e7f-4d42-82d5-d58fa95ea0bc">Dragging in Sequence diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1afac70c-c182-40c8-893f-1683057df901">In</ac:inline-comment-marker> a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3bd72de4-c838-4cbd-ad81-46f07eee6808"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></ac:inline-comment-marker> you can:</p><ul><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Drag an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link> in a Sequence diagram. </span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">The Message becomes a Call Message </span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">once the operation is assigned.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45ffa160-1aca-405a-a7d4-41e3e1fac9e8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /></ac:link><span> type must have or inherit this Operation.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Drag an Interaction to an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /></ac:link>. The Interaction will be set as the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Refers To</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> element.</span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Drag a Signal to a Message. The Message is converted into a Send Signal Message and the Signal is assigned to the Message.</span></li></ul><h4>Dragging in State <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f8c341cb-c3cd-4a68-9d95-86eeff491064">Machine diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><ul><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link> you can d<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">rag an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Event" /></ac:link> element to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link> element. A Trigger with this Event is created for the Transition element.</span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">You can drag behaviors from one state shape to another.</span></li></ul><h4>Dragging in A<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fabbf0e6-5a95-4078-9bf7-6bfa7603c06a">ctivity diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>In an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link> you can:</p><ul><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Drag an Activity, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction" style="line-height: 1.42857;">Call Behavior A</span>ction</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Drag a Signal onto an Activity diagram to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Accept Event Action" /></ac:link> to set or change the Signal.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674594 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00424: Drawing diagram shapes

- page_id: `169674594`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674594/Drawing+diagram+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-06T08:59:50.878+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568251568

568251570

568251569

You can draw diagram shapes in any type of diagram.

To display a diagram shape on a diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] , select the diagram.
2. Drag the diagram to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_shape_on_dgr_pane.png']

You can change the representation of the diagram shape by changing the diagram stereotype display mode.

To change the representation of the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select a new value for the Show Stereotypes property. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_dgr_shape_repres_sbml_prop.png']

You can also select whether to show or hide the diagram constraints or tagged values on the diagram shape.

To show diagram stereotype constraints and tagged values on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select the Show Constraints and Show Tagged Values check boxes.

To display the context stereotype icon instead of the diagram stereotype icon on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog , change the Use Stereotype property value to Context .

The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**.

There is a possibility to show the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram frame' space='MD2024xR1']of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type on the diagram shape.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- From the diagram shape shortcut menu, open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog and select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

568251567

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Complex Composition

761371663

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Complex Composition

761371665

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Complex Composition

761371664

One of the problems caused by the old approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated project usages' space='MD2024xR1'],was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected.

The following examples illustrate this problem.

#### INFO: Another types of problems

Example 1

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with 3 sub-used projects: *Capacitors*, *Inductances*, and *Resistances*. Consider another project *RadioSet*, which uses the used project *ElectricComponents*.

Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet*.

###### Old problematic behavior

Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:

*RadioSet*→*Capacitors,*

*RadioSet*→*Inductances,*and

*RadioSet*→*Resistances.*

Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project *ElectricComponents.*

If you tried to refactor the used project *ElectricComponents*by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project *RadioSet* would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects.

No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data.

###### New behavior

As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project *ElectricComponents* is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project *RadioSet* are automatically rearranged.

#### NOTE: Another types of problems

Another types of problems

#### INFO: Complex Composition

Example 2

Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project *RadioSet* uses the 100th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which in turn uses the 150th version of the sub-used project *Resistances.*

###### Old problematic behavior

Whenever the project *RadioSet* referenced the sub-used project *Resistances,* the direct usage would be created to the 150th version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project *RadioSet* to use the 200th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which used the 300th version of the sub-used project *Resistances,* a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150th version.

**New behavior**

As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.

#### NOTE: Complex Composition

Complex Composition

If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature.

761396174

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673740 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00427: Ecore modeling

- page_id: `169673740`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673740/Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T18:02:36.137+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Diagrams and elements in Ecore models

There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.

Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.

Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.

Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.

There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their **opposite** property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::**eSuperTypes** property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.

Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, **SortedList<T extends Comparable<? super T>>**. 
Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.

##### Element properties in Ecore models

There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:

- The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix ( Namespace Prefix ) and nsURI (corresponds to the URI property specified in the UML v2.4.1). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_package.png']

- The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName ( Instance Class Name ) and instanceTypeName ( Instance Type Name ). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png']

- Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile ( Volatile ), transient ( Transient ), unsettable ( Unsettable ), and resolveProxies ( Resolve Proxies , used for references only). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png']

##### Annotation modeling

Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.

However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. 
 
To create an Ecore annotation

1. In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.
2. Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotations_specification_window.png']

To create a key-value pair

1. In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the Annotation Details property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_details.png']
2. Click the Create button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_string_to_string.png']
3. Enter values for both the Value and Key properties.
4. Click the Back button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_vision.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Diagrams and elements in Ecore models</h3><p>There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.</p><p>Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.</p><p>Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.</p><p>Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.</p><p>There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their <strong>opposite</strong> property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::<strong>eSuperTypes</strong> property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.</p><p>Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, <strong>SortedList&lt;T extends Comparable&lt;? super T&gt;&gt;</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.</p><h3>Element properties in Ecore models</h3><p>There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:</p><ul><li>The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix (<strong>Namespace Prefix</strong>) and nsURI (corresponds to the <strong>URI</strong> property specified in the UML v2.4.1).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_package.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName (<strong>Instance Class Name</strong>) and instanceTypeName (<strong>Instance Type Name</strong>). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile (<strong>Volatile</strong>), transient (<strong>Transient</strong>), unsettable (<strong>Unsettable</strong>), and resolveProxies (<strong>Resolve Proxies</strong>, used for references only). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Annotation modeling</h3><p>Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.</p><p>However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create an Ecore annotation</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.</li><li>Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotations_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create a key-value pair</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the <strong>Annotation Details</strong> property group. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_details.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_string_to_string.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Enter values for both the <strong>Value</strong> and <strong>Key</strong> properties.</li><li>Click the <strong>Back</strong> button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_vision.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673735 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00428: Ecore support

- page_id: `169673735`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673735/Ecore+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:39:33.636+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305838392

305838394

305838393

Ecore model is a model type supported by Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF). This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).

Ecore models can be used for various purposes:

- Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).
- Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.

Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.

305838390

**Related pages**

****

This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).</p><p>Ecore models can be used for various purposes:</p><ul><li>Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).</li><li>Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.</li></ul><p>Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13b48495-04a9-4916-b419-21693e6a1a0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="adfefb38-4bfc-49a1-b3bc-f6d93ba5028e" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00429: Edit Hyperlink dialog

- page_id: `169675821`

#### Introduction

You can add new or manage existing hyperlinks in the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog, consisting of:

- Three main tabs containing all modeling tools: **Address** tab, **File** tab, and **Element/Symbol** tab.
- One tab required to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin : **OSLC Link** tab .

The following figure illustrates the tabs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All available tabs in the Edit Hyperlink dialog.

#### Hyperlink types

The following table provides details of available hyperlink types.

| Tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog | Hyperlink type | Required plugins | Tab descriptions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Address tab | Links to webpages or other external resources. | - | Learn more about Address tab >> |
| File tab | Opens the file from your file system. | Learn more about File tab >> |  |
| Element/Symbol tab | In HTML text, opens the element Specification window.In plain text, on element/symbol, or diagram, opens the diagram. | Learn more about Element/Symbol tab >> |  |
| OSLC Link tab | Links to OSLC resources. | Cameo DataHub Plugin. How to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin >> | Learn more about OSLC Link tab >> |

#### Opening the Edit Hyperlink dialog

To open the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks on elements' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

841401715

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675659 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00430: Editing logical values

- page_id: `169675659`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675659/Editing+logical+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-03-22T08:55:48.374+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Logical (boolean) type property value can be either *true*or *false*. Examples of logical type properties: *Is Abstract*, *Is Read Only*.

To edit a logical property value, do one of the following

- Select the check box to set the value to *true*.
- Clear the check box selection to set value to *false*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675657 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00431: Editing long textual values

- page_id: `169675657`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675657/Editing+long+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-12-23T11:34:06.904+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Editing textual properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In case of editing a value that consists of several words, the value cell may seem too short. In order to avoid this inconvenience you can edit the property value in a dialog specially designated for typing long values.

To edit a textual value consisting of several words

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the opened dialog, type a value and click **OK**.

There are some textual properties, whose values can keep text formatting settings. You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR1']HTML editorin such cases. To use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR1'], select the **HTML**check box in the dialog opened for editing this special kind of textual property value.

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing textual properties' space='MD2024xR1']modified1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675658 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00432: Editing multiline textual values

- page_id: `169675658`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675658/Editing+multiline+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-12-23T11:59:44.721+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Editing textual properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some properties can have more than one textual value, for example, the **Body**property of an opaque expression, the **Pre Condition**and **Post Condition**properties of a use case. You can use a special editor to edit multiline textual values.

Using the editor for multiline textual values, you can perform the following actions:

- Create a new value.
- Remove a selected value.
- Edit each value in the HTML editor separately.
- Reorder values.
- Strip long values (available only in the Specification window).

To strip long values, make sure that the Strip Multiline Text mode isturned on in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

The following table describes functions of buttons used in the editor.

| Button | Shortcut Key | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about theHTML toolbar, refer to "HTML editor toolbar" on page 428. |
|  | ALT+ENTER | Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value. |
|  | ALT+BACKSPACE | Click to remove a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+UPARROW | Click to moves up a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+DOWNARROW | Click to moves down a selected row. |

Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key as an alternative for the ALT key.

## PAGE 00433: Editing property values

- page_id: `169675655`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

- version_date: `2017-02-09T10:02:40.990+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit property values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024xR1']
- Element tab on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Properties**tabin the Model Browser

Property values in gray are non-editable. They are derived from other elements used in your model.

Learn about editing values of different property types in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing textual properties' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing logical values' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting values' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cases of advanced editing' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675679 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00434: Editing property values in property groups

- page_id: `169675679`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675679/Editing+property+values+in+property+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:17:25.730+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Cases of advanced editing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some elements can be related to each other as the owner and the owned one, for example, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR1'] can be an owner of a trigger, and the trigger can be owned by the transition.

The modeling tool allows the handy editing of owned element property values via owner’s properties. Owned element properties are available as appropriately named property groups, for example, the *Entry*, *Do Activity*, and *Exit*property groups in the state’s Specification window (see the following figure).

When editing owned element properties, keep in mind that you create a new element in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A property setin the property group may vary before and after owned element specification. For example, let’s say,we have an activity assigned as a behavior type in the **Entry**property group. As a result the additional properties appear in the **Entry** property group (**Name** and **Owned Diagram**) after creating a new activity (see the following figure).

Keep in mind that the owner’s specification contains only ownership-relevant properties of an owned element. 
To view and edit all other properties of the owned element, open its Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an owned element as a property value

1. In the property group, wherein you want to create the owned element, select either a type or an elementproperty specification cell.
2. From the list, select an element you need to create.
3. Specify additional properties corresponding to the created element.

To remove an owned element, do one of the following

- Either

1. Select the **<owned element type>**property specification cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

- Or

1. Select the <owned element> property cell and click **...** button.
2. From the list, select **Delete <element>**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675470 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00435: Editing tagged value

- page_id: `169675470`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675470/Editing+tagged+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:34:29.004+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Tag
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new tagged value, do one of the following

- In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , Tags property group, select an available tag definition and click the Create Value button.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the Specification window tag value area.

To create default tag values

1. In the stereotype's Specification window, expand the Tag Definitions property group and select the tag definition.
2. Assign a value for the Default Value property.
3. Create an element and assign a stereotype for this element. The element will have tags with assigned default values.

##### Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value

Modeling tool allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.

To set default values instead of empty values

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Set Empty Tags to Defaults . The Select Package dialog opens.
2. Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values. If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the *All* data tree select the root package *Data* and click the **Add All** button. The root package *Data* is added to the **Selected** objects list. Click **OK**.
3. In the opened dialog, click Yes . Now, the element with stereotype tags has been assigned default values.

The following conditions are required to set the default tag values

- The element should have an assigned stereotype with a specified default value property.
- The tag definition, to which you want to assign a default value, should contain no value.

The default tag values are not set when the stereotype property (tag definition) multiplicity is equal to 0, 0..1, 0..*, *.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024xR1']

The element will have tags with assigned default values.</li></ol><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="66458d2c-4a05-4c4b-a183-c28d967c3755">Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>Modeling tool allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.</p><p><br /></p><p>To set default values instead of empty values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Set Empty Tags to Defaults</strong>. The <strong>Select Package</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9a5e19f-c7a9-498a-89c3-bf18e7f08f41"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the <em>All</em> data tree select the root package <em>Data</em> and click the <strong>Add All</strong> button. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675656 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00436: Editing textual properties

- page_id: `169675656`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675656/Editing+textual+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-07-13T12:10:35.725+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Textual type property values are usually simply typed in a property value cell. In some specific cases you can also use advanced editing features, i.e., long textual values editor and multiline textual values editor (both available in HTML). 
Examples of textual type properties: *Name*, *Body*.

To edit a textual property value

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Type a value.

Learn about advanced cases of editing textual values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing long textual values' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing multiline textual values' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677459 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00437: Editing the content of a used project

- page_id: `169677459`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677459/Editing+the+content+of+a+used+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-01T10:43:06.923+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To switch versions of the used projects you must have an**Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects can only use other projects in the read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.

To open a used project for editing

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package Project Usages .
2. Click Project Usages and select:
  - **Open Latest Version** to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does **not** change after editing the used project. Updating used project versionsIf you select the check-box **Notify on New Version** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Usages**dialog for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version while reloading the main project.
  - Open This version to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.

Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.

#### WARNING: Updating used project version

Updating used project version

Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To switch versions of the used projects you must have an </span><strong>Administer Resources</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud185" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[permission]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects can onl<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0885cc63-f2d7-4520-8705-6cc0d04d1515">y u</ac:inline-comment-marker>se other projects in the read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.</p><p> </p><p>To open a used project for editing</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package <em>Project Usages</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Project Usages</strong> and select:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Open Latest Version</strong> to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does <strong>not</strong> change after editing the used project. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df1e882b-86c6-4380-b341-b0f86032077f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project versions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select the check-box <strong>Notify on New Version</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version <span>while reloading the main project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Open This version</strong> to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.</li></ul></li></ol><p>Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ecc64b9-4974-4f09-ab53-3e90c70944ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the usage manually]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677691 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00438: Editing used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE

- page_id: `169677691`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677691/Editing+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-20T14:24:28.203+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**3D**EXPERIENCEplatform projects can onl y u se other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone.

To open a used project for editing

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package Project Usages .
2. Click Project Usages and select:
  - **Open Latest Version** to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does **not** change after editing the used project. Updating used project versionsIf you select the check-box **Notify on New Version** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project iterations' space='']**Project Usages**dialog for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version while reloading the main project.
  - Open This version to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in read-only mode.

Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.

#### WARNING: Updating used project version

Updating used project version

Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project iterations' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform projects can onl</span> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0885cc63-f2d7-4520-8705-6cc0d04d1515">y u</ac:inline-comment-marker> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">se other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone.</span></p><p>To open a used project for editing</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package <em>Project Usages</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Project Usages</strong> and select:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Open Latest Version</strong> to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does <strong>not</strong> change after editing the used project. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df1e882b-86c6-4380-b341-b0f86032077f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project versions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select the check-box <strong>Notify on New Version</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing used project iterations" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version <span>while reloading the main project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Open This version</strong> to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in read-only mode.</li></ul></li></ol><p>Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ecc64b9-4974-4f09-ab53-3e90c70944ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing used project iterations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the usage manually]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673314 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00439: Editor Tabs

- page_id: `169673314`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673314/Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-04T13:10:36.539+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access diagrams and model samples.

On this page:

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. Displays the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- Diagram Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with diagrams. You can have multiple diagram tabs open simultaneously.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, select the appropriate command.

##### Viewing samples

You can view the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation either through the Samples tab or the Welcome tab.

###### Viewing samples in the Welcome tab

To view samples in the Welcome tab

- Once the modeling tool is launched, in the Welcome tab, click Samples .

###### Invoking the samples tab

To invoke the samples tab

- In the Main Menu , click Help , then Show Samples .

##### Navigating content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

You can use the search bar to navigate the content in the Welcome and Samples tabs. This is especially useful for locating specific samples.

To navigate content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

1. Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the search bar.
2. In the Find input field, type the search keyword.
3. (Optional) Use the commands in the search bar for efficient search.

The table below displays the search bar's commands.

| Command icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to close the search bar. |
|  | Type the search keyword(s). |
|  | Click to highlight the next search result. |
|  | Click to highlight the previous search result. |
|  | Click to highlight all search results. |
|  | Select the check box to find results matching the search input letter case. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676931 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00440: Element not in diagram

- page_id: `169676931`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676931/Element+not+in+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-04T10:30:17.978+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

Element(s) not in diagram

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the element is displayed on the diagram pane when that element and the diagram have the same owner (behavior context). This rule applies to Sequence, Activity, State Machine, and Communication diagrams.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Diagram

**Solvers**

- Display Inner Elements - displays the element that is not displayed in the diagram that belongs to the same owner as the element.
- Remove Element - removes the element that is not displayed in any of the diagrams that belong to the same owner as the element from the Model.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air* and the diagram *Cool Down Cabin Air*belong to the same owner, Activity *Cool Down Cabin Air*, but the Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air* is not displayed on the diagram *Cool Down Cabin Air*. 
The error is resolved by displaying the Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air*via the **Display Inner Elements**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>Element(s) not in diagram</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the element is displayed on the diagram pane when that element and the diagram have the same owner (behavior context). This rule applies to Sequence, Activity, State Machine, and Communication diagrams.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Display Inner Elements </strong>- displays the element that is not displayed in the diagram that belongs to the same owner as the element.</li><li><strong>Remove Element</strong> - removes the element that is not displayed in any of the diagrams that belong to the same owner as the element from the Model.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element_not_in_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Element not in diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air</em> and the diagram <em>Cool Down Cabin Air </em>belong to the same owner, Activity <em>Cool Down Cabin Air</em>, but the Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air</em> is not displayed on the diagram <em>Cool Down Cabin Air</em>. <br />The error is resolved by displaying the Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air </em>via the <strong>Display Inner Elements </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675898 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00441: Element Numbering dialog

- page_id: `169675898`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675898/Element+Numbering+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-28T17:09:09.098+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The**Element Numbering** dialog is designed to number elements by setting the ID, select different numbering schemes, or modify numbering properties defined by the numbering customization. Using this dialog, you can change, remove, and update the element ID, as well as change the number separator, prefix, and the numbering scheme.

Numbering changes you make for the selected element will be applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

To open the**Element Numbering** dialog

1. Select the model elemen t you want to number.
2. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click **Element Numbering**. The command **Element Numbering** is available, if [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space='MD2024xR1'] for the selected element type is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Your model is represented in the Model browser on the left side of the dialog. If the element of the type filtered in the**Filter Elements by Element Type** list is selected in the Model browser, owned elements are listed on the right side of the dialog.

In the **Filter Elements by Element Type** list, you can select which type of elements you want to number. The selected element type separator, prefix, and numbering scheme are displayed in the numbering properties list.

The usability of the numbering properties list is described in the following table:

| Item name | Item type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Separator | Text box | Enter your custom separator, which can be any symbol. The separator is changed for all numbers of the selected element type.All previously defined separators will be overridden. |
| Prefix | Text box | Enter your custom prefix, which can be any symbol. Just the one prefix can be entered for the selected numbering scheme. The defined prefix is displayed before the element number.All previously defined prefixes will be overridden. |
| Numbering Scheme | Drop-down list | Select one of the available numbering schemes for the selected element type. Numbering schemes are defined in numbering customization. |
| Renumber from Initial Value | Check box | Select if you want elements to be renumbered from the initial value after you have changed it.Available in the renumbering options area. |

For more information on numbering properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your first numbering customization' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can have several numbering schemes defined for one type of elements. 
All defined numbering schemes for the selected element type are displayed in the numbered properties list.

The **Element Numbering** dialog buttons are described in the following table.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Edit | Click to change the numbering symbol for the selected element. The button is available when the selected element has a number and the element is editable. It has two options:Click Edit to change the last ID symbol of the selected element.Click Set Custom ID to change the whole ID (including the prefix) of the selected element. |
| Create / Remove | The Create button is available if a selected element in the element list is not numbered. Click the Create button to number the selected element. A unique successive number will be created for the selected element.The Remove button is available if a selected element in the element list is numbered and editable. This button has two options:Click Remove to delete the selected element’s number.Click Remove Recursively. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element. |
| Increase | Click to increase an element number.The button is available if the selected element is numbered and editable.The element’s place in the list changes after increasing the number. |
| Decrease | Click to decrease an element number. The button is available if the element is editable, is numbered, and that number is not the first one.The element’s place in the list changes after decreasing the number. |
| Details | Click to open the renumbering options area. |
| Update Last ID | Click to set the maximum ID in the numbered sequence as the last one. |
| Renumber | Click to update the element numbers. The numbering is updated for all elements in the list. |
| Renumber Recursively | Click to update all element numbers recursively. The numbering is updated for all elements in the element list, including their owning elements. |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df6e716a-8d23-4f91-81bb-7d8841fadaa7"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<strong> Element Numbering</strong> dialog is designed to number elements by setting the ID, select different numbering schemes, or modify numbering properties defined by the numbering customization. The button is available if the element is editable, is numbered, and that number is not the first one.</p><p>The element’s place in the list changes after decreasing the number.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Details</td><td colspan="1">Click to open the renumbering options area.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Update Last ID</td><td colspan="1">Click to set the maximum ID in the numbered sequence as the last one.</td></tr><tr><td>Renumber</td><td><p>Click <span>to update the element numbers. The numbering is updated for all elements in the list.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Renumber Recursively</span></td><td colspan="1">Click <span>to update all element numbers recursively. The numbering is updated for all elements in the element list, including their owning elements.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8f617d-f9a0-4dc1-ab94-5728ce7190ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e63ac504-51bc-4f1f-b122-416b4516fd14"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with generic numbering mechanism" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675831 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00442: Element Symbol tab

- page_id: `169675831`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675831/Element+Symbol+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:17.612+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Active check box rule

62007185

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Active check box rule

62007187

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Active check box rule

62007186

The**Element/Symbol** tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleting hyperlinks to elements in your model.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

The table below describes the boxes and buttons of the **Element/Symbol** tab.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text box (highlighted in orange in the figure) | Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. It dynamically changes according to the selected option in the Text to display drop-down box and the specified element/symbol in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text. |
| Text to display drop-down box | Allows you to select the hyperlink representation options of the specified text in the text box:Name. Displays the element name from its .Representation text. Displays the full element title which is visible in .Icon+Representation text. Displays the element icon together with the full element title which is visible in .Custom text. Allows you to type your own text in the text box; however, then you cannot specify the Update mode option.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text. |
| Update mode drop-down box | Allows you to select when the hyperlink will be updated if the referenced element and the hyperlink become inconsistent. This option depends on the option selected in the Text to display box and the element type specified in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box.Automatically updated. Updates the hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically.Automatically checked. Enables active validation rules that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.Manually checked. Allows you to run validation manually to check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.Do not update. Does not update if the hyperlink text and the referenced element name become inconsistent. Validation will not find any inconsistencies.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text.Conditions of bidirectional update modeIf the hyperlink is inserted on text referring to LiteralString, LiteralInteger, LiteralReal, or LiteralBoolean Value Specification (e.g. default value in Value property), the update is bidirectional (from value to text and from text to value) when the following options are set in the Edit Hyperlink dialog or the Project Options dialog: - the Text to display/Display Mode is set to Representation text or Icon + Representation text. - the Update Mode is set to Automatically updated, Automatically checked, or Manually checked.In all other situations, the update is only one direction: from text to value. |
| Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box | Displays the unique URL of the selected element from the Select Model Elements/Symbol dialog. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used elements/symbols from your model. You can select them to fill in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box. |
|  | Opens the the Select Model Elements/Symbol dialog where you can select an element from your model to add it as hyperlink. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously selected elements/symbols from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

62007183

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR1']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676842 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00443: Element usage in diagrams

- page_id: `169676842`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676842/Element+usage+in+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-18T17:15:25.714+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Additional information

1181916137

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Additional information

1181916139

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Additional information

1181916138

INLINE

An element symbol is a visual representation of a model element in a diagram. There are two types of symbols: shapes and paths. In a diagram, all types of relationships are represented as paths. Model elements that are not relationships are represented as shapes.

No Magic modeling tools allow you to see element usages in diagrams by listing all diagrams that display the symbols of a particular model element. You can access the diagrams in which an element is used from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] of that element, the element's shortcut menu, or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Main menu' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### TIP: Additional information

Additional information

For more information about element usages and relations between model elements, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024xR1'].

To search for element usage in diagrams from the Specification window

1. Double-click the desired element to open its Specification window.
2. On the left side of the Specification window, in the property group list, select the Usage in Diagrams node. The area on the right side of the window shows all diagrams in which the element is represented as a symbol. To list other types of diagrams (e.g., Generic Table) the selected element is used in, click the Check Other Diagrams button.
3. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram and click the Open button or double-click the desired diagram. Alternatively, you can right-click the diagram and select Open from the drop-down menu
  - To open the Specification window of one of the listed diagrams, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_specification.png'] near the desired diagram in the list.

If you open a diagram (described in the first option of step 3), the symbol of the related element is selected in that diagram. If the diagram includes several symbols of the same element, all of those symbols are selected in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a model element displaying all the diagrams it is used in.

To search for element usage in diagrams from the element’s shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the desired element in the model browser or its symbol in a diagram.
2. From the element’s shortcut menu, select Go To > Usage in Diagrams .
3. Select either Diagrams with Symbols, Other Diagrams , or Realizing Elements .
4. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram.
  - To open all listed diagrams, click Open All Diagrams .

Depending on the action you performed in step 3, one or all of the diagrams in which the related element is used are opened with the selected element symbol(s).

To search for element usage in diagrams from the main menu

1. Select either the desired element in the model browser or its symbol in a diagram.
2. In the main menu, select Analyze > Go To > Usage in Diagrams .
3. Select either Diagrams with Symbols, Other Diagrams , or**Realizing Elements**.
4. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram.
  - To open all listed diagrams, click Open All Diagrams .

The selected diagram or all of the diagrams in which the related element is used are opened with the element symbol(s) selected.

1183619084

INLINE

##### Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model analysis' space='MD2024xR1']

If the diagram includes several symbols of the same element, all of those symbols are selected in the diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usage_in_diagrams_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Element usage in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Specification window of a model element displaying all the diagrams it is used in.</h6><p>To search for element usage in diagrams from the element’s shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the desired element in the model browser or its symbol in a diagram.</li><li>From the element’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Go To</strong> &gt; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675866 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00444: Elements multiple selection

- page_id: `169675866`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675866/Elements+multiple+selection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:19:39.757+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402498987

402498990

402498989

The **Multiple Selection** button is available only when it is allowed to select more than one element.

To select more than one element

1. Click the Multiple Selection button. The Selected elements area appears on the right side of the element selection dialog.
2. In the model tree or list, select one or more elements at a time by holding the Ctrl or Shift keys.
3. Move selected elements to the Selected elements area.

To return to the single selection

- Click the Single Selection button.

When more than one element is selected in the tree or in the list in the multiple selection mode, the selection will be reduced to one element (the first one based on alphabetical order), when switching to the single selection mode.

402498986

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677596 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00445: Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool

- page_id: `169677596`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677596/Enabling+3DEXPERIENCE+collaboration+in+a+modeling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-19T13:26:02.654+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To start collaborating on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, first you need to enable the collaboration environment powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform in your modeling tool.

If you launch your modeling tool through the platform, these steps are not necessary because you will be automatically logged in to the platform.

To enable collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Environment .
2. On the left side of the Environment Options dialog, select the Collaboration option group.
3. In the option specification pane, set the Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform option to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='collaboration_in_environment_options.png']
4. Click OK to close the Environment Options dialog.
5. Click OK in the message dialog that pops up: [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_options_message.png']
6. Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.

After enabling the collaboration environment, you can log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform and start a collaboration session.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>In the option specification pane, set the <strong>Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform</strong> option to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_in_environment_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> in the message dialog that pops up: <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.</li></ol><p><br />After enabling the collaboration environment, you can log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform and start a collaboration session.</p>
