<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678574 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00446: Enabling and disabling warning messages

- page_id: `169678574`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678574/Enabling+and+disabling+warning+messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-10T18:29:37.596+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Debug report template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can enable or disable any invalid properties, references, and exception messages except invalid syntax messages by editing the **config.xml** file or through Report Environment options. Invalid syntax messages are always enabled.

##### Enabling or disabling warning messages by editing the **config.xml** file

To enable or disable a warning message by editing the **config.xml** file, do one of the following

- Edit the config.xml file in the ...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data folder to enable or disable all templates’ warning messages.
- Edit the config.xml file and create a file in the ...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data\templatefolder folder to enable or disable each template's warning message.

For example, to enable or disable a warning message of the Class Specification report, you need to edit the **config.xml** file in the *...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data\Class Specification Report*folder.

**Adding tags and values to enable or disable warning messages**

The table below lists the warning message types and their tags.

| Warning message type | Tag |
| --- | --- |
| Invalid property | <template.warn.invalid.property> |
| Invalid reference | <template.warn.invalid.reference> |
| Invalid method reference | <template.warn.invalid.method> |
| Exception | <template.warn.exception> |

To enable or disable a warning message by adding its tags and values in the **config.xml** file

1. Apply the following XML with a warning message type and its tags: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Set the boolean value to true to enable the warning message or false to disable the warning message.

An example of how to enable an invalid reference warning message of the Class Specification report is shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing the config.xml file with tags and values to enable an invalid reference warning message of the Class Specification report.

##### Enabling or disabling warning messages through the Report Environment options

To enable or disable a warning message through the Report Environment options

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane, click Report Wizard .
3. Set the value of the following properties in the table under the **Configure report properties** section to**true** to enable the warning message or **false** to disable the warning message. **Property****Description**template.warn.invalid.propertyEnable or disable the invalid property warning message.template.warn.invalid.referenceEnable or disable the invalid reference warning message.template.warn.invalid.methodEnable or disable the invalid method reference.template.warn.exceptionEnable or disable the exception message. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Wizard Environment Options Dialog.png']
4. Click OK to apply the updates.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

To allow the updates set in the Report Wizard **Environment Options** dialog to take effect, you must close the UI before running Report command line.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674866 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00447: Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option

- page_id: `169674866`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674866/Enabling+or+disabling+diagram+aspects+option
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:55.732+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Use Aspects**option allows you to use definable diagram aspects in your project. By default, its value is *false* in the **Project Options** dialog. The procedures below describe how to enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project.

To enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, expand the General property group, and select Diagrams .
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Use Aspects option value to true to enable the functionality, false - to disable it.
4. Click OK .

After enabling the diagram aspect functionality, you are able to create new aspect diagrams, and apply aspects for preexisting diagrams. The following procedures describe how to perform these functions:

- Specifying or removing ignored aspects
- Finding predefined diagram aspects
- Creating custom aspects
- Creating an aspect diagram
- Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams
- Navigation among aspect diagrams
- Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Use Aspects </strong>option allows you to use definable diagram aspects in your project. By default, its value is <em>false</em> in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. The procedures below describe how to enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project.</p><p>To enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> property group, and select <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Use Aspects </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality, <em>false</em> - to disable it.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>After enabling the diagram aspect functionality, you are able to create new aspect diagrams, and apply aspects for preexisting diagrams. The following procedures describe how to perform these functions:</p><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specifying+or+removing+ignored+aspects">Specifying or removing ignored aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Finding+predefined+diagram+aspects">Finding predefined diagram aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+custom+aspects">Creating custom aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+an+aspect+diagram">Creating an aspect diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Applying+or+removing+aspect+for+preexisting+diagrams">Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Navigation+among+aspect+diagrams">Navigation among aspect diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects">Displaying parts and ports according to aspects</a></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673427 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00448: Enterprise Architect Import

- page_id: `169673427`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673427/Enterprise+Architect+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-02T14:05:09.283+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats
- labels: ['enterprise-architect-import', 'ea-import', 'importing-xmi']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

478499527

478499529

478499528

The Enterprise Architect Import has the functionality to import UML models conforming to various XMI versions (including XMI 2.1) from Sparx Systems Enterprise Architect (EA), a modeling and visualization tool based on the UML 2.3 standard. EA has the ability to import and export XMI compliant models, so you can use EA to import UML2.1(XMI2.1). However, XMI models exported from EA contain some XMI conflicts and EA-specific data that do not conform to UML standards.

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to migrate XMI models from EA to a modeling tool flawlessly by using an additional transformation process with a set of mapping rules.

The main purpose of Enterprise Architect Import is to help users who need to import models from EA by managing conflicts that can cause problems while loading the XMI models in the modeling tool, as well as transforming some EA-specific data into UML elements with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1'].

Enterprise Architect Import supports Enterprise Architect Versions 7.1, 7.5, and 8.0 (most of the testing procedures performed on EA 7.1.833 and EA 7.5.847). The feature helps you import and transform an EA exported XMI using the UML2.1 (XMI2.1) option into a model file (*.mdxml).

In addition to the ability to import model elements, the feature also allows for the import of diagrams. The feature supports:

- Class diagrams
- Package diagrams
- Object diagrams
- Component diagrams
- Deployment diagrams
- Use Case diagrams
- Activity diagrams
- Sequence diagrams
- Communication diagrams
- StateMachine diagrams
- CompositeStructure diagrams
- InteractionOverview diagrams

478499525

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

EA has the ability to import and export XMI compliant models, so you can use EA to import UML2.1(XMI2.1). However, XMI models exported from EA contain some XMI conflicts and EA-specific data that do not conform to UML standards.</p><p>Enterprise Architect Import allows you to migrate XMI models from EA to a modeling tool flawlessly by using an additional transformation process with a set of mapping rules.</p><p>The main purpose of Enterprise Architect Import is to help users who need to import models from EA by managing conflicts that can cause problems while loading the XMI models in the modeling tool, as well as transforming some EA-specific data into UML elements with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Enterprise Architect Import supports Enterprise Architect Versions 7.1, 7.5, and 8.0 (most of the testing procedures performed on EA 7.1.833 and EA 7.5.847). The feature helps you import and transform an EA exported XMI using the UML2.1 (XMI2.1) option into a model file (*.mdxml).</p><p>In addition to the ability to import model elements, the feature also allows for the import of diagrams. The feature supports:</p><ul><li>Class diagrams </li><li>Package diagrams </li><li>Object diagrams</li><li>Component diagrams</li><li>Deployment diagrams</li><li>Use Case diagrams </li><li>Activity diagrams</li><li>Sequence diagrams</li><li>Communication diagrams</li><li>StateMachine diagrams</li><li>CompositeStructure diagrams</li><li>InteractionOverview diagrams</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9660b0ef-a2f2-4182-a09f-f9b5a0db7c40"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">478499525</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675419 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00449: Entry Point

- page_id: `169675419`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675419/Entry+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T21:54:16.956+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An entry point connection point reference as the target of a transition implies that the target of the transition is the entry point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state. As a result, the regions of the submachine state machine are entered at the corresponding entry point pseudo states.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An entry point connection point reference as the target of a transition implies that the target of the transition is the entry point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state. As a result, the regions of the submachine state machine are entered at the corresponding entry point pseudo states.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675081 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00450: Enumeration

- page_id: `169675081`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675081/Enumeration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T04:57:02.603+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Data Type
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enumeration defines a kind of data type whose range is a list of predefined values called Enumeration Literals. An Enumeration can contain Operations, but they must be pure functions (this is the rule for all data type elements).

An Enumeration can be specified by changing its property values in the Enumeration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

##### Enumeration Literals

Enumeration Literal defines an element of the run-time extension of an Enumeration data type. It has no relevant substructure, therefore, it is atomic.

To create an Enumeration Literal, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Enumeration Literal**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Enumeration Literal .

##### Enumeration Attributes

To create an Attribute for the Enumeration, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Property**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Property .

As you can see, the Attribute is by default read-only.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676232 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00452: Evaluating operation performance

- page_id: `169676232`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676232/Evaluating+operation+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-08T09:54:03.215+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33447654

33447665

33455046

**On this page**

33447655

You can use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality to see the performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performanceif it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. Matrix and table building can also be analyzed.

700349

##### Evaluating operation performance

To evaluate operation performance

1. In the Main menu, click **Tools** and select **Evaluate Operation Performance**.
2. Click the **Start**button.
3. Run an operation whose performance you want to evaluate.
4. Click the **Stop**button when the operation is finished and you want to stop the evaluation.The performance diagnostics are displayed in the dialog, indicating:
  - The time taken to execute an expression in percentages.
  - The time taken to execute an expression in milliseconds.
  - The number of times the expression was executed.
  - The name of the executed expression.
  - The type of the executed expression. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Evaluate Operation Performance dialog buttons

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Start the evaluation. |
|  | Stop the evaluation. |
|  | Expand the selected result recursively. |
|  | Collapse the selected result recursively. |
|  | Add the element associated with the tested expression to the search results tab. |
|  | Select the element associated with the tested expression in the Containment tree. |
|  | Export the diagnostics to a log file. |

##### Evaluate Operation Performance example

For example, you noticed that opening a particular diagram took longer than expected. A progress bar titled "Adorning" popped up for a few seconds before the diagram opened. To know what caused this performance issue, run the Evaluate Operation Performance tool. In its dialog, clickthe 'Start' button, then open the diagram again reproducing the slow operation, and once it is done, click the 'Stop' button to see the results.You can use this information to investigate the particular expression and improve its efficiency.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675111 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00453: Event

- page_id: `169675111`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675111/Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T04:57:57.346+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An event is the specification of some occurrence that may potentially trigger effects by an object, that is, an event shows what should happen to change a particular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR1']in a system. There are the following event types:

Events are important in diagrams which represent a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR1']of a system. These diagrams are listed in the following table.

| Diagram name | Event usage description |
| --- | --- |
|  | To specify an event type for the . |
|  | To specify a event type for the , or transition to self. |
|  | To specify an event type for the protocol transition, or protocol transition to self. |

When specifying an event type for a transition, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MD2024xR1'] on the diagram pane. The same assignment is valid for a transition to self, protocol transition, and protocol transition to self.

Event types, their functions, samples, and command syntax are described in the following table.

| Name | Function and Sample | Command syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Any Receive Event | A trigger for an AnyReceiveEvent is triggered by the receipt of any that is not explicitly handled by any related trigger. | all |
| Call Event | A call event specifies the receipt by an object of a invoking a call of an :In this example, the call event type is specified with the create() operation. | <operation ()> |
| Change Event | A change event specifies a change in the system configuration that makes a condition true:In this example, the change event type is specified, and its Change expression property is specified as phone is busy. | when (<expression>) |
| Signal Event | A signal event represents the receipt of an asynchronous signal instance. A signal event may, for example, cause a statemachine to trigger a transition:In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as phone answered. | <signal name> |
| Time Event | A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of eventoccurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as 90 sec.For more information, see . | after (<time>) - an event occurrence at the relative timeat (<time>) - an event occurrence at the absolute time |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A signal event may, for example, cause a state<p>machine to trigger a transition:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as <em>phone answered</em>.</p></div></td><td><em>&lt;signal name&gt;</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Time Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of event<br />occurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event11.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as <em>90 sec</em>.</p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying a time for a time event" /></ac:link>.</p></div></td><td><ul><li><em>after (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the relative time</li><li><em>at (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the absolute time</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b6cf08a-97eb-41d3-bb72-a527d2b25d9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="01b10abf-8c58-4f1a-bc27-6b5cc64511ee" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678691 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00454: Examples

- page_id: `169678691`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678691/Examples
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-09-23T07:07:59.454+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Query tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides three sets of sample code to demonstrate some scenarios with the query tool. The codes may not be executable or return a correct result. They are just examples to show you how to use the Query tool and how it works.

**(i) Example 1**

Print any classes whose containing in package com.nomagic.reportwizard.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text

```

**(ii) Example 2**

Print any classes whose containing in model Design and name ends with Bean.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text

```

**(iii) Example 3**

Matches any property element which owner element is class whose name attributes value is exactly equal to foo.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text
property"))
  $p.name
#end]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section provides three sets of sample code to demonstrate some scenarios with the query tool. The codes may not be executable or return a correct result. They are just examples to show you how to use the Query tool and how it works.</p><p><strong>(i) Example 1</strong></p><p>Print any classes whose containing in package com.nomagic.reportwizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e738701c-e2d0-4431-8719-81963989d573"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($c in $Class)
  #if ($c.qualifiedName.startsWith("com.nomagic.reportwizard"))
    $c.name
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a922019-5e2d-4cc6-a459-fca60e4a7bdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
#foreach ($c in $query.get("class[qualifiedName^=com.nomagic.reportwizard]"))
  $c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>(ii) Example 2</strong></p><p>Print any classes whose containing in model Design and name ends with Bean.</p><p>Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="803d35cf-98e7-401a-b6d0-d9c52d12f5c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($match = false)
#macro (recursiveCheck $e)
  #if ($e.elementType == "model" && e.name == "Design")
    #set ($match = true)
  #else
    #recursiveCheck($e.owner)
  #end
#end
 
#foreach ($c in $Class)
  #set ($match = false)
  #recursiveCheck($c)
  #if ($check)
    #if ($c.name.endsWith("Bean"))
      $c.name
    #end
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9dbc818-3887-4f47-b26a-2d4f1fe87dbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
 
#foreach ($c in $query.get("model[name=Design] class[name$=Bean]"))
  $c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>(iii) Example 3</strong></p><p>Matches any property element which owner element is class whose name attributes value is exactly equal to foo.</p><p>Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d3daedf-ec41-4367-9c26-fc8806f22844"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($p in $Property)
  #set ($owner = $p.owner)
  #if ($owner.elementType == "class" && $owner.name == "foo")
      $p.name
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0014fd1e-a197-4487-8d57-d742ca732179"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
 
#foreach ($p in $query.get("class[name=foo] > property"))
  $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678685 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00455: Examples of dependency matrix tool

- page_id: `169678685`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678685/Examples+of+dependency+matrix+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-04-01T07:56:19.453+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Dependency Matrix tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with using the Dependency Matrix tool.

For not empty cells, prints the row element name, column element name and the name of dependency inside the intersecting cell

```text

```

Counts the number of relations for each column.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with using the Dependency Matrix tool.</p><p>For not empty cells, prints the row element name, column element name and the name of dependency inside the intersecting cell</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed1738e6-8664-46d6-947b-14b0bce71753"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
 #set ($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))
 #foreach ($row in $matrix.getRowNodes())
 #foreach ($col in $matrix.getColumnNodes())
 #foreach ($rel in $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col))
 $row.element.name has $rel.name with $col.element.name
 #end
 #end
 #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Counts the number of relations for each column.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db5ce61-0646-4e19-a5cf-541c358b4a50"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
#set ($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))
#foreach ($col in $matrix.getColumnNodes())
#set ($count = 0)
#foreach ($row in $matrix.getRowNodes())
#set ($numberOfrelation = $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col).size())
#set ($count = $count + $numberOfrelation)
#end
$col.element.name has $count relations
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675597 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00456: Excel and CSV Import dialog

- page_id: `169675597`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675597/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:12.912+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1960831571

1960831575

1960831574

**On this page**

5

1960831570

##### Introduction

The**Excel/CSV Import**dialog allows you to specify settings before importing content from an Excel/CSV file to the model. This mechanism allows you to create mapping between an Excel or CSV file columnand target element properties. Once you specify mapping options, you can save your map for reuse or import it as an existing one.

##### Opening the Excel/CSV Import dialog

To open the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog

- On the main menu, select File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . 
The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.

##### Description of the Excel/CSV Import dialog areas

The**Excel/CSV Import** dialogconsists of the following areas:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Import dialog: select file, mapping options, and mapping.

###### Select the Excel/CSV file area

The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024xR1']]]>

The Select Excel/CSV file area contains the buttons described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (mandatory) | If you select From file system, the Open dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field.If you select From model, the Select Excel/CSV file dialog opens. The selected attached file name is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field. |
|  | Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file. |

###### Mapping Options area

The Mapping Options area allows you to specify mapping options. These options define how file columns meet the element properties after data import. The following table lists all available options and provides their descriptions in detail.

| Mapping option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Import Type (mandatory) | Select either elements or instances you want to create in the model after data import. |
| Element Type (mandatory) | Click and select the type of the elements that are created in the model after data import. |
| Instance Classifier (mandatory) | Click and select the element which is set as classifier for all imported i. |
| Stereotype | Click and in the Select Stereotype dialog select one or more stereotypes to apply them for created elements after the import.You can select stereotypes only if the Element Type option value is selected. |
| Properties to Map (mandatory) | Click and in the Properties to Map dialog select one or more properties of the element type. The imported elements contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.You can specify properties only if the Element Type option value is selected. |
| Nested Properties to Map (mandatory) | Click and in the Nested Properties to Map dialog select one or more nested properties of the . The imported instances contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.You can specify nested properties only if the Instance Classifier option value is selected. |
| Target Scope | Click and in the Select Owner dialog select or create the element that contains the created elements after the import.To create a new element using the Select Owner dialoga. Click the Creation Mode button.b. Click the Create Owner button.c. Select an appropriate element from the list. |
| Search for Elements In | Select the search scope of imported elements:Target Scope Only (default) - searches for an imported element only under the element selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.Target Scope Recursively - searches for imported elements recursively from the element which is selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option might slowdown the import speed. |
| Search for References In | Select search scope of elements that are referenced with imported elements:Target Scope Only (default) - searches for referenced elements only under the element selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.Entire Model - searches for referenced elements recursively from the Model package. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option might slowdown the import speed. |
| Sheet | Select the Excel file sheet you want to import. The first sheet is selected by default.It is available only for Excel files. |
| First cell | Select the Excel file cell you want to start importing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.It is available only for Excel files. |
| CSV Delimiter | Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify a custom delimiter in the next box, select Other. By default, the comma (,) is selected.It is available only for CSV files. |
| Identification Property | Select the property that identifies if the imported element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.Select the Default value to identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID. The rest of the values are the same as those selected in the Select Properties to Map option but only .If the imported element Identification Property option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Source by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value.Click and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Target by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value.Click and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end. Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| First Row Contains Headings check box | Clear the check box if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings. The check box is selected by default. |
| Import Plain Text check box | Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default. |

###### Mapping area

The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between element properties and Excel/CSV file columnsby creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:- To create a new map or re-map individually, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/left tree. 
 - To create a new map or re-mapall columns in the ordinal position, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area. 
-To delete mapping individually, select an appropriate map and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete all mappings, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area.

If you do not define your own mapping (that is, the mapping area is empty), the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.

The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.

1960839142

**Related pages**

- [Importing data from Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files)
- Saving Import Map
- Working with map groups
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024xR1']
- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select either elements or instances you want to create in the model after data import.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_type.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Element Type</strong> (mandatory)</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the type of the elements that are created in the model after data import.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Instance Classifier </strong>(mandatory)</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the element which is set as classifier for all imported i<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nstances]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Stereotype</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Select Stereotype</strong> dialog select one or more stereotypes to apply them for created elements after the import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e87a7900-18cd-49e5-b5f8-ce628d06cea6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select stereotypes only if the <strong>Element Type</strong> option <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aef887d2-d037-41d2-b0e7-4def59e68ca2">value is selec</ac:inline-comment-marker>ted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Properties to Map </strong>(mandatory)</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Properties to Map </strong>dialog<strong> </strong>select one or more properties of the element type. The imported elements contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80800671-f0d8-49f8-9f49-94d6cc1f4c32"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify properties only if the <strong>Element Type</strong> option <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="dc42d4ad-ac5a-4388-b20a-c58464cbd95c">value is selected.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Nested Properties to Map</strong> (mandatory)</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click </span><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and in the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Nested Properties to Map </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dialog</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">select one or more nested properties of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link>. The imported instances contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="284a03f4-55d0-4d32-89aa-b514e66e6a97"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify nested properties only if the <strong>Instance Classifier</strong> option value is selected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Target Scope</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog select or create the element that contains the created elements after the import.</p><p>To create a new element using the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog</p><hr /><p>a. Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Creation Mode</strong> button.<br />b. Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Owner</strong> button.<br />c. Select an appropriate element from the list.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Search for Elements In</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the search scope of imported elements:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Target Scope Only</strong> (default) - <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8462cf7b-8d63-40ea-b1a2-3a4d478b5288">searches for</ac:inline-comment-marker> an i<span>mported element only under the element selected as the <strong>Target Scope</strong> option value. <span>If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.</span><br /></span></p></li><li><strong>Target Scope Recursively - </strong>searches for i<span>mported elements re<span>cursively </span>from the element which is <span>selected as the </span><strong>Target Scope</strong><span> option value</span>. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option <span>might slowdown the import speed</span>.</span></li></ul></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Search for References In</strong></td><td><p>Select search scope of elements that are referenced with imported elements:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Target Scope Only</strong> (default) - searches for <span>referenced elements only under the element selected <span>as the </span><strong>Target Scope</strong><span> option value</span>. <span>If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.</span></span></li><li><strong>Entire Model</strong> - searches for <span>referenced elements recursively from the Model package. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option might slowdown the import speed.</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Sheet </strong></td><td><span>Select the Excel file sheet you want to import. The first sheet is selected by default.</span><br /><span>It is available only for Excel files.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>First cell </strong></td><td><span>Select the Excel file cell you want to start importing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.</span><br /><span>It is available only for Excel files.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>CSV Delimiter</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify a custom delimiter in the next box, select </span><strong>Other</strong><span>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.</span><br /><span>It is available only for CSV files.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the property that identifies if the imported element already exists in the model. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p>Select the <span><strong>Default</strong> value to identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID. The</span> rest of the values<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc110f7-7263-4bc5-b5cb-2c8877ac45a1"> are the same</ac:inline-comment-marker> as those selected in the <strong>Select Properties to Map </strong>option but only <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fc117fa-6270-4213-b0a3-6821ec11692f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If the imported element </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.</span><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is displayed o<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3dba51e8-a50b-46b4-88bd-7527da156654">nly if a</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element Type</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> option value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Target by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c1989d2-3dfd-4c23-b5ac-e5bae50d230e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is displayed option value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span> option</span> value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.</span><span><br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>First Row Contains Headings </strong><span>check box</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Clear the check box </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings.</span><span> The check box is selected by default.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box    </td><td colspan="1">Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><br />Mapping area</h4><p>The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between element properties <span style="color: <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674521 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00457: Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog

- page_id: `169674521`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674521/Excel+and+CSV+Sync+Options+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:53.671+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

155935942

155935944

155935941

**On this page**

43

155935943

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog allows you to specify settings before syncing data between an Excel/CSV file and a table. You can select files for syncing and define syncing and mapping options in this dialog.

##### Opening the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog

To open the**Excel/CSV Sync** **Options** dialog

- In the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] and do one of the following: - Select the Sync Options command. - Select the Excel/CSV File > Select File . Click the Details button on the left bottom corner of the dialog. The Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog opens.

##### Description of the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog areas

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog consists of the following areas:

44

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog: select file, sync, mapping options, and mapping.

###### Select Excel/CSV file area

The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024xR1']]]>

You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.

The Select Excel/CSV file area contains the buttons described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | If you select From file system, the Open dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field.If you select From model, the Select Excel/CSV file dialog opens. The selected attached file name is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field. |
|  | Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file. |

###### Sync Options area

The Sync Options area contains one option that allows you to specify the sync mechanism behavior when importing data to a table if rows in your file are deleted. All available values are described below.

| Sync option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| If rows in the file are deleted | Delete elements from the model - deletes the same elements that were deleted in the file from your model.If some elements are read-only in Excel file, they cannot be deleted from the model.Remove elements form the table - removes the same elements that were deleted in the file only from the modeling tool table, not from the model.Mark as obsolete (default) - The table Legend automatically displays and highlights the removed elements (obsolete) in red. |

###### Mapping Options area

The Mapping Options area allows you to specify mapping options. The following table lists all available options.

| Mapping option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Sheet | Select the Excel file sheet you want to sync. The first sheet is selected by default.It is available only for Excel files. |
| First cell | Select the Excel file cell you want to start syncing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.It is available only for Excel files. |
| CSV delimiter | Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select Other. By default, the comma (,) is selected.It is available only for CSV files. |
| Identification Property | Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.The Default value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID first. The rest of the values are the same as columns shown in the table of your model but only of .If the imported element Identification Property option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Source by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Target by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value.If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| First row contains headings check box | Clear the check box if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings. The check box is selected by default. |
| Sync Plain Text check box | Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default. |

###### Mapping area

The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:- To create a new map or re-map one by one, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/left tree. 
 - To create a new map or re-map all columns in the ordinal position, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete mapping one by one, select an appropriate map and click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete all mappings, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area.

If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.

The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.

155947252
