### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3126b638-4596-423f-9686-5e386ce05b5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155935942</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d735619-0d95-4c5e-a5b7-95108de13e80"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155935944</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a0ad9ef-5276-471e-90ba-e534eef2eb66"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155935941</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47fda864-033f-479c-bd5a-c6281876bdb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d408736d-e6dc-4d33-9030-9471486da3f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155935943</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog allows you to specify settings before syncing data between an Excel/CSV file and a table. You can select files for syncing and define syncing and mapping options in this dialog.</p><h3>Opening the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog</h3><p>To open the<strong> Excel/CSV Sync</strong> <strong>Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and do one of the following:<br /> - Select the <strong>Sync Options</strong> command.<br /> - Select the <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Select File</strong>. Click the <strong>Details</strong> button on the left bottom corner of the dialog.<br />The <strong>Excel/CSV Sync</strong><strong> Options</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><h3>Description of the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog areas</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog </span> consists of the following areas:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29a4b8f2-7ef1-4b78-ad05-96d4a80f0635"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_options_dialog_areas.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog: select file, sync, mapping options, and mapping.</h6><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select Excel/CSV file area </span><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d0cf46f0-4761-4cac-a109-32aeb45bd64e">The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to attach the file to a project >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f63921-f8cc-4623-addb-aa4ddb019d71"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br />The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Select Excel/CSV file area</span> contains the buttons described in the following table.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_file.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If you select <strong>From file system</strong></span>, the <strong>Open</strong> dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> field.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">I<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">f you select <strong>From model</strong></span>, the <strong>Select Excel/CSV file</strong> dialog opens</span>. The selected attached file name is displayed in the <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> field.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Refresh Mapping" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Refresh Mapping" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sync Options area</span><br class="auto-cursor-target" /></span></h4><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Sync Options area contains one option that allows you to <span class="confluence-link">specify <span class="confluence-link">the sync mechanism behavior</span> when importing data to a table if rows in your file are deleted. All available values are described below. <br /></span></span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Sync option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>If rows in the file are deleted</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Delete elements from the model </strong>- deletes the same elements that were deleted in the file from your model.<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dba0abc1-a161-44f6-9860-442f95a3acd4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If some elements are read-only in Excel file, they cannot be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Remove elements form the table </strong>- removes the same elements that were deleted in the file <strong>only</strong> from the modeling tool table,<strong> not</strong> from the model.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Mark as obsolete</strong><span> (default) - <span>The table</span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Legends"> Legend</a><span> <span><span>automatically</span></span> displays and highlights the removed elements (obsolete) in red.</span></span></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></h4><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Mapping Options area</span><br /></span></span></h4><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Mapping Options area <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">allows you to</span></span> specify mapping options. The following table lists all available options.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.454%;" /><col style="width: 84.546%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Mapping option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Sheet </strong></td><td>Select the Excel file sheet you want to sync. The first sheet is selected by default.<br />It is available only for Excel files.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>First cell </strong></td><td>Select the Excel file cell you want to start syncing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.<br />It is available only for Excel files.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>CSV delimiter</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select <strong>Other</strong>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.<br />It is available only for CSV files.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>The </span><span><strong>Default</strong> value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2186fac8-f852-4b88-bbfd-f8bd51c81c9c">ID first</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The</span><span> rest of the values </span>are the same<span> as columns shown in the table</span><span> of your model but only of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f620f492-819c-4f3e-867a-e5fcf8d1b4f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the imported element </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>This option is displayed o</span>nly if a<span>ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong>Element Type</strong><span> option value</span> </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Target by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e82097bf-6da5-413a-bdfe-00a194dd62d6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the <strong>Element Type</strong> option value </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.</li><li>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value.</li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>First row contains headings </strong>check box</td><td>Clear the check box <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings.</span> The check box is selected by default.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Sync Plain Text</strong> check box</td><td colspan="1">Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Mapping area</h4><p>The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns</span> by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="auto-cursor-target" />          -  To create a new map or re-map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one, drag</span> columns from the left tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">right tree</span> onto columns in the right tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> left tree</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a new map or re-map</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">all columns in the ordinal position</span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Map 1:1" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_1_1_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span>.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">delete mapping</span> one by one</span></span></span>, select an appropriate map and c</span>lick<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:title="Delete " ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete " ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the mapping area<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To delete all mappings</span></span></span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Delete All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete All" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_all_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. <br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded18c57-dbe0-4132-898a-917401c53c18"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb3948b1-3153-4fe5-aae3-1113b27e23d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155947252</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><span class="current"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></span></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><span class="current"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ac:link></span></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The warning message, see the figure below, will open whenever an exception, such as IndexOutOfBoundException, occurs during the report generation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exception Warning Message.

## PAGE 00459: Exception Handler

- page_id: `169675118`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675118/Exception+Handler
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:04.118+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943151593

943151596

943151594

An Exception Handler element specifies a body to execute if the specified exception occurs during the execution of the protected node.

The Exception Handler may be drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin or from an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024xR1'] to an Input Pin, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Representation of an Exception Handler.

943151592

**Related pages**

- Specification window
- Model Elements
- Editing property values
- Class diagram
- Action

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673084 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00460: Excess ActivityParameterNodes with Return Parameter

- page_id: `169673084`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673084/Excess+ActivityParameterNodes+with+Return+Parameter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-10T08:24:51.877+02:00`
- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

Excess return Parameters

**Description**

This validation rule checks if no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for the Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Activity Parameter Node

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for the Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**There are two Activity Parameter Nodes with *return*parameters, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by changing at least one of the parameter's direction from *return* to a different one.********

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>Excess return Parameters</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for the Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Activity Parameter Node</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for the Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Excess ActivityParameterNodes with Return Parameter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excess ActivityParameterNodes with Return Parameter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>There are two Activity Parameter Nodes with <em>return </em>parameters, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by changing at least one of the parameter's direction from <em>return</em> to a different one.<strong><br /></strong><strong> </strong></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673086 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: Excess Return Parameters

- page_id: `169673086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673086/Excess+Return+Parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-10T08:34:49.424+02:00`
- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

Excess return Parameters

**Description**

This validation rule checks if no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for an Opaque Behavior or an Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Parameter

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure that no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for an Opaque Behavior or an Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### There are two parameters with a *return*direction, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by changing at least one of the parameter's direction from *return* to a different one.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>Excess return Parameters</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for an Opaque Behavior or an Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Parameter</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure that no more than one parameter with a return direction is specified for an Opaque Behavior or an Activity Parameter Node (under the same owner/Activity?).</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Excess Return Parameters.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excess Return Parameters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">There are two parameters with a <em>return </em>direction, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by changing at least one of the parameter's direction from <em>return</em> to a different one.</h6>
````

## PAGE 00462: Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `169673057`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673057/Exchanging+data+between+tables+and+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:46:55.418+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can exchange data between the tables on your modeling tool and Excel/CSV files. Depending on the expected end result, choose the most appropriate data exchange method from those listed below:

- (import/export)
- (import/export)
- (import/export)
- (import)
- (export)
- (export)

| Data Exchange Method | Description | Key Benefits | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | You can transfer information from any textual resource, including HTML, by simply copying and pasting data. Data should be in a tabular format to be pasted correctly. copy and paste | No additional tools or plugins are required.Data can be copied from external resources to modeling tool tables, from modeling tool tables to other resources, and between the cells in the modeling tool table.New elements are created in the model upon copying and pasting data from the external resource to the modeling tool table.Elements already existing in the model are automatically updated upon copying. are set automatically after pasting data into the modeling tool table. | Copy/paste destinationRowsColumns |
| Pasting data to the Excel fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format (converted to the style used by Excel) |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the CSV fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the modeling tool tableTextBooleansNumbersExcel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool) |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the specific table cellsTextBooleansNumbers |  |  |  |
| LimitationsMerged cells in the Excel/CSV file.Non-editable cells.If the number of columns in the Excel/CSV file and the modeling tool table does not match, some columns are left out.No data mapping.ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.Images are unsupported. |  |  |  |
|  | You can sync information in tables and Excel/CSV files using the Excel/CSV Sync feature that comes packed with the modeling tool. The mechanism is bidirectional and ensures the continuity between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables.excel csv sync | Excel/CSV Sync enables you to:link an existing Excel/CSV file with a modeling tool table for a quicker and more convenient data import/export.create a new Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table and link it at the same time.open the linked Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table.import data from a selected sheet in the Excel file into a modeling tool table.conveniently track data import status.specify sync and mapping options using the . | Import/Export destinationRowsColumnsExporting data to the Excel/CSV fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format (converted to the style in the Excel file only)Importing data from CSV to the modeling tool tableTextBooleansNumbersExcel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool) |
|  |  | Data Mapping During data import, elements in the model are searched by the following criteria:stereotype tag value. It must be of a String type and have the Is ID property value set to true.name. |  |
|  |  | CustomizationPossibility to:specify the Excel/CSV file location: file system or model.specify mapping options, including the sheet number (Excel files) to import data from, the first cell to start the data import from, and the delimiter type (CSV file).display/hide row headings.specify how the modeling tool table is updated when elements are deleted from the source file, i.e., Excel/CSV.customize data mapping between Table columns and Excel/CSV file columns. |  |
|  |  | LimitationsSyncs only one data unit (table) from a single sheet at a time.Non-editable cells (data import only).Impossible to import/export relationships between elements.ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.Images are unsupported. |  |
|  | Data Hub provides a complete solution for requirement management. It can be used to copy, link to one another, and synchronize requirements with system design models, e.g., Use Cases, artifacts, and test cases. Before you can use it, you need to install it via the . data hub | Elements can be transferred to and from CSV file repositories. Supports almost all types of MagicDraw model elements. Supports external requirement management tools, including IBM® Rational® DOORS®, IBM® Rational® DOORS® Next Generation, and HP Application Lifecycle Management.Provides unidirectional and bidirectional synchronization capabilities between target and source nodes. Allows creating and maintaining DHLink and DHTrace links between requirements and SysML use cases and test cases. | Exporting data to the CSV fileTextNumbersBooleansModel elements (they are converted to textual data)Importing data to the modeling tool tableTextNumbersBooleansDelimiters (indicates separate table columns)Elements in textual data (e.g., SysML requirements)Each column in the CSV file represents a column in the modeling tool table.Data MappingTwo modes are used:Group type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into groups and can be mapped to the selected model element all at once).Individual type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into a tree-view structure and can be mapped to different model element types).CustomizationAttribute mapping is customizable via the .LimitationsThe Excel file is not supported by the plugin.CSV file with columns is needed before DataHub can be used to export model to the CSV file. DataHub does not support the direct data import into the table, i.e. the CSV file is imported as model elements. |
| (based on the framework)export button | Report Wizard is a report engine that supports text-based templates to generate report documents from the models. report wizard | Reports can be generated from a predefined template.The format of the generated report file depends on the selected template.Custom templates can be created (some knowledge is required).The template style can be easily formatted. For instance, you can add page numbers, headers and footers, and a table of contents.A template can be "attached" to the project to share it with other users who open the same project.You can set up an automatic report generating and uploading to a predefined remote server using the Command line (CMD). | Exporting data to the Excel fileRowsColumnsHeadingsTextModel elements (converted to textual data)The applied style, e.g., colors.ImagesData is exported using text-based templates.CustomizationPossibility to:create a new or modify existing template variables, e.g., title, author, and purpose.create a new customized template.specify the report file output location.specify how empty values will be displayed.specify the desired scope for generating a report, for example, export several tables at the same time.Five keyboard shortcuts that allow generating reports with the last used options from the most recent template are assigned automatically.Reports can be automatically generated using the Command line.LimitationsGenerates only one report at a time.No default template for the data export to the CSV file. |
| The Export button allows exporting tables from the modeling tool to a .csv or .xlsx file with a single click. | Enables to export data from the modeling tool without creating a reference, i.e., without linking the table and the exported file. | Exporting data to the ExcelData from the modeling tool table is exported using the style specified in the template file. RowsColumnsHeadingsTextModel elements (converted to textual data)The applied style, e.g., colorsImagesCustomizationPossibility to specify the specific diagram to export and the location of the exported file.Exporting data to the CSV fileTextNumbersBooleansModel elements (they are converted to textual data)LimitationsExports only one diagram at a time. |  |

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><p><strong>Customization</strong></p><ul><li>Possibility to specify the specific diagram to export and the location of the exported file.</li></ul><p><strong>Exporting data to the CSV file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Model elements (they are converted to textual data)</li></ul><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li>Exports only one diagram at a time.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676159 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00463: Executable concepts

- page_id: `169676159`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676159/Executable+concepts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:20.025+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We call executable such concepts that can be specified by some expressions and calculated. In modeling tools developed by No Magic, executable are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1'] - that are predefined or custom constraints having a purpose to validate a model or particular model elements.
- Derived properties - properties that are automatically calculated from the other properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MD2024xR1'] - that are special collections of model elements.
- Executable opaque behaviors - reusable expression fragments callable form other executable concepts.
- Metric definitions - calculate aggregate parameters of models.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Macro Engine Documentation' space='MD2024xR1'] - a script that is created using a particular script language. With the help of macros, you can control items that are allowed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MD2024xR1'] - a behavior with implementation-specific semantics.

In a modeling tool, executable concepts are defined in one of the languages that our modeling tool can evaluate:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting started with specifying criteria' space='MD2024xR1'] - a graphical way to construct expressions out of predefined blocks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object Constraint Language' space='MD2024xR1']ObjectConstraint Language .
- Binary - an expression that is written in Java, compiled and added to our modeling tool with a plugin.
- One of the following scripting languages:

- 
  - Groovy
  - JavaScript
  - JavaScript Rhino
  - Jython

#### INFO: Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages can be plugged into our modeling tool, if they have JSR-223-compliant implementation.

You can test and evaluate your expressions with the help of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Expression Evaluation' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>We call executable such concepts that can be specified by some expressions and calculated. In modeling tools developed by No Magic, executable are:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - that are predefined or custom constraints having a purpose to validate a model or particular model elements.</li><li>Derived properties - properties that are automatically calculated from the other properties.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Smart Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Smart packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - that are special collections of model elements.</li><li>Executable opaque behaviors - reusable expression fragments callable form other executable concepts.</li><li>Metric definitions - calculate aggregate parameters of models.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Macro Engine Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Macros]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a script that is created using a particular script language. With the help of macros, you can control items that are allowed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Developer Guides Home" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Developer Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Opaque Behavior" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opaque behaviors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a behavior with implementation-specific semantics. </li></ul><p>In a modeling tool, executable concepts are defined in one of the languages that our modeling tool can evaluate:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StructuredExpression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a graphical way to construct expressions out of predefined blocks. </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Object Constraint Language" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Object </span><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Language</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Binary - an expression that is written in Java, compiled and added to our modeling tool with a plugin.</li><li>One of the following scripting languages:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Groovy</li><li>JavaScript</li><li>JavaScript Rhino</li><li>Jython</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="229657b4-a0f4-4a51-b96a-604b480a4ca3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional scripting languages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional scripting languages can be plugged into our modeling tool, if they have JSR-223-compliant implementation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can test and evaluate your expressions with the help of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Expression Evaluation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Expression Evaluation tool]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e911d4d6-d4ab-4178-b243-3086a134c0f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c26fc222-1788-441e-9539-ff5fff4d89eb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675121 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00464: Execution Environment

- page_id: `169675121`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675121/Execution+Environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-02T14:06:37.198+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include *<OS>*, *<workflow engine>*, *<database system>*, and *<J2EE container>*. 
 
Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

The Execution Environment dialog box contains the following specific panes:

- Nested Nodes : lists nodes defined (nested) in the Execution Environment .
- Deployed Artifacts : an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.

You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include <em>&lt;OS&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;workflow engine&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;database system&gt;</em>, and <em>&lt;J2EE container&gt;</em>. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.   </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span class="hcp2">Execution</span> <span class="hcp2">Environment</span> <span class="hcp1">dialog box contains the following specific panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Nested</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="hcp1">defined (nested) in the</span> Execution Environment<span class="hcp1">.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">: </span>an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678768 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00465: Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report

- page_id: `169678768`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678768/Exercise+Building+a+simple+class+diagram+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:31:54.734+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351791524

1351791526

1351791523

**On this page**

3

1351791526

#### Building a Class Diagram Report

In this exercise, you will learn how to create a simple Class Diagram report. A Class Diagram report must contain the following information:

- A class diagram's name.
- A diagram.
- A table that describes the class (we will call it a class description table) based on the diagram shown below.
- The table must have the following structure: Class NameDocumentation:AttributesAttribute Type 1NameAttribute Type 2NameAttribute Type 3NameAttribute Type 4Name
- The layout of the report should look like the example below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout of a basic class diagram on the diagram pane.

###### Instructions

The first and most important step in creating a report is setting up the document and its layout.

1. Open Microsoft Word.
2. Create a table that will be used as the basic layout for a class description table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout for a class description table in a Word document containing the name of the class, the attributes, and the relationships.

As shown in the preceding figure, a diagram will appear at the top of the report followed by a class description table. Accordingly, each diagram on the Class diagrams list has a class description table describing each component in the diagram. Follow the next steps to create the document template:

Iterates through a list of diagrams.

1. Print the diagram and its name.
2. Get a list of class elements that are displayed in the diagram.
3. Iterate through the list of class elements.
4. Create a class description table for each class element in the list. 
 
The steps listed above contain two **#foreach** loops: the outer loop that iterates through a list of diagrams, and the inner loop that iterates through a list of Class elements. The list of diagrams in a project is represented by **$Diagram**. 
****
5. At the top of the page of the word document type: text **$Diagram** is a list of all the diagrams in a project. This means that a project will be in the list if it contains more than one type of diagram. To filter out other types of diagrams, add the **#if** directive with a conditional statement to test whether or not **$diagram** is a class diagram: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Next, you need to get all the elements contained in the diagram by using **$report**. **$report** is a standard tool containing helpful methods such as **getDiagramElements()** that returns a list of elements inside a diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the example above, **$eList** has been assigned with a list of Elements inside **$diagram**. Now, you need to create an inner loop that will cover the table that you created earlier, and then filter out the elements inside the diagram whose type is not Class element. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Enter the information of the class in the table by calling its attributes, etc.: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Class Name and Documentation values are obtained by referencing the name and documentation attributes of **$e**.
9. Use the **#forrow** directive as the attribute to print rows of the class attribute type (**$[att.type.name](http://att.type.name)**) and the class attribute name (**$[att.name](http://att.name)**).
10. Save the file as Rich Text Format. Open the modeling tool and draw a simple class diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A simple class diagram.
11. Add the template to Report Wizard, and generate a class diagram report using this template. The following figure shows the result of the generated report. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The generated report of the class diagram in the Word document.

#### Glossary

- Class diagram A class diagram is a diagram in which the primary symbols in the contents area are class symbols.

#### More information about working with report templates using Report Wizard

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e12d15d3-6420-444a-8179-f0f32c0b4da1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47143e0c-92ac-4b9c-8343-71b2621f86dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791526</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74249153-07ca-47dc-9721-37000a1d5d98"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d472b43d-4178-4b4e-8f60-e0435b019635"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe2c7d0-108c-4290-b063-95b5dc85d9e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791526</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Building a Class Diagram Report</span></h2><p>In this exercise, you will learn how to create a simple Class Diagram report. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675420 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00466: Exit Point

- page_id: `169675420`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675420/Exit+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T21:55:24.632+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An exit point connection point reference as the source of a transition implies that the source of the transition is the exit point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state that has the exit point connection point defined. When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An exit point connection point reference as the source of a transition implies that the source of the transition is the exit point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state that has the exit point connection point defined. When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675298 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes

- page_id: `169675298`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675298/Expansion+Region+and+Expansion+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T21:43:08.129+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes can be drawn in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can create those elements using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1']diagram palette:

- The Expansion Region button. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_region.png']
- The Input and Output Expansion Nodes are located under the Object Node button group. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_nodes.png']

To display Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border:

1. Select the Expansion Region shape of the Expansion Nodes you want to display.
2. Perform one of the following steps: - From the shortcut menu, select Display > Display Expansion Nodes . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Expansion Nodes.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677699 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00468: Exporting a package to a new 3DEXPERIENCE server project

- page_id: `169677699`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677699/Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+3DEXPERIENCE+server+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-28T14:41:58.497+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of model decomposition. Then you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using a 3DEXPERIENCE server project in your project' space='MD2024xR1'] that project in other projects. You can export a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new **3D**EXPERIENCE server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the **Package Dependencies** panel : [ATTACHMENT filename='package_dependencies.png'] You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies. Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.
5. If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click OK.
6. Wait for package export to finish.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024xR1'] the newly created server project in other projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Then you can </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="be32ff2e-d034-4d44-9f5c-1007d3a26682"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using a 3DEXPERIENCE server project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that project in other projects. You can e</ac:inline-comment-marker>xport a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. You can use the part of the model you moved in <em>more than one</em> project.</p><p>To export a package to a new <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to New Server Project</strong>. The <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Package Dependencies</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> panel</span></span>:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_dependencies.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting a package to a new 3DEXPERIENCE server project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><u>You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies.</u> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a00a888-a41d-4faa-a604-6568215da5e2">Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li>If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click <strong>OK.</strong></li><li>Wait for package export to finish.<br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adad3d55-5d95-4bac-836b-0e6fe375f07c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package <em>Project Usages</em> in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="443c6de6-a6b9-4900-b26c-4db8287e9c4e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="451920f1-a81f-432c-b351-7090b8391e58">Model Browser</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_export.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting a package to a new 3DEXPERIENCE server project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the newly created server project in other projects.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677434 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00469: Exporting a package to a new server project

- page_id: `169677434`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677434/Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+server+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:26.208+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To export packages to a new server project, you must have:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] permissions on the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] permission for the selected category a new server project will be stored in

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of the model decomposition.It is followed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024xR1'] that project in other projects.Exporting a package to a new server project is necessary when you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. When the message asking to show used read-only projects appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation).
5. When the message asking to check for dependencies between the package and the rest of the model appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation). If the package depends on other projects, eliminate those dependencies before exporting it.
6. In the Project Name dialog, do the following:
  1. Change the default name of the new server project, if you choose.
  2. Select a category to store the new project.
  3. Add a comment for the project.
  4. Click OK .
7. Wait while the export completes.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024xR1'] the newly created server project in other projects.

## PAGE 00470: Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates

- page_id: `169678235`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678235/Exporting+images+for+TXT+and+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:15:01.689+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050292507

2050292511

2050292505

**On this page**

43

2050292510

Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates returns absolute paths, e.g., *file:/C:/Users/username/Desktop/output/out_files/_18_5_1_133803de_1519700709957_140433_4668.jpg*. There are five functions for handling paths of exported images as follows.

###### **$image.createSilentImage(image)**

Create an image without returning a path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |

###### **$image.createImageWithRelativePath(image)**

Create an image and return a relative path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |
|  | - | java.lang.String | A relative path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.getRelativePath(image)**

Return a relative path only.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A relative path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.createImageWithAbsolutePath(image)**

Create an image and return an absolute path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |
|  | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.getAbsolutePath(image)**

Return an absolute path only.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path of the exported image. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
