<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675741 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00489: Extract Structure Wizard

- page_id: `169675741`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675741/Extract+Structure+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:16.251+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Extracting > Extracting in Composite Structure diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

266372509

266372521

266372508

**On this page**

****

266372510

The **Extract Structure Wizard** is used for extracting a selected part of a composite structure diagram to a separate diagram. Using this wizard, you can:

- Create and specify the target diagram that will be created as the result of the extraction. The selected part will be extracted to the newly created diagram.
- Select the ports that will be created in a new diagram. The ports are listed for each intersected connector from the source diagram.
- Specify a referencing element (in this case, a property) that is created in the source composite structure diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.

To start the **Extract Structure Wizard**

1. In the composite structure diagram, select a part that you want to extract.
2. From the selected part’s shortcut menu, choose Refactor > Extract . The Extract Structure Wizard opens.

This section describes components of the **Extract Structure Wizard**, such as text boxes and buttons. The Extract Structure Wizard consists of three steps:

1. Specification of a new element.
2. Creation of ports.
3. Creation of a property.

###### Step #1. Specification of a new element

In this step, you can specify a diagram into which the extracted part will be moved and an owner wherein this new diagram will be created. You can also define the type of the newly created classifier element that will own the diagram. The class element and the composite structure diagram type are selected by default. The name of the composite structure diagram will be the same as the specified name of the classifier element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Type element name | Type the name of the new element, that is, classifier, wherein the extracted part will be stored. |
| Select element type | Open the list and select the element type. The default element type is a class. By using the customized plugins, you can add custom elements to the element type list. |
| Select owner | In the model tree, select a package, profile, or model as an owner of the newly created element. You may also:Create a new owner. Click the Create Owner button, select an element type, and specify its properties.Clone an existing package, profile, or model. In the model tree, select an owner, click the Clone button, and specify properties of the cloned element. |
| Select diagram type | Open the list and select the diagram type for the extracted part. The default diagram type is a composite structure diagram. By using the customized plugins, you can add custom diagrams to the diagram type list. |

After you have specified the new element name, type, owner of the diagram, and the type of the new diagram, do one of the following:

- Click Next , if you want to specify ports and to define a referencing element. The next wizard step opens.
- Click Finish . The selected part of a composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram that is specified in this step. Instead of the extracted part, the selected element type is created in the composite structure diagram referencing to the newly created diagram. The second and the third wizard steps are skipped, that is, the default options are taken.

###### Step #2. Creation of ports

In this step, there are listed the port(s) that will be created for the:

- intersected connector(s)
- incoming connector(s)
- outgoing connector(s)

The **Create ports** list is empty unless there are intersected, incoming and/or outgoing connector(s) selected in the source composite structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After you have selected the ports, do one of the following:

- Click Back to return to the first step.
- Click Next , if you want to define a property (that is, referencing element). The next wizard step opens.
- Click Finish . The selected part of the composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram. The third wizard step is skipped, that is, the default options are taken.

###### Step #3. Creation of a property

A property is created as the result of the extraction. The property is created under the classifier that was specified in the first wizard step. The symbol of the property is created in the source composite structure diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After you have selected the parameters, do one of the following:

- Click Back to return to the previous step.
- Click Finish . The selected part of a composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram.

266381145

**Related pages**

- Extracting
- Extracting in Composite Structure diagram
- Working with model elements

By using the customized plugins, you can add custom diagrams to the diagram type list.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>After you have specified the new element name, type, owner of the diagram, and the type of the new diagram, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> , if you want to specify ports and to define a referencing element. The next wizard step opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>. The selected part of a composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram that is specified in this step. Instead of the extracted part, the selected element type is created in the composite structure diagram referencing to the newly created diagram. The second and the third wizard steps are skipped, that is, the default options are taken.</li></ul><h4>Step #2. Creation of ports</h4><p>In this step, there are listed the port(s) that will be created for the:</p><ul><li>intersected connector(s)</li><li>incoming connector(s)</li><li>outgoing connector(s)</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0e309a7-c52c-4287-b7d5-4df133ec3729"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Create ports</strong> list is empty unless there are intersected, incoming and/or outgoing connector(s) selected in the source composite structure diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title=" Extract Wizard. Create ports" ac:alt=" Extract Wizard. Create ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extrac_structure2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extract Structure Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After you have selected the ports, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Back</strong> to return to the first step.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong>, if you want to define a property (that is, referencing element). The next wizard step opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>. The selected part of the composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram. The third wizard step is skipped, that is, the default options are taken.</li></ul><h4>Step #3. Creation of a property</h4><p>A property is created as the result of the extraction. The property is created under the classifier that was specified in the first wizard step. The symbol of the property is created in the source composite structure diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Extract Wizard. Create a property" ac:alt="Extract Wizard. Create a property"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extrac_structure3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extract Structure Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After you have selected the parameters, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Back</strong> to return to the previous step.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>. The selected part of a composite structure diagram is extracted to a new diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ee22667-9479-4eb8-9334-c43ec18f0162"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">266381145</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting">Extracting</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting+in+Composite+Structure+diagram">Extracting in Composite Structure diagram</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Working+with+model+elements">Working with model elements</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675700 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00490: Extracting

- page_id: `169675700`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675700/Extracting
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:15.021+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

256887376

256887397

256887387

Extracting feature is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.

The extraction functionality allows for moving a selected part of a diagram to a newly created diagram. A referencing element will be created in a place of the extracted element(s) in the source diagram.

In order to make the diagrams more readable and usable, you can extract a specific part of the diagram to another diagram. By using the extraction, you can reuse that part of the diagram to simplify a complicated system.

###### Definitions

**Source diagram**

A source diagram is the diagram from which the elements are extracted.

**Target diagram**

A target diagram is the newly created diagram resulting from the extraction. The selected elements are moved from the source diagram to the target diagram.

**Extracting**

Extracting means moving selected elements from one location to another. In other words, the selected elements are deleted from the source diagram and moved to the newly created target diagram.

**Referencing element**

A referencing element is created as the result of the extraction. The referencing element is created in the source diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.

A referencing element contains the reference to the target diagram. Therefore, double-clicking the referencing element on the diagram pane or in the Model Browser will open the target diagram.

**Intersected relationship**

An intersected relationship is the relationship selected in the source diagram. The intersection appears when the selection intersects the relationship - one end element of the relationship is selected, but not both.

You can use the extraction functionality in the following diagrams:

- Extracting in Sequence Diagram
- Extracting in Activity Diagram
- Extracting in State Machine diagram
- Extracting in Composite Structure diagram

To extract a selected part

1. On the diagram, select a part that you want to extract.
2. From the selected part's shortcut menu, select Refactor > Extract . An extract wizard specific to the particular type of diagram opens.

256887374

**Related pages**

- Extracting in Sequence diagram
- Extracting in Activity diagram
- Extracting in State Machine diagram
- Extracting in Composite Structure diagram
- Working with model elements

Therefore, double-clicking the referencing element on the diagram pane or in the Model Browser will open the target diagram.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Intersected relationship</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">An intersected relationship is the relationship selected in the source diagram. ## PAGE 00491: Extracting in Activity diagram

- page_id: `169675710`

263888951

263888962

263888952

The extraction functionality allows for extracting a selected part of an activity diagram to a newly created diagram. After extraction, in the source diagram, a call behavior action is created instead of the extracted part. That is, the call behavior action is a referencing element that references the newly created activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you can see in the example, during extraction, the selected part of the activity diagram is moved to the newly created *Review Items* activity diagram. In the source diagram, a *Review Items* call behavior action is created in place of the selected elements.

263888950

266320618

266320608

With the SysML plugin, the extracting in composite structure diagram feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The extraction functionality allows for extracting a selected part of a composite structure diagram to a newly created diagram. After extraction, a property is created in the source diagram in place of the extracted part(s). That is, the property is a referencing element that references the newly created activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you can see in the example, during extraction, the selected part of the composite structure diagram is moved to the newly created *Loaning* composite structure diagram. In the source composite structure diagram, a *loaning* property is created in a place of the selected elements.

266320596

- Extract Structure Wizard
- Extracting
- Working with model elements

As you can see in the example, during the extraction, the selected part of the state machine diagram is moved to the newly created Login state machine diagram. In the source diagram, a Login submachine state is created in place of extracted elements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675712 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00495: Extraction rules in Activity diagram

- page_id: `169675712`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675712/Extraction+rules+in+Activity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:15.738+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Extracting > Extracting in Activity diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

263943965

263943977

263943967

Extracting rules are the rules that should be followed in order to proceed with the correct extraction. For example, there can be invalid selections in a source diagram. Or there are unsupported elements that cannot be extracted. Without knowing the rules you will not be able to proceed with extraction (you will have to change your selected elements and/or re-select elements according to the rules) or you may lose some data. Here are the extraction rules:

###### Rule 1. Only one incoming and/or outgoing control flow is allowed for a valid diagram extraction

If the selected part of an activity diagram has two or more intersected incoming or outgoing control flows, the extraction is not valid.

Let’s analyze an example of the extraction when two incoming control flows are selected in the activity diagram as depicted in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After you start the extraction, you will be warned that only one incoming control flow and only one outgoing control flow is allowed. Click the **OK** button to close the message and then change the selected elements in the activity diagram.

###### Rule 2. The selected elements should exist in the same owner, action, or activity partition

The extraction will be invalid if:

- The selected elements do not belong to the same owner. For example, if one of the selected actions is located in a structured activity node and the other does not belong to the same structured activity node. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity15.png']
- Selected elements are in different activity partitions. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity17.png']

If an attempt is made to extract using an invalid selection, a warning will be displayed. Make a valid selection in order to proceed with the extraction.

###### Rule 3. The intersected Exception Handler is not extracted to a new diagram

The Exception Handler may be drawn either from an Action to an Input Pin or from an Output Pin to an Input Pin. The extraction procedure differs based on the chosen option. Let’s analyze examples when the intersected exception handler is selected in the source activity diagram.

**Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin**

1. In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity24.png']
2. Start the extraction. The following message is displayed. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Click **OK**. Extracting an action with an Exception Handler that is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin is not allowed. Select other elements to be extracted and try extracting again.

**Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin**

1. In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action and the Output Pin. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png']
2. Start the extraction. The following message is displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_wizard_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png']
3. Click **Yes** to continue with the extraction. See the example of the extraction result in the following figures. After the extraction, the referencing element is created in the source activity diagram in place of the selected exception handler and the *Confirm Return* action. The path of the exception handler is not represented in the new target diagram because in this case, the exception handler does not have the two symbols between which it could be added. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click No to close the message. The extraction is canceled and you can select elements for extraction again.
- Use Undo to restore the original element and its data.

263943964

**Related pages**

- Extracting
- Extracting in Activity diagram
- Extract Activity Wizard
- Working with model elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ac46fa6-9d01-406f-adcc-101d805e2c4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70bd2fe5-eac8-46ba-8b47-52d9d5bca462"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943977</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d523c53-fb12-4941-837e-b2cccb459a1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943967</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Extracting rules are the rules that should be followed in order to proceed with the correct extraction. For example, there can be invalid selections in a source diagram. Or there are unsupported elements that cannot be extracted. Without knowing the rules you will not be able to proceed with extraction (you will have to change your selected elements and/or re-select elements according to the rules) or you may lose some data. Here are the extraction rules:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fca64af9-ecb2-4d0c-b54a-6f864262ac56" /></p><h4>Rule 1. Only one incoming and/or outgoing control flow is allowed for a valid diagram extraction</h4><p>If the selected part of an activity diagram has two or more intersected incoming or outgoing control flows, the extraction is not valid.</p><p>Let’s analyze an example of the extraction when two incoming control flows are selected in the activity diagram as depicted in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity28.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After you start the extraction, you will be warned that only one incoming control flow and only one outgoing control flow is allowed. Click the <strong>OK</strong> button to close the message and then change the selected elements in the activity diagram.</p><h4>Rule 2. The selected elements should exist in the same owner, action, or activity partition</h4><p>The extraction will be invalid if:</p><ul><li>The selected elements do not belong to the same owner. For example, if one of the selected actions is located in a structured activity node and the other does not belong to the same structured activity node.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity15.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Selected elements are in different activity partitions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity17.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>If an attempt is made to extract using an invalid selection, a warning will be displayed. Make a valid selection in order to proceed with the extraction.</p><h4>Rule 3. The intersected Exception Handler is not extracted to a new diagram</h4><p>The Exception Handler may be drawn either from an Action to an Input Pin or from an Output Pin to an Input Pin. The extraction procedure differs based on the chosen option. Let’s analyze examples when the intersected exception handler is selected in the source activity diagram.</p><p><strong>Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin </strong></p><ol><li>In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity24.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Start the extraction. The following message is displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:height="165"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Click <strong>OK</strong>. Extracting an action with an Exception Handler that is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin is not allowed. Select other elements to be extracted and try extracting again.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong>Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin</strong></p><ol><li>In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action and the Output Pin.<br /><ac:image ac:height="132"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Start the extraction. The following message is displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:height="197"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_wizard_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to continue with the extraction. See the example of the extraction result in the following figures. After the extraction, the referencing element is created in the source activity diagram in place of the selected exception handler and the <em>Confirm Return</em> action. The path of the exception handler is not represented in the new target diagram because in this case, the exception handler does not have the two symbols between which it could be added.<br /><ac:image ac:height="243"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin_final.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b6d8b6c-21e5-4cd6-9619-c6bedce3bde9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click <strong>No</strong> to close the message. The extraction is canceled and you can select elements for extraction again.</li><li>Use <strong>Undo</strong> to restore the original element and its data.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bd0f00c-76ce-4791-80ca-acf5b7795522"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943964</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting">Extracting</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting+in+Activity+diagram">Extracting in Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extract+Activity+Wizard">Extract Activity Wizard</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Working+with+model+elements">Working with model elements</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677715 space=MD2024xR1 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00496: FAQ about used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE

- page_id: `169677715`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677715/FAQ+about+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T15:04:53.966+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Can I import a used project?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform does not support importing used projects.

**Can I remove a used project but keep the references?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform doesnot support recovered elements. Thus, you cannot remove used projects while keeping the references. You only have the option to remove a used project together with its references, as shown below:

****

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Removing a used project.

**I cannot open a project that uses other projects and see a message that a used project is missing. Why?**If you do not have access to a used project or it is deleted, you cannot open the main project. You must have access to all content that is used in the main project.

**Why can I not see the option to export packages to a new project?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform does not support exporting packages to a new project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Thus, you cannot remove used projects while keeping the references. Y<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ou only have the option to remove a used project together with its references, as shown below:</span></p><p><strong> </strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_used_project.png" /></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removing a used project.</h6><p><strong>I cannot open a project that uses other projects and see a message that a used project is missing. Why?<br /></strong>If you do not have access to a used project or it is deleted, you cannot open the main project. You must have access to all content that is used in the main project.</p><p><strong>Why can I not see the option to export packages to a new project?</strong></p><p>The <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform does not support exporting packages to a new project.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678777 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00497: FAQs

- page_id: `169678777`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678777/FAQs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:30:45.838+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1208381210

1208381231

1208381209

**On this page**

3

1208381221

##### Generating an output report document from Report Wizard

To generate an output report document from Report Wizard

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select the template or create a new one. Click Next .
3. Specify Report Data and add information for the variables. Click Next .
4. Select the document scope from the corresponding packages. Click Next .
5. Select the output location, images format, and text for blank fields.
6. Click Generate .

##### Adding a new report template

To add a new report template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Click New . The New dialog will open.
3. Enter the template name and description. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='button-1.png'] button to specify the template file location.
4. Click Create .

Once a new template has been created, Report Wizard will add a folder with the entered template name and save the selected template in this folder.

##### Removing a template

To remove a template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template from the list and click Delete .

Once removed, the selected template with its folder and reports cannot be recovered.

##### Modifying a template file

To modify a template file

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template from the list and click Open . The default editor and a template file for editing will open.
3. Modify the template and perform the save command in the editor.

A different editor will be used for each template format. For example, MS Word could be used for **.rtf* template modification, or Macromedia Dreamweaver could be used for **.html* template editing.

##### Adding **Report Data** into the template

To add **Report Data** into the template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template or create a new one. Click Next . The Select Report Data pane will appear.
3. Click New . The New dialog will appear.
4. Enter the Report Data name and description . Click Create . A new Report Data will be created. In the next step, you may add new fields or delete the existing ones.

##### Removing **Report Data** from the template

To remove **Report Data** from the template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template or create a new one. Click Next . The Select Report Data pane will appear.
3. Select the Report Data from the list and click Delete .

##### Setting the default viewer option for the report file

To set the default viewer option for the report file

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select the Display in viewer after generating report check box. The report output will be displayed in the default editor or browser.

##### Changing an output image format

To change an output image format

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select the output image format from the Report Image Format box.

The supported image formats include:

****.PNG*** and ****.JPG*** 
for all supported report templates. 
****.SVG*** 
for *HTML, XML* and Text report templates. 
****.EMF*** and ****.WMF*** 
for *RTF, OOXML* and *Text* report templates

##### Changing an empty value configuration

To change an empty value configuration

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope each time.Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select an option for output on blank fields:

- 
  - Select Display empty value or Display value as , and select NA or
  - Enter the text to represent other than null value when the template query fields return empty.

##### Adding a new variable

To add a new variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed.
3. Click the New button when the Variable pane opens. The Variables dialog will open.
4. Enter the name of a new variable and its value (the value can be modified later after the variable has been created).
5. Click Create .

##### Deleting a variable

To delete a variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed.
3. Select a field and click Delete when the Variable step opens.

You cannot recover any deleted **Report Data**.

##### Modifying a variable

To modify a variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed with the steps.
3. Select a field and modify its value when the Variable step opens. The value can be modified in the properties list or in the Field Value box below the properties list.

##### Selecting a package for report generation

To select a package for report generation

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. The package tree will open. Select the package of the project and click the Add button to add the elements from the package to the report.

Clicking the **Add** button will add only the selected element, not its children, to the report scope. In order to include all children, click the**Add Recursively** button instead.

##### Generating a normal text output format

To generate a normal text output format

1. Create the query in the text file using the text editor; see the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering Query in the Normal Text Template.
2. Create a new template in the **Report Wizard** dialog through the **New Template** dialog; see figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating New Text Template.
3. Select Text Template, see the first figure below, and generate the output report; see the second figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting Text Template to Generate Report Output. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Text Output Report.

##### Calculating formulas on exported XLSX file

If formulas are used in a template file, Excel will not automatically calculate them when the file is opened. It is caused by a limitation of the Velocity language, as the engine is not designed to work with Excel files. See the options below for fixing this issue.

To calculate formulas on exported XLSX file

- A single formula is calculated when the text field is double-clicked.
- Search and Replace triggers the formula's recalculation. Press Ctrl+H, search for "=" and replace it with any symbol that is not used in the file, e.g., "[". Then, after replacing the symbols back, all formulas are recalculated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the <strong>Next</strong> button to proceed.</li><li>Click the <strong>New</strong> button when the <strong>Variable</strong> pane opens. The <strong>Variables</strong> dialog will open.</li><li>Enter the name of a new variable and its value (the value can be modified later after the variable has been created).</li><li>Click<strong> Create</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Deleting a variable</h3><p>To delete a variable</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog will open. </li><li>Select a template and <strong>Report Data</strong>. Click the <strong>Next</strong> button to proceed.</li><li>Select a field and click <strong>Delete</strong> when the <strong>Variable</strong> step opens.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3fb1f41-0149-448f-9ed1-a201e9b6828c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot recover any deleted <strong>Report Data</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying a variable</h3><p>To modify a variable</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog will open.</li><li>Select a template and <strong>Report Data</strong>. Click the <strong>Next</strong> button to proceed with the steps.</li><li>Select a field and modify its value when the <strong>Variable</strong> step opens. The value can be modified in the properties list or in the <strong>Field Value</strong> box below the properties list.</li></ol><h3>Selecting a package for report generation</h3><p>To select a package for report generation</p><hr /><ol><li>On the<strong> Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog will open.</li><li>Select a template and <strong>Report Data</strong> and specify the variables. Click the<strong> Next</strong> button to proceed.</li><li>The package tree will open. Select the package of the project and click the<strong> Add</strong> button to add the elements from the package to the report.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b8af434-01b8-450c-b0dc-906a4488a010"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Clicking the <strong>Add</strong> button will add only the selected element, not its children, to the report scope. In order to include all children, click the<strong> Add Recursively</strong> button instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Generating a normal text output format</h3><p>To generate a normal text output format</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create the query in the text file using the text editor; see the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Entering Query in the Normal Text Template" ac:alt="Entering Query in the Normal Text Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="notepad-screenshot-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="FAQs" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Entering Query in the Normal Text Template.</p></li><li><p>Create a new template in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog through the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog; see figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Creating New Text Template" ac:alt="Creating New Text Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-template-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="FAQs" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Creating New Text Template.</p></li><li><p>Select Text Template, see the first figure below, and generate the output report; see the second figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting Text Template to Generate Report Output" ac:alt="Selecting Text Template to Generate Report Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rep-wiz-new-template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="FAQs" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Selecting Text Template to Generate Report Output.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text Output Report" ac:alt="Text Output Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="notepad-screenshot-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="FAQs" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Text Output Report.</p></li></ol><h3 style="text-align: left;">Calculating formulas on exported XLSX file</h3><p style="text-align: left;">If formulas are used in a template file, Excel will not automatically calculate them when the file is opened. It is caused by a limitation of the Velocity language, as the engine is not designed to work with Excel files. See the options below for fixing this issue.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To calculate formulas on exported XLSX file</p><hr /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>A single formula is calculated when the text field is double-clicked.</li><li>Search and Replace triggers the formula's recalculation. Press Ctrl+H, search for &quot;=&quot; and replace it with any symbol that is not used in the file, e.g., &quot;[&quot;. Then, after replacing the symbols back, all formulas are recalculated.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675900 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00498: Favorites

- page_id: `169675900`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675900/Favorites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-28T17:10:27.684+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can mark any element as a favorite, including diagrams, types, packages, etc., that you regularly use in your model and need to access quickly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using favorites saves time, as you are able to:

- Navigate quickly to any desired place in your model.
- Easily narrow the search scope to get rid of unneeded elements and thus find desired elements faster.
- Easily narrow the scope for model analysis.

In a server project, each user has his own list of favorites.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678385 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00499: Features and layout

- page_id: `169678385`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678385/Features+and+layout
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-05-17T09:18:37.427+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard > Web Publisher 2.0 report
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1335772202

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1335772203

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1335772203

The Web Publisher 2.0 report consists of four panels:

1. Containment panel - This panel contains two tabs: Containment and Diagrams . The Containment tab shows data of a project in a tree structure, and the Diagrams tab shows all diagrams in a project. In each tab, there are four toolbar buttons as follows.
  - Collapse All : Collapses all expanded elements in the active tab.
  - Collapse Selected Recursively : Collapses all child elements of the selected element in the active tree recursively.
  - Open In New Tab : Opens a new tab of the specific element. When you select an element in the tab and click this button, the selected element and its children will be listed as a new tree recursively in a new tab.
  - **Favorite**: Navigates to the favorite elements listed in this menu. When you select a favorite element, you will be navigated to that favorite element in the active tab. NoteIf there is no favorite element, the **Favorite** button will be hidden.
2. Content panel - This panel shows an element's content.
3. Search panel - This panel contains a Quick Search box. You can search for an element in a project by either typing in a specific keyword or by using a regular expression as a keyword.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Web Publisher 2.0.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Containment tab.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Diagrams tab.

The Content panel of Web Publisher contains three tabs: Diagram, Specification, and Appears in. You can click any element in the Containment tree of Web Publisher to open three tabs.

- The Diagram tab shows diagram images.
- The Specification tab shows elements specification.
- The Appears in tab shows you all diagrams in which a particular element is present. You need to select the element in the Containment tree to open the Appears in tab.

| Tab name | UI component |
| --- | --- |
| Diagram |  |
| Specification |  |
| Appears in |  |

1335772201

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2024xR1']

## PAGE 00500: File tab

- page_id: `169675827`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675827/File+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:17.427+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Active check box rule

61930727

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Active check box rule

61930730

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Active check box rule

61930729

Use the**File** to add, edit, or delete hyperlinks to files on your file system.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of the File tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text "SysML specification" links to the SysML specification pdf document from your computer.

The table below describes the boxes and buttons found in the **File** tab.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text to display box | Shows the text to be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field, too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text. |
| Type the file name box | Allows typing or pasting the directory to reference to an appropriate file, or click next to the box to select it from your file system. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used directories. You can select them to fill in the Type the file name box. |
|  | Opens the the structure of your file system. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously used directories from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

61930726

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR1']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

You can select them to fill in the <strong>Type the file name </strong>box.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="27"><ri:attachment ri:filename="go_to_hyperlink_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="File tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the the structure of your file system.</td></tr><tr><td>The <strong>Clear</strong> button</td><td>Deletes all previously used directories from the <strong>Select from list</strong> box.</td></tr><tr><td>The <strong>Active</strong> check box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Activates the hyperlink if selected. ## PAGE 00501: File tool

- page_id: `169678206`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678206/File+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T12:33:21.088+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267293507

1267293511

1267293503

**On this page**

33

1267293511

$file allows generating an output report file in a template file.

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | void |  |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| Return | - | void |  |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename, importObject)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile, importObject)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename, outputFileName, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | The output filename. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile, outputFileName, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | The output filename. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateType, templateFilename, outputname, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateType | java.lang.String | A template type such as rtf, html, or htm. |
| templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |  |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | An output filename without a filename extension. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateType, attachedFile, outputname, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateType | java.lang.String | A template type such as rtf, html, or htm. |
| attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |  |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | An output filename without a filename extension. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.create(templateFilename)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| context | java.util.Map<String, Object> | An additional context added to a new template. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output filename. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.exists(pathname)**

Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | pathname | java.lang.String | A pathname string. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | True if and only if a file or directory denoted by a specific pathname exists; otherwise, false. |

****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
| name | java.lang.String | A filename (excludes the filename extension). |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory and name. |

For example:

```text

```

##### ****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
|  | name | java.lang.String | A filename (excluding the extension). |
|  | fileType | java.lang.String | A file type, such as rtf, txt, and html. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory, name, and file type. |

For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ba4a030-65c7-4273-823c-97917cefc0bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.silentCreate('html',$attachedFile,'overview','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)</strong><br /><br /><br /></td><td>templateType </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A template type such as rtf, html, or htm.</td></tr><tr><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFileName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output filename without a filename extension.</td></tr><tr><td>importObject</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>void </td><td>- </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(templateFilename)</strong></h3><p>A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b805ae0a-fb47-4a7a-b841-f2d11ce67be6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('overview.html')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong> Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>templateFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(attachedFile)</strong></h3><p>A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object.
