It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b399fb7-8d35-4ec3-8603-3dddfbba447d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path <br />to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57f32d7f-5374-4aad-95fb-0bae58b41316"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb8a654-eab2-47f6-ad09-f7696141b25e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc32ce4-9e74-4469-afb8-d6949a127b29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c477362-e675-4790-ac30-9226ac65ca7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156632f6-c44c-41b0-ac55-86b60402559c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td> An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7eedfdf7-a0a6-44fd-a99e-252291e3e3e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td> contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89bf2ffa-199a-4dba-816c-644b91b0e25d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ee3f106-bc69-4f6b-a535-0ee7556a25e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report <br />filename.</td></tr><tr><td> context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context <br />added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to <br />generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29501624-dafa-48c3-8c3e-775bc804ebef"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="625ab104-2416-42d6-9de4-a9ba3ed8e694"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.copy('icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> inputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input filename </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24c27b18-a4f5-4e27-b697-be8abcbb628f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ $file.copy('icon.gif','icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td> inputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.exists(pathname)</strong></h3><p>Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="314ef9bc-4803-426d-abd4-b76ea31633b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.exists("$template.resourcesLocation/myimage.png")
````html
$file.exists("C:/myfolder/myimage.png")
$file.exists("mytemplate.txt")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>pathname </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname string. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>True if and only if a file <br />or directory denoted by <br />a specific pathname exists; <br />otherwise, false. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"> Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td> name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excludes <br />the filename extension).</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory and name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f0d0e6c-2f43-4bb7-8592-7b1e84f8e817"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.computeName('actors', $ac.ID, 'html')
output: 'actor/_123456789.html']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong><br /></strong></h3><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excluding <br />the extension).</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>fileType  </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A file type, such as rtf, <br />txt, and html. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory, name, <br />and file type. </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679262 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00502: Files of Properties

- page_id: `169679262`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679262/Files+of+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:58:21.531+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: ['supported-languages']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419473883

419473885

419473884

Code can be generated out of prepared UML models. Mapping should be implemented between the identifiers used in the UML model and the language to which the model is being generated. This mapping includes the following sections:

- Build-in types (their default values)
- Generalization types
- Possible class declarations. Attributes and operations declaration and visibility modifiers
- Code generation options.

A separate *prop* file is created for every language supported in modeling tools developed by us. Files are located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\data* folder. The file name pattern is *lang.prop*, where lang stands for the name of the programming language.

The following table lists supported languages:

| Supported Language | File of Properties |
| --- | --- |
| Java | java.prop |
| C++ | C++.prop |
| CORBA IDL | idl.prop |
| JAVABytecode | javabytecode.prop |
| DDL | ddl.prop |
| IDL | idl.prop |
| XML Schema | xmlschema.prop |

Files of language properties are separated into sections where all related elements are grouped. You can edit existing entities, add new ones, and change the default values.

We strongly recommend that you edit default values only. In general, all sections have a list of possible and default values for the element.

419473882

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674418 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00503: Filling the table with elements

- page_id: `169674418`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674418/Filling+the+table+with+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-07-08T13:14:43.032+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Generic table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill the Generic Table for the specified Element Type in the following ways:

43

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']

##### Creating new elements

At least one element type must be selected in the **Element Type** box before adding a new element.

To create a new element

1. Click the **Add New** button on the Edit toolbar. If there is more than one element type selected, a submenu with the list of available element types opens. Only element types available to create in a possible diagram owner which contains the Generic Table will be displayed on the submenu.
2. Select an element type. The element of the selected type will be added to the last row of the table and to the model.
3. Name the new element in the table.

##### Adding existing elements from the model

Elements you want to add should correspond to selected element types.

To add an existing element from the model

1. Click the Add Existing button on the Edit toolbar. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1']**Select Element** dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_select_elements.png']
2. Select the element you want to add to the Generic Table. This element and its name will be added to the table as the last row. Use the **Multiple Selection**mode to add more than one element at a time.

If you have not defined the element type before starting to add elements to the table, it is automatically set after the first element is added to the table. The type of this element becomes the value of the **Element Type** box.

To add an existing element, you can drag a selected element to the contents of the Generic Table directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1']. 
If you drag a container, for example, a package or a smart package, the Generic Table will display its contents, but not the container itself.

##### Copying and pasting elements from another resource

You can copy the data from other sources such as HTML, Excel, Word, and paste it to the Generic Table. The main rules for successful copy-and-paste operations in Generic Tables are described on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Copying and pasting data' space='MD2024xR1'] page. The copy-and-paste operations create new elements or update already existing elements in the Generic Table.

To create elements in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Generic Table.The data types of columns in the Generic Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Generic Table.
3. In the Criteria area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three-dot-btn1.png'] to specify the Element Type.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Show Columns** button and choose the columns that you want to fill with the copied information. The cells must be editable in the Generic Table.
5. Do one of the following: 
 - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
 - In the main toolbar, select **Edit** > **Paste.** 
Each row displays one element with its properties. If the element already exists in the model, the information updates.If elements have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set only for pasted elements in the Generic Table after the copy-and-paste command. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Element Numbering** dialog. The following example demonstrates the main steps for pasting data into a Generic Table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can fill the Generic Table for the specified Element Type in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d4c545c-e30e-451a-a8c7-386ad65d0b76"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing elements from Excel or CSV files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Creating new elements</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d33d694-134e-46ef-ac06-71b9a4ad3f9f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>At least one element type must be selected in the <strong>Element Type</strong> box before adding a new element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a new element</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button on the Edit toolbar. The copy-and-paste operations create new elements or update already existing elements in the Generic Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create elements in the table by pasting data from another columns in the Generic Table must be compatible with copied information.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Open the Generic the Generic Table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br /> - In the main toolbar, select <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Paste.</strong><br />Each row displays one element with its properties.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91ed84e9-89ff-46a9-a83e-5992bd300d7e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the element already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If elements have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set only for pasted elements in the Generic Table after the copy-and-paste command. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following example demonstrates the main steps for pasting data into a Generic Table.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Creating elements in generic table from another resource" ac:alt="Creating elements in generic table from another resource"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_elements_from_another_resource.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filling the table with elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674466 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00504: Filling the table with instances

- page_id: `169674466`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674466/Filling+the+table+with+instances
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-07-11T07:37:37.378+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following outlines how to fill the Instance Table for the specified classifier:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024xR1'] [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_example_two_classifiers.png']

create_new_instanceTo create a new instance

1. On the table toolbar, click the **Create** button. The new instance can be created only if the classifier is already specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create** button is not available. If there is more than one classifier specified, from the menu of the button, select a classifier for which you need to create an instance. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The new instance is added to the Instance Table as the last row.
2. Type the instance name and specify slot values.

create_new_with_partsTo create a new instance with parts

1. On the table toolbar, click **Create**>**Create with Parts**. The new instance with nested parts is created if:the classifier is specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create with Parts** button is not available.the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024xR1'] in the Instance Table.

add_existing_instancesTo add an existing instance

1. On the table toolbar, click Create > Add Existing . The Select Instance Specification dialog opens.
2. In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select an element and add it to the Selected elements list. You can select more than one element. Elements available for selection:
  - Instances of one or more classifiers specified for the table. An instance is available if it belongs to at least one of the classifiers.
  - Packages that contain these instances. In this case, the contents of a selected package, but not the package itself, will be added to the table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The selected instance is added to the table as the last row. You can drag a selected element to the contents of the Instance Table directly from the Model Browser.To select adjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.To select nonadjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.

copy_instancesTo copy and paste instances from another resource

1. Copy the content of the table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Instance Table.The data types of columns in the Instance Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Instance Table where you want to paste the copied data.
3. Make sure the Classifier is specified in the Criteria area.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Columns** button and choose columns of instance slot values that you want to fill with copied information. The cells must be editable in the Instance Table.
5. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one instance with its slot values. If an instance already exists in the model, the information updates.If instances have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted instances in the Instance Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 00505: Filter By Package Imports filter

- page_id: `169675875`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675875/Filter+By+Package+Imports+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-01T14:37:25.485+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1038774317

1038774329

1038774315

**On this page**

****

1038774319

##### Restricting type selection list to imported elements

To restrict the type selection list to imported elements

1. Create a Package diagram.
2. Drag the package with typed elements, as well as packages with types, on the diagram pane.
3. Create a package import between them by clicking Package Import in the diagram palette. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_package_import_button.png']

To apply the filter

1. Click a typed element to open the **Select Type** dialog.
2. Apply the **Filter By Package Imports filter**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As a result, the type selection list is restricted to imported elements only.

There are more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'] you can use for the same purpose.

##### Different abstraction levels

You can also use a Model instead of a package, in which case, the elements cannot be accessed from outside, and you can only select the types from packages that are owned within the Model element.

To only show elements from the desired abstraction level

1. Right-click the package to which you want to restrict your type selection, and select Refactor > Convert To > Model .
2. Select a typed element nested in that model, and specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_port_type.png']
3. Apply Filter By Package Imports to only list the elements that belong to the same abstraction level.

##### Validation

Use active validation to evaluate which element types are out of the scope specified by package imports.

1. Go to Analyze > Validation > Validate.
2. In the Validation dialog , select UML correctness constraints from the Validation Suite drop-down list.
3. Click Validate .
4. In the Validation Results panel, right-click the element you want to change or unset type for, and select Change Type , Unset Type , or Create Package Import Relationship accordingly. [ATTACHMENT filename='elements_out_of_scope.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673635 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00506: Filtering

- page_id: `169673635`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673635/Filtering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-06-01T08:24:11.323+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

632682683

632682687

632682686

INLINE

The modeling tool filtering mechanism allows filtering types of model elements in particular lists. For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.

To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Containment** tab

1. In the Model Browser, open the Containment tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tab_filter.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_type_dialog.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the Containment tab.
  - Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the **Containment** tab. An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the **Hide Metatype** property set to *true*in its customization.
4. Click OK . Items in the Containment tab are now filtered.

Containment

Model

Containment

To filter the types of model elements when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Searching' space='MD2024xR1']

1. From the Edit main menu, select Find . The Find dialog opens.
2. Next to the Type box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png']
3. Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.
4. Click OK . You are now back to the Find dialog then click Find . The Search Results tab opens in the Model Browser.

632682682

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024xR1']

For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:link-body><strong>Containment</strong> tab</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Containment</strong> tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Filter</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tab_filter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br />The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_type_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24dfbfa7-b88f-41ad-be30-c4af96af58c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the <strong>Hide Metatype</strong> property set to <em>true </em>in its customization.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span>Click <strong>OK</strong>. Items in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are now filtered.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63141028-7df0-4345-b256-5b4121ee2267"><ac:rich-text-body>All elements in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are displayed in hierarchical order. If you clear the <em>Model</em> check box, no elements will be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab. The same is valid for all element types – if you clear the check box next to the type of element that has inner elements in the Model Browser, you won't be able to see these inner elements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To filter the types of model elements when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[searching]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, select <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Find</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. You are now back to the <strong>Find</strong> dialog then click <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Search </strong><strong>Results</strong> tab opens in the Model Browser.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="087068b7-4811-4101-9f5a-6bf4b2630565"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682682</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674695 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00507: Filtering by Legend items

- page_id: `169674695`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674695/Filtering+by+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-08T13:46:14.597+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1947346740

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1947346743

2010910600

**On this page**

**43**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1947346742

Diagrams showing multiple aspects of the system are often difficult to understand and analyze since they are quite crowded. Filtering diagrams by Legend items enables users to reduce diagrams to the chosen views only, i.e., hide unnecessary layers or show only a few necessary ones in order to make them more comprehensible. The feature is especially useful when there is a need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024xR1'] to stakeholders only one (e.g., electrical) or a few aspects displayed in the diagram.

700500

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Filtering a diagram by the selected Legend item *iElectricity*. 
Filtering preference is set to *Show*(on the left) and *Hide***(on the right).

##### Filtering diagram by Legend items

Once you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR1']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024xR1'] a Legend and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1'] for it, you can filter your diagram by Legend items to highlight the chosen layers.

You can filter the diagram directly from:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1'] (mainly used when the Legend shape is not displayed).

##### Selecting filtering preferenceSelecting filtering preference

Before using the filter, you need to select the filtering preference. There are two options you can choose from: *Show* or *Hide*.

Selecting the *Show* preference allows you to display the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while hiding all the other element symbols. Selecting the Hide preference allows you to hide the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while all the other element symbols of the diagram are displayed. Keep in mind that only one preference can be selected - the *Show* and *Hide* preferences cannot be combined.

###### Selecting the filtering preference for a specific diagram

To select the filtering preference for a specific diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, select either *Show* or *Hide*to set the preference to filter your diagram by. The default filtering preference is *Show*. You can change it via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1'].If there are multiple Legends on the diagram, the same preference applies to all of them.If the applied filter is saved, the filtering preference remains as is once the diagram is closed and reopened. Otherwise, the preference resets to default.
4. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Filtering comments using different filtering preferences:

- Hide : all comments are hidden;
- Show : all comments are shown including anchored elements.

###### default filtering preferenceSelecting the default filtering preference

The default filtering preference is *Show*, however, you can change it via the Project Options. The change applies to all existing and future diagrams of the project, except fordiagrams with an already applied filter.

To select the default filtering preference

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. Set the **Filtering Preference** option to *Show* or *Hide*. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Filtering from legend symbolFiltering when Legend shape is displayed

If the Legend shape is displayed on the diagram pane, you can filter your diagram by Legend items directly from it. To be more specific, you can either filter your diagram from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1'] or the selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1']. The latter method comes in handy when there is a need to filter the diagram by multiple Legend items at once.

###### **Filtering from smart manipulator**Filtering by Legend items from the smart manipulator toolbar

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the Legend shape on the diagram pane, select a Legend item to filter your diagram by.
3. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The diagram is filtered by the selected Legend item, meaning that the diagram is limited to one specific aspect. If the filtering preference is *Show*, the diagram displays only the element symbols filtered by the Legend item, while the rest of the element symbols are hidden. If the filtering preference is *Hide*, the diagram hides the element symbols filtered by the Legend item while the rest of the element symbols are displayed. TipTo filter the diagram by multiple Legend items, repeat the procedure.

To filter a diagram by all Legend items at once

1. Select a Legend shape on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_all_legend_items.png']
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_button_smart_manipulator.png'] . The diagram is filtered by all items specified in the Legend, meaning that all diagram aspects are either hidden or displayed.

###### **Filtering from legend item shortcut menu**Filtering by Legend items from the shortcut menu

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend items from the shortcut menu

1. In the Legend shape on a diagram, select the Legend item(s) to filter your diagram by.
2. Right-click and then in the shortcut menu, select Filtered . [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_multiple_legend_items.png']

##### Filtering from diagram toolbarFiltering when Legend shape is not displayed

Alternatively, you can filter your diagram by the selected Legend items directly from the diagram toolbar. This is the primary filtering method when the Legend shape is not displayed on the diagram. In this case, you can select whether to filter the diagram by a single or multiple Legend items as well.

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item(s)

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, specify the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR1'].
4. Select the Legend items to filter your diagram by.
5. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Managing and removing the applied filter

Once diagram layers are filtered, the following notification appears above the diagram:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click **Clear**to clear the applied filter.
- Click **Save** to keep the filter applied after the diagram is reloaded.

Alternatively, to clear the applied filter, you can:

- click[IMAGE alt='' src='']in the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.
- uncheck the box next to **Filtered** in the Legend item shortcut menu.
- clear the corresponding boxes in the **Filter by Legend Items**([IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Filter by Legend Items**) dialog.

The diagram filtered by Legend items is read-only, meaning that it cannot be edited unless the filter is removed.

1010241802

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *Layers* sample model that comes with the SysML plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='layers.mdzip'] .
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML directory .

1947346737

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024xR1']

/></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filter by Legend Items</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the open dialog, specify the <ac:link ac:anchor="Selecting filtering preference"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtering preference]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the Legend items to filter your diagram by.</span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click <strong>OK</strong>. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Filtering_by_Legend_items_from_the_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></li></ol><h3>Managing and removing the applied filter</h3><p>Once diagram layers are filtered, the following notification appears above the diagram:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="notification_filtering_diagrams_by_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Clear </strong>to clear the applied filter. </p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br">Click <strong>Save</strong> to keep the filter applied after the diagram is reloaded. </p></li></ul><p>Alternatively, to clear the applied filter, you can:</p><ul><li><p>click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulaor_filter_by_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>in the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.</p></li><li><p>uncheck the box next to <strong>Filtered</strong> in the Legend item shortcut menu.</p></li><li><p>clear the corresponding boxes in the <strong>Filter by Legend Items </strong>(<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Filter by Legend Items</strong>) dialog.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="235f807c-52a5-4a82-be07-479a937bd680"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The diagram filtered by Legend items is read-only, meaning that it cannot be edited unless the filter is removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26a85e54-d74a-4dab-bcef-29dcd497037e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010241802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <em>Layers</em> sample model that comes with the SysML plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="layers.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></em>. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open the model from the </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML directory</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10e7f92e-538a-46f2-ac5a-a570dc6ff10b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1947346737</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Mode" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674373 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00508: Filtering table data

- page_id: `169674373`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674373/Filtering+table+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-06T14:37:25.434+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Using wild card

936188854

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Using wild card

936188865

936188853

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Using wild card

936188864

By filtering data in the table you can quickly find the elements you need. This is especially handy when you are working with a large table containing many rows and columns. Elements can be filtered by a phrase specified in the Filter box of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1']. The following figure shows the Generic table before and after filtering.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Filtered rows and columns in the Generic table.

##### Filtering a table

To filter a table

1. In the Table toolbar's Filter box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_settings.png'] icon and specify the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. In the input field, write the keyword to search the table and either press **Enter**or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to see the results. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Disabled filter during table loadingFiltering is disabled if the table is still loading, as indicated by the warning icon [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Once the table is loaded and the warning disappears, either press **Enter** or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to filter the table. You can also track the table loading status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024xR1'], [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon stands for **Loading** and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for **Loading completed**.
3. Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_green_chek_mark.png'] .

##### Specifying filter settingsSpecifying filter settings

The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:

- Specify the columns to search (1). You can search the entire table by selecting the **All** option or selecting one or several particular columns.
- Specify case sensitivity (2).
- Specify wild card or regular expression (3). Using wild cardIn the **Filter**box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “Address” and “ThreadDeath”.“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “Address”. “ThreadDeath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.Be sure the **Match Anywhere**command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!
- Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).
- Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5). 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The main areas in the filter settings.

You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. The filter criteria will be saved even after reloading the project or restarting the modeling tool. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving the filter criteria' space='MD2024xR1'] page.

This is especially handy when you are working with a large table containing many rows and columns. Elements can be filtered by a phrase specified in the Filter box of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The following figure shows the Generic table before and after filtering.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_in_generic_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br />Filtered rows and columns in the Generic table.</h6><h3>Filtering a table</h3><p>To filter a table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Table toolbar's <strong>Filter</strong> box, click the <ac:image ac:title="Filter settings" ac:alt="Filter settings"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_settings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon and specify the <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying filter settings"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filter settings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ac:link>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the input field, write the keyword to search the table and either press <strong>Enter </strong>or click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to see the results. <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0905b272-27a9-4af1-9167-b51a0fb30e77"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Disabled filter during table loading</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Filtering is disabled if the table is still loading, as indicated by the warning icon <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_disabled.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. Once the table is loaded and the warning disappears, either press <strong>Enter</strong> or click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to filter the table. You can also track the table loading status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Table info area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon stands for <strong>Loading</strong> and <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_complete_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> for <strong>Loading completed</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_green_chek_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded46565-d860-442d-b141-11d6d38c90f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specifying filter settings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specifying filter settings</h3><p>The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Filter settings" ac:alt="Filter settings"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_settings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:</p><ul><li><p>Specify the columns to search (1).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40996788-1d4a-46ee-bb72-057e434a5588"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can search the entire table by selecting the <strong>All</strong> option or selecting one or several particular columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify case sensitivity (2).</p></li><li><p>Specify wild card or regular expression (3).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea48d02-4c40-45e4-aaeb-a54461c04c84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using wild card</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Filter </strong>box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:</p><ul><li>“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress” and “Threa<u>dD</u>eath”.</li><li>“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress”. “Threa<u>dD</u>eath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.</li></ul><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Anywhere </strong>command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).</p></li><li><p>Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quickFind_in_generic_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;">The main areas in the filter settings.</h6></li></ul><p>You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. The filter criteria will be saved even after reloading the project or restarting the modeling tool. To learn more, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving the filter criteria" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675417 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00509: Final state

- page_id: `169675417`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675417/Final+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-07T12:11:58.167+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The final state symbol (a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle) is used to represent the object destruction. The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='MD2024xR1']

The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c2481e8e-3300-47ca-a89e-0b94602a4af1"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673618 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00510: Find and Replace dialog

- page_id: `169673618`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673618/Find+and+Replace+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-15T09:10:53.953+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The Find and Replace functionality allows replacing one specified model value with another. You can change the values for the following properties:

- Names
- Documentation
- Tag values
- Text included to Notes
- Text included to Text Boxes
- Expressions

To open the **Find and Replace** dialog, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find and Replace .
- Press Ctrl + R.
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow by the Find button [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] , then select Find and Replace .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following table provides descriptions of the **Find and Replace** dialog components.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Find What | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list to search the value of the element to be replaced. |
| Replace With | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list the value of element that will replace the found value. |
| Options area |  |  |
| Type | Text box with | Click the button and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results. Alternatively, you can leave the default value <any>.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | Click the button and select a package wherein the content search will be performed. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects in read-write mode | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. Used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included into the search, if the load mode of that used project is Manual load. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. |

You may see an error when trying to replace a value with an invalid one.

- For example, if the value type is boolean , you may replace values from true to false or from false to true , but if you will try to replace the value true or false to other, for example, to Motor, an error message will be displayed.
- You will not be allowed to change the Integer value to String when it is a part of the value. For example, if you have the 120 value, trying to replace 20 with AB will cause an error.

You can change the values for the following properties:</p><ul><li><p>Names</p></li><li><p>Documentation</p></li><li><p>Tag values</p></li><li><p>Text included to Notes</p></li><li><p>Text included to
