ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes <span>and custom types</span> of the selected element type in the search results. Alternatively, you can leave the default value &lt;any&gt;.</p><ul><li>Select the<strong> Include Subtypes</strong> check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.</li><li>Select the <strong>Include Custom Types</strong> check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scope</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Text box with <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find and Replace dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Click the button and select a package wherein the content search will be performed.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects in read-write mode</strong></p></td><td><p>Check box</p></td><td><p>Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or <br />used projects that are not loaded. Used project will not be loaded and elements <br />from it will not be included into the search, if the load mode of that used project is <strong>Manual load</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Include elements from standard/system profiles</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to include elements from standard/system profiles.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Include elements from additional content</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select to include additional elements from Smart packages</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> if Smart packages fall into the search scope. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbf620ab-1d54-4292-9984-73fbd674babb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may see an error when trying to replace a value with an invalid one.</p><ul><li>For example, if the value type is <em>boolean</em>, you may replace values from <em>true</em> to <em>false</em> or from <em>false</em> to <em>true</em>, but if you will try to replace the value <em>true</em> or <em>false</em> to other, for example, to <em>Motor,</em> an error message will be displayed.</li><li>You will not be allowed to change the <em>Integer</em> value to <em>String</em> when it is a part of the value. For example, if you have the <em>120</em> value, trying to replace <em>20</em> with <em>AB</em> will cause an error.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673582 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00511: Find dialog

- page_id: `169673582`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673582/Find+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:11:50.238+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760352

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760355

599760349

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760354

#### EXCERPT: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

INLINE

The **Find** dialog provides a quick way to search for specific model elements. Use it to search for elements by name, applied stereotype name, tag value and documentation content. The dialog can be opened from several different places in your modeling tool. To complete a simple search, use the main area of the dialog, or click the **Options** button, to specify criteria that will reduce your search results to only applicable ones.

To search for elements using the Find dialog

1. Open the Find dialog in one of the following ways:

1. 
  - In the main menu, click Edit > Find .
  - In the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='find.png'] .
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+F.
2. Specify the search criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] .
3. If you would like your search to be more specific, click the Options button to expand the **Options** area[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] , and select the desired options.
4. When you finish specifying your search criteria, click the Find button.

After completing the steps above, the search results are displayed in the **Search Results** tab of the model browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Find** dialog with the extended **Options** area.

#### TIP: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Searching tips

- To search for textual information in an active diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar or press Ctrl+F .
- To quickly access your search results, add them to Favorites or save them as the contents of a Smart Package.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

1. In the Search Results tab of the Model browser, select the element(s) you want to add to Favorites. Press and hold the Shift button to select multiple items.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab. Then select Add Selected to Favorites from the menu.

The selected search results are now added to Favorites. They can be accessed by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the desired element under the **Go to Favorites** group.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_search_results_button.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab containing the search results you want to add to a Smart Package.
2. From the open menu, select one of the following:
  - Save Query as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be dynamic. The content of the Smart Package will then be updated according to your search. The specified search criteria will be saved as the Query property value.
  - Save Results as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be static. In this case, your model is not queried, and new elements meeting the specified search criteria are not automatically included in the Smart Package.
3. In the open dialog, select the Package that will own the created Smart Package.

A new Smart Package containing all the search results from the **Search Results** tab is created under the selected owner.

main

##### Main area of the Find dialog

Use the main area of the **Find**dialog to specify the most common search criteria, such as an element name or the scope of the search. The table below provides detailed descriptions of all the elements in the main area.

Find dialog options

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| What | Text box with drop- down list | The What box allows you to search for model elements by the text used in their names or textual properties. To search for desired model elements, enter the text you want to find. To search in element names, select the Search in names check box. To search in textual properties including Documentation, select the Search in all texts check box. Note that both of these options are available only if the Options area is collapsed. To make your search more specific, click as illustrated below.You may use wildcard characters (,?) in your search phrase. "" means zero, one, or more characters. If your search phrase is "dd", all the elements containing "dd" in their names can be found, e.g. "Address" and "ThreadDeath". "?" means exactly one character. If your search phrase is "?dd", only the elements with "dd" in their names, beginning with the second character (e.g., "Address"), can be found. "ThreadDeath" cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase "dd". Be sure to check the Match exactly command when using wildcard characters! |
| Type | Text box with | Click and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | The scope refers to the package of your model in which the content search will be performed. Click and select the desired package. Specify the search scope before opening the Find dialog by performing one of the following:Right-clicking a package in the Model Browser, and selecting Find from its shortcut menu.Selecting a package in the Model Browser and pressing Ctrl + F. |
| Properties | Text box with | The box is available only when the Options area is expanded. Click , select the properties applicable to your search and specify their values. The property list in the open dialog contains only the properties of the element types selected in the Type box. Select at least one element type to make the button available. Keep in mind that the property list contains primitive value properties, e.g. string, boolean, integer, and a non-primitive value property named Applied Stereotype. |

options

##### Options area of the Find dialog

The **Options** area allows you to select from a number of additional search options that will make your search more specific and narrow down the search results.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clear Previous Results | Check box | Select to remove all previous search results from the Search Results tab in the Model Browser. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. If the load mode of the used project is Manual load, the used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included in the search. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. By default, this check box is not selected and elements from Smart packages are excluded from search results. This enables you to avoid search results being duplicated by results outside of Smart packages. |
| Search data unused in diagrams | Check box | Select to search for only those elements that do not have symbols in any diagram. Note that the check box is unavailable if the Find dialog is opened by using the Find in Diagram command in the diagram shortcut menu. |
| Show results in new window | Check box | Select to display the results of every subsequent search in a new Search Results tab of the Model Browser. |

##### Advanced search options

The **Find** dialog offers a number of advanced search options allowing you to narrow down search results. Below are some specific cases illustrating how search criteria can be defined.

To find an element by an element ID

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. In the What box, enter the prefix " xmi.id =" followed by the ID of the element you want to find.
3. Click the Find button. [ATTACHMENT filename='find_dialog_fragment1.png']

To find abstract Classes

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, and select Class .
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Is Abstract property, and set its value to true .
5. Click the Find button.

To find the elements with a specific stereotype and a certain phrase in their documentation

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, select the element type you want to find (e.g, Actor ) and select the Include Subtypes check box.
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded .
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Applied Stereotype property, and select a custom stereotype (e.g., «BusinessActor») as its value.
5. In the same dialog, select the Documentation property and enter a search phrase as its value.
6. Click the Find button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

By default, this check box is not selected and elements from Smart packages are excluded from search results. This enables you to avoid search results being duplicated by results outside of Smart packages.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Search</strong> <strong>data</strong> <strong>unused in diagrams</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to search for only those elements that do not have symbols in any diagram. Note that the check box is unavailable if the <strong>Find</strong> dialog is opened by using the <strong>Find in Diagram</strong> command in the diagram shortcut menu.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Show</strong> <strong>results</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>new window</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to display the results of every subsequent search in a new <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the Model Browser.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Advanced search options</h3><p>The <strong>Find</strong> dialog offers a number of advanced search options allowing you to narrow down search results. Below are some specific cases illustrating how search criteria can be defined.<br /> </p><p>To find an element by an element ID</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>What</strong> box, enter the prefix &quot;<a href="http://xmi.id">xmi.id</a>=&quot; followed by the ID of the element you want to find.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_dialog_fragment1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To find abstract Classes</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, and select <strong>Class</strong>.</li><li>Expand the <strong>Options</strong> area if it is not yet expanded.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Properties</strong> box, select the <strong>Is</strong><strong>Abstract</strong> property, and set its value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To find the elements with a specific stereotype and a certain phrase in their documentation</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, select the element type you want to find (e.g, <strong>Actor</strong>) and select the <strong>Include Subtypes</strong> check box.</li><li>Expand the <strong>Options</strong> area if it is not yet expanded<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);">.</span></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Properties</strong> box, select the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> property, and select a custom stereotype (e.g., «BusinessActor») as its value. <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>In the same dialog, select the <strong>Documentation</strong> property and enter a search phrase as its value.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673616 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00512: Find TODO

- page_id: `169673616`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673616/Find+TODO
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-16T08:20:11.643+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Command **Find TODO** performs a quick search for all *To Do* values.

You can specify *To Do* values in the element Specification window, the **To** **Do** property cell. You can use the **To Do** property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the **To Do** properties defined.

Results of this kind of search are displayed in the **Search Results** tab on the Model Browser.

To start the **Find TODO** search, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find TODO .
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] button and then select Find TODO .

The **Search Results** tab opens with results represented in it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f14189ed-2da7-44ac-8704-8a1bb678b3f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Command <strong>Find TODO</strong> performs a quick search for all <em>To Do</em> values.</p><p>You can specify <em>To Do</em> values in the element Specification window, the <strong>To</strong> <strong>Do</strong> property cell. You can use the <strong>To Do</strong> property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the <strong>To Do</strong> properties defined.</p><p>Results of this kind of search are displayed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab on the Model Browser.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To start the <strong>Find TODO</strong> search, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find TODO" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and then select <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Search Results</strong> tab opens with results represented in it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674868 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00513: Finding predefined diagram aspects

- page_id: `169674868`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674868/Finding+predefined+diagram+aspects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:55.948+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581335849

581335852

581335851

There are predefined aspects that are only included in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] or other customizations. The predefined diagram aspects are the following: <<optical>>, <<mechanical>>, <<electrical>>, <<software>>, <<conceptual>>, and <<logical>>. The procedure below explains how to find them in your project. You can use predefined aspects in your project, or you can create custom aspects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

To find predefined aspects in the project when SysML Plugin is installed

1. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option.
2. Expand packages *MD Customization for SysML* > *additional_stereotypes > aspects*.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The location of the predefined diagram aspects in the project when SysML plugin is installed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581335848

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The predefined diagram aspects are the following: &lt;&lt;optical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;mechanical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;electrical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;software&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;conceptual&gt;&gt;, and &lt;&lt;logical&gt;&gt;. The procedure below explains how to find them in your project. You can use predefined aspects in your project, or you can create custom aspects. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create custom aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To find predefined aspects in the project when SysML Plugin is installed<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Containment tree, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Finding predefined diagram aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>,</span> and select the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Expand packages <em>MD Customization for SysML</em> &gt; <em>additional_stereotypes &gt; aspects</em>.</span></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="predefined_diagram_aspects.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Finding predefined diagram aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>The location of the predefined diagram aspects in the project when SysML plugin is installed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2bd5f95-8800-49ce-b9f4-2345c2e3c53a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581335848</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676086 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00514: Finishing and canceling merge

- page_id: `169676086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676086/Finishing+and+canceling+merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-12-20T12:42:18.767+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge
- labels: ['finish-merging']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Availability

1077613678

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Availability

1077613680

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Availability

1077613679

To finish the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Finish Merging button and a ll accepted changes will be copied to the target.

After the merge results are copied to the project, do not forget to either save or commit the project to the server.

To cancel the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Cancel button. The question dialog box opens asking you if you want to cancel the merge procedure.
  - Click Yes if you are sure that you want to cancel merge procedure.
  - Click No and you will be returned back to the Merge window.

Also you can cancel merge procedure by clicking **Close** button on the **Merge** window.

**#diagramannotationson Turning on merged diagram annotations**

Before finishing the merge procedure, you can show merged diagram annotations. For this, click the **Annotate Merged Diagram** button on the toolbar located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Annotate Merged Diagram button in the Merge Results window toolbar.

#### NOTE: Availability

Availability

This button is available if any positions of symbols in diagrams have been changed in both contributors.

The purpose of the annotation is to mark symbols that have different positions on the same diagrams in ancestor and source projects on a merged diagram. If the Annotate Merged Diagram button is enabled, those symbols are annotated in yellow, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Annotated symbols in a merged diagram.

Click the selected symbol to open a smart manipulator and select a resolution for symbol merging.

1077613677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675126 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00515: Flow Final Node

- page_id: `169675126`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675126/Flow+Final+Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-27T14:56:44.878+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Flow Final Node terminates a flow and destroys all tokens that arrive at it. It has no effect on other flows in the activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of flow final node element notation

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Decision and Merge' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Flow Final Node terminates a flow and destroys all tokens that arrive at it. It has no effect on other flows in the activity.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-19 at 8.05.52 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Flow Final Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of flow final node element notation</h6><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Note" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Decision and Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00516: Flows on decision

- page_id: `169676933`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676933/Flows+on+decision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-30T14:51:25.476+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

DecisionOut

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

DecisionNode

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Decision Node has only one Outgoing Edge. The error is resolved by creating a second Outgoing Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>DecisionOut</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>DecisionNode</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flows_on_decision.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Flows on decision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Decision Node has only one Outgoing Edge. The error is resolved by creating a second Outgoing Edge.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675425 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00517: Fork and Join

- page_id: `169675425`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675425/Fork+and+Join
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-06-19T14:40:13.714+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: ['fork-node', 'join-node']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

727781504

727781506

727781505

The Fork Vertices are used to split an incoming transition into two or more transitions terminating on the orthogonal target vertices (i.e., vertices in different regions of the composite state). The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.

The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.

1. A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.
2. A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.

727781503

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c902cf81-5f32-4707-a698-210d7af051d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781504</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e215b3-7d48-47ad-b630-d0ac85e828fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="014d4885-020e-4033-aa17-cda423d28c88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Fork Vertices are used to split an incoming transition into two or more transitions terminating on the orthogonal target vertices (i.e., vertices in different regions of the composite state). The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.</p><p>The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.</p><ol><li>A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.</li><li>A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dbdf741-78b3-4c5f-9d81-69a5c98c3fd7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675311 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00518: Fork and Join Nodes

- page_id: `169675311`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675311/Fork+and+Join+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-06-19T14:54:35.484+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.

1. A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).
2. A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).

The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the**Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1'] to draw different notations for the**Fork**and**Join**. This option is set to *true*by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example shows the different Fork and Join notations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.</p><ol><li>A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li><li>A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the </span><strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Fork </strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and </span><strong>Join</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. This option is set to <em>true </em>by default.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fork-join.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Fork and Join Nodes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example shows the different Fork and Join notations</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675747 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00519: Formatting symbols

- page_id: `169675747`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675747/Formatting+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-07T13:03:07.616+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2038795777

2038795781

2038795780

| Definition | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Symbol | The term "symbol" means a visual representation of some model elements in the diagram. Symbols are subdivided into shapes and paths (lines in the model, for displaying various relationships). |
| Symbol properties | Every symbol may have its own style: color, font, size, and so forth. Symbol properties may be defined for the concrete symbol, for all symbol of one element, or according to the diagram type. |

You may define the symbol properties in the following ways:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- In the Symbol tab on the Properties panel at the bottom of the Model Browser .
- In the Project Options dialog. Using this dialog you can change all available symbol properties, create your own style for the project, apply different symbol properties for different diagrams, define stereotype properties that may be bound to the symbol, and more.
- On the diagram toolbar. Using this toolbar you can change the color, font, path style of a symbol, and paste the symbol properties.

2038795776

**Related pages**

true****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678171 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00520: Formatting template code

- page_id: `169678171`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678171/Formatting+template+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-17T06:40:49.660+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template language
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports the **Tab** key to help you format template code at ease. The **Tab** key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the **Tab** key after the symbol "#" or "$" followed by the velocity code. The **Tab** key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The velocity code in the figure above uses the **Tab** key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The**Tab** key support is available in *RTF*, *DOCX*, and *ODT* format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports the <strong>Tab</strong> key to help you format template code at ease. The <strong>Tab</strong> key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the <strong>Tab</strong> key after the symbol &quot;#&quot; or &quot;$&quot; followed by the velocity code. The <strong>Tab</strong> key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Indented Template Code " ac:alt="Indented Template Code "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 59.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting template code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The velocity code in the figure above uses the <strong>Tab</strong> key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The<strong> Tab</strong> key support is available in <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX</em>, and <em>ODT</em> format files.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676369 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00521: Forward traceability - realization

- page_id: `169676369`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676369/Forward+traceability+-+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-26T15:42:21.050+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Traceability > Predefined traceability rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The forward traceability ensures that all specified artifacts are covered by elements from the lower abstraction level.

| Property Name | Description | Applied for | Reference through... | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested By Artifacts | The property shows artifacts that physically render the current component. | Component | Relationships: Manifestation | Artifact | Example |
| Realizing Component | The property shows the components representing the class realization in the Implementation model. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Component | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows classifiers that realize components through component realization. | Component | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Class | The property shows the classes representing the use case realization in the Design model. | Use Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Realizing Use Case | The property shows the realizing use cases of the current use case in the lower level of abstraction thus demonstrating how the use case is implemented. For example, the Requirements Use Case realizes the Business Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows the classifiers conforming to the contract specified by the interface and related with this interface through the interface realization relationship. | Interface | Relationships: Interface Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Element, All Realizing Elements.Available in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Realizing Element property gathers the source elements of the abstraction relationship.The All Realizing Elements property gathers recursively (performs a realizing element search for the result) source elements of the abstraction relationship. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675129 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00522: Fragment

- page_id: `169675129`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675129/Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-08T23:58:36.316+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A fragment is an abstract notion of the most general interaction unit. It is a piece of an interaction. Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an alternative flow' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>A fragment is an abstract notion of the most general interaction unit. It is a piece of an interaction. Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.</span></p><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59bc2015-43d9-41f0-a825-33aa65d60798"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related References</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4fbb2ebd-c062-4358-a8cd-b2c2997293bd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="335ed78c-5d96-4333-9fc2-5753c5c4e64f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating an alternative flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Alternatives]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675134 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00524: Function Behavior

- page_id: `169675134`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675134/Function+Behavior
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:05.859+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The function behavior allows modeling external functions that take only inputs and produce outputs. It has no effect on the specified system.

To create a function behavior

1. In the **Model Browser**, right click any root package in your model, in the figure below the root package is *Car*.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'], select **Create Element > Function Behavior [IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a package importing a function.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675634 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00525: Functions of Specification window

- page_id: `169675634`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675634/Functions+of+Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:16:04.683+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Specification window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1291722198

1291722200

1291722199

Using Specification window you can perform various actions that are necessary when working with model elements. Every model element has its own specification.

The following table describes only common functions of the Specification window.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add/modify modelelement propertiesand inner elements | Choose the desired property group and specify its properties in the general specification pane. |
| Open referencedelementspecifications | From the shortcut menu, select Open Specification or click . When another element specification is open, you can work with both specifications in the same window. |
| Navigate betweenopen specifications | To navigate between open specifications, use the Back and Forward buttons. |
| Manage relationships | View all relationships in which the element participates. Modify the name of the relationship.View/change the direction of the relationship.Modify the target element.Create new outgoing or incoming relationships. |
| Add/edit elementdocumentation | Add element description, describe use case scenarios, or add other relevant information that can be used for generating reports or technical documentation. Documentation also can be written in HTML using the . |
| Manage elementhyperlinks | Open, edit, add, or remove from the selected model element to a file, address, other element / symbol, or requirements. |
| Manage element tags and tagged values | Create tag definitions, default tagged values. |
| Manage elementconstraints | to an element. |
| Track elementsymbol usage in diagrams | Find out in which diagrams the symbol is used. Open these diagrams directly from the specification window. |
| Select modelelement in theContainment tree | From the shortcut menu, choose Select in Containment Tree or click . |
| View element traceability | Monitor element changes and the impact of those changes using t. |

Select a specification property and from the shortcut menu, select the actionyou want to perform.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675137 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00526: Gate

- page_id: `169675137`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675137/Gate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:14:58.739+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool allows for displaying the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1']when leaving or entering a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1']. The Gate is a connection point for representing a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] from the outside to the current [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'].

Gates can be used in three cases:

- For passing and returning arguments to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'] , which calls some Interaction.
- For displaying “exceptions“ as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] that stops an interaction execution and leaves it.
- For “calling“ sequence blocks represented as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'] .

Gate has no notation. Gates are created as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] ends when drawing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] to/from a diagram frame, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'], or a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'].

To create a formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] from the diagram frame.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] to the diagram frame.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'] (inside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'] ).

- You can view the created gates of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] in the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. In the **Send [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024xR1']**list box you can see a formal gate. The **Receive**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024xR1']**Event**lists the actual gate.
- The Gate uses text from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.

To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024xR1'] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'] (outside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR1'] ). The Select Formal Gate dialog appears.

You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.

To select a formal gate for the actual gate

1. Do one of the following:
  - Draw a message.
  - From the message shortcut menu, choose Select Formal Gate .
2. In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click OK . An actual gate is created.

The**Select Formal Gates**command is available only if there are formal gates.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']Activity diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The modeling tool allows for displaying the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>when leaving or entering a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Gate is a connection point for representing a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the outside to the current <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Gates can be used in three cases:</p><ul><li>For passing and returning arguments to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[InteractionUse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which calls some Interaction.</li><li>For displaying “exceptions“ as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that stops an interaction execution and leaves it.</li><li>For “calling“ sequence blocks represented as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CombinedFragments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>Gate has no notation. Gates are created as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> ends when drawing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to/from a diagram frame, an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (inside <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b1ee808-c94e-4183-8228-eebc06d67590"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p>You can view the created gates of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. In the <strong>Send <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Event ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>list box you can see a formal gate. The <strong>Receive </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:link-body><strong>Event</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>lists the actual gate.</p></li><li><span>The Gate uses text from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens. </li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (outside <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog appears.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e783d43e-08e7-4010-aa09-1222e7aa2a27"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link><span>, Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To select a formal gate for the actual gate</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Draw a message.</li><li>From the message shortcut menu, choose <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click <strong>OK</strong>. An actual gate is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d5f9bbd-3931-4ac1-ac23-1b54c618de41"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The </span><strong>Select Formal Gates</strong><span> command is available only if there are formal gates.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bcf321e-b978-4308-b7fa-e6212d58c3e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e58255f-45e4-4706-9c1f-f027869c05be"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:link-body>Activity diagram<br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675141 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00527: Generalization

- page_id: `169675141`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675141/Generalization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:17:17.100+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general classifier and a more specific classifier. Each instance of the specific classifier is also an indirect instance of the general classifier. Thus, the specific classifier inherits the features of the more general classifier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Generalizations can be grouped to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization tree' space='MD2024xR1']. 
Generalization may be assigned to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

A generalization can be specified by changing its property values in the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

#### INFO: Sample Model

Sample Model

You can see a generalization relationship sample in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Magic Library.mdzip**.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00529: Generalization Language Properties

- page_id: `169679426`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679426/Generalization+Language+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-22T13:17:06.491+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering > Conversion from an Earlier Project Version > Language Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This is the example of generalization forchildClassclass that extends fromParentClassclass.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Example in Class Diagram

There areInheritance typeandVirtual modifierin the Generalization C++ Language Properties that need to be translated and applied to the«C++Generalization» stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Inheritance visibility

| Generalization - Inheritance visibility type |  |
| --- | --- |
| Old value | Translation |
| public | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to public. |
| protected | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to protected. |
| private | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to private. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Virtual inheritance modifier

| Generalization - Virtual inheritance modifier |  |
| --- | --- |
| Old value | Translation |
| nonvirtual | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set virtual inheritance tag value to false. |
| virtual | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set virtual inheritance tag value to true. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Inheritance visibility type</p></td></tr><tr><th><p><span>Old value </span></p></th><th><p><span>Translation </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>public</p></td><td><p><span>Apply the </span><span>«C++Generalization</span><span>» stereotype and set </span><span>inheritance visibility </span><span>tag value to </span><span>public</span><span>. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>protected</p></td><td><p><span>Apply the </span><span>«C++Generalization</span><span>» stereotype and set </span><span>inheritance visibility </span><span>tag value to </span><span>protected</span><span>. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>private</p></td><td><p><span>Apply the </span><span>«C++Generalization</span><span>» stereotype and set </span><span>inheritance visibility </span><span>tag value to </span><span>private</span><span>. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-18_18-5-15.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization Language Properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Generalization Virtual inheritance modifier</h6><table class="relative-table" style="width: 44.8711%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.295%;" /><col style="width: 72.705%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Generalization - <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675148 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00530: Generalization set

- page_id: `169675148`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675148/Generalization+set
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:06.680+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization set is a packageable element whose instances define collections of subsets of generalization relationships. Each generalization set defines a particular set of generalization relationships that describe the way which a general classifier may be divided using specific subtypes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of *Movie* and *Audiobook***generalization sets

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog. For more information on how to open the generalization set's Symbol Properties dialog, scroll down to read on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can specify generalization set properties in the Generalization Set's Specification window. For more information on how to open the Generalization Set's Specification window, scroll down to read on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024xR1']. In the same window, you can find the description of each Generalization Set property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For a generalization set, use the name of a selected generalization set (not the relationship path) to open the **Symbol Properties** dialog or Specification window.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

##### Creating a new generalization set

You can create a generalization set using a generalization Specification window or a command from the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024xR1'].

To create a new generalization set using the Specification window

1. Select the generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the **Generalization Set**property value cell, click the Edit button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'].
3. Select a generalization set owner and click the Create button. If this command is unavailable, turn on the Creation Mode .
4. In the opened list, click Generalization Set . The Generalization Set Specification window opens.
5. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need. Click Close when you are done.
6. The created generalization set is displayed in the model tree or list and in the Selected elements area of the Select Generalization Set dialog.
7. Select the created generalization set and click **OK**. The generalization set is created and assigned for the current generalization. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Selected Elements

Selected Elements

In the **Generalization Set** property value cell, several generalization sets can be listed, but only the first generalization set name is displayed on the diagram pane near the generalization.

createTo create a new generalization set from the generalization shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Generalization Set . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. If it's already there, select the generalization. Otherwise, click the Create Generalization Set button.
4. The Select Owner dialog opens.
5. In the dialog, select an existing or create a new generalization set owner and click OK when you are done.
6. The Generalization Specification window opens. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need.
7. Click Close when you are done.

#### INFO: Several Generalizations

Several Generalizations

To create a generalization set for several generalizations at a time

1. Select one generalization, then hold down the Shift key and select the other generalizations you desire.
2. While hovering your mouse over one of the generalizations, right click to show the shortcut menu and select Generalization Set.
3. Repeat steps 2-7 from the aforementioned set of instructions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Assigning a generalization set for a generalization

#### WARNING: Assigning a Generalization Set

Assigning a Generalization Set

Before assigning a generalization set, make sure you have at least one generalization set in your model.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the Specification window

1. Select a generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the Generalization Set property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. Select the desired generalization set and click **OK**. The selected generalization set is displayed in the **Generalization Set** property value cell. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'].
4. Close the Specification window.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu
2. Click **Generalization Set**. In the opened Select Generalization Set dialog, select the generalization set you need. If you do not see your desired Generalized Set then you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024xR1'] from the dialog.

#### TIP: Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Draw or move a generalization line to a generalization set tree or to a generalization that belongs to a generalization set (see the following figure). The newly created generalization is assigned to the same generalization set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign a generalization to a particular generalization set

1. Open the Generalization Set Specification window.
2. In the Generalization property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization dialog opens.
3. From the list of generalizations, select all you need and click Apply .

You can select generalizations that belong to other sets. Selecting such a generalization removes it from the previous set and adds it to the current one.

##### Opening the Generalization Set Specification window and Symbol Properties dialog

specificationTo open its Specification window

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, double click on the generalization set name revealing the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Invoking the Generalization Set Specification window from the diagram pane shortcut menu
  1. On the diagram pane, right click the generalization set name revealing its shortcut menu.
  2. Select the Specification command opening the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Opening the Generalization Set Specification window from the Generalization Specification window.
  1. Open the Generalization Specification window by double-clicking on the Generalization arrow.
  2. In the Generalization Specification window, select the Generalization Set property cell.
  3. Select the generalization set you desire.
  4. Right-click to open the generalization set shortcut menu. Select Open Specification .
  5. The Generalization Set Specification window opens.

symbolTo open its Symbol Properties dialog

- On the diagram pane, select the generalization set name and from its shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.

Grouping generalizations to generalization set trees

#### NOTE: Make Generalization Set Tree command

Make Generalization Set Tree command

The Make Generalization Set Tree command is available if the general element has at least two generalizations and at least two generalizations has assigned the same generalization set.

To group the generalizations to the generalization set trees

1. Select a general element on the diagram pane and invoke its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Make Generalization Set Tree .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
