Each generalization set defines a particular set of generalization relationships that describe the way which a general classifier may be divided using specific subtypes.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 9.56.13 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;">Example of <em>Movie</em> and <em>Audiobook</em></span><em style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;"> </em><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;">generalization sets</span></p><p>You can format the generalization symbol properties in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675143 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00531: Generalization tree

- page_id: `169675143`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675143/Generalization+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-07T21:21:39.954+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following

- When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one. [ATTACHMENT filename='connect_generalization_to_first.png']
- If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:
  - Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_generalization.png']
  - Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select Refactor > Make Sub Tree . [ATTACHMENT filename='make_sub_tree.png']
  - Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.

To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following

- Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click Ungroup tree .
- Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_generalization_to_first.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_generalization.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Make Sub Tree</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_sub_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><p>To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click <strong>Ungroup tree</strong>.</li><li>Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db7e8412-9f3a-4180-b306-a9dae86f712e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Formatting Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalization Set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678057 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00532: Generate Output pane

- page_id: `169678057`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678057/Generate+Output+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-05T10:04:46.596+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Generate Output** pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Generate Output pane.

The table below describes the function of each option in the **Generate Output** pane of Report Wizard.

| Component Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report file | Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be \data\template_folder\reports\. The default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. |
|  | Opens the Select Location dialog in order to locate the report file. |
| Report image format | Selects an image format for your report: JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF, or WMF.Note:Use *.JPG and *.PNG for any template format.Use *.SVG for text, HTML, DOCX, and DocBooK templates.Use *.EMF and *.WMF for text and Microsoft Office templates (RTF, DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX). |
| Auto image size | Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:No Resize-Image will not be resized or rotated.Fit image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit to within the paper size.Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit towithin the paper size and rotated clockwise.Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)- Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise. |
| Display empty value as | Empty text-Leave an empty value of the report output blank.Custom text as-Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is NA. |
|  | Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location. |
|  | Generates a report. |
|  | Cancels the report generation process and closes the Report Wizard dialog. |
|  | Provides the Help content. |

Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:

- Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example: Using normal 
codeUsing macro 
code#if (!$var || $var == "")Empty#end#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)#if (!var || $var == "")EMPTY#end#end

- Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.

Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard environment options' space='MD190SP1']*).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Generate Output</strong> pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate Output Pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Generate Output pane.</h6><p> The table below describes the function of each option in the <strong>Generate Output</strong> pane of Report Wizard.</p><table style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Component Name </th><th>Description</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Report file</td><td>Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be <em>\data\template_folder\reports\</em>. The <br />default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selectfilelocation_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Opens the <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog in order to locate the report file.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Report image format</td><td><p>Selects an image format for your report: <em>JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF</em>, or <em>WMF</em>.</p><p>Note:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Use <em>*.JPG</em> and <em>*.PNG</em> for any template format.</li><li>Use <em>*.SVG</em> for text,<em> HTML</em>,<em> DOCX</em>, and<em> DocBooK</em> templates.</li><li>Use <em>*.EMF</em> and <em>*.WMF</em> for text and Microsoft Office templates (<em>RTF, DOCX, XLSX,</em> and <em>PPTX</em>).</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Auto image size</td><td><p>Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><strong>No Resize-</strong>Image will not be resized or rotated.</li><li><strong>Fit image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to within the paper size.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to<br />within the paper size and rotated clockwise.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong> <br />Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise.</li></ol></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Display empty value as</td><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Empty text-</strong>Leave an empty value of the report output blank.</li><li><strong>Custom text as-</strong>Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is <strong>NA</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Generates a report.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Cancels the report generation process and closes the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Help_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1">Provides the Help content.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ae31dea-c121-4d00-b575-7d8b1cd6fe74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a template code to replace empty value with text. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676085 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00533: Generating a differences report

- page_id: `169676085`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676085/Generating+a+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-02T08:43:50.147+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1056652507

1056652509

1056652508

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the toolbar that is located at the top of the Merge window. [ATTACHMENT filename='merge_toolbar_report.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Merged Result tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files located in *<configuration files directory>\data\merge reports* are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.

You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Creation Tutorial' space='MD2024xR1']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

1056652506

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676903 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00534: Generating a project comparison differences report

- page_id: `169676903`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676903/Generating+a+project+comparison+differences+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-12-16T09:56:00.326+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the Difference Viewer toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='difference_viewer_toolbar.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Differences tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678509 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00535: Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server

- page_id: `169678509`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678509/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:27.464+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1300364630

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1300364632

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1300364631

INLINE

Apart from the mandatory commands described in [using the Generate command to generate reports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports), you can use the following command arguments in the command line to generate a report from the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) server.

- -server , -login , and -password are key arguments to log on to TWCloud server. You will be prompted for a password when trying to enter -server and -login without a password.
- -pversion is a key argument to specify a TWCloud project version. If either you do not specify any version, or the version you specify does not match any version in the TWCloud repository, Report Wizard will generate the latest project version of the specified branch.
- -branch option is used to retrieve a particular project branch. If you do not specify any branch, the trunk would be specified.
- -updatemodule A Boolean parameter. True for updating the used module of the project to the latest version. False by default, when the user does not specify this option in the command.
- **-tag** is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with "**-incremental true**". If you use this command with **-branch,**it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.
- **-projectpassword** is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.
- **-usagepackage** specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).
- **-usageelement** specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of TWCloudproject to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;). NoteFor TWCloud projects, **-package** / **-element** is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement**. You can also set **-includeAuxiliary** as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement** argument can be used with **-package** / **-element** to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.

If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a TWCloud project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:

```text

```

The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.

```text

```

The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.

```text

```

The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.

```text

```

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.

#### WARNING: Note

Note

If the project version specified is not the latest, the **-updatemodule**option would not affect the **generate** command.

1300364629

INLINE

False by default, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">when the user does not specify this option in the command.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-tag</strong> is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with &quot;<strong>-incremental true</strong>&quot;. If you use this command with <strong>-branch, </strong>it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-projectpassword</strong> is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-usagepackage</strong> specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-usageelement</strong> specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">TWCloud </span>project to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be995a6d-4ed9-42c1-884b-eb5c3f74649a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For TWCloud projects, <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify <strong>-usagepackage</strong> / <strong>-usageelement</strong>. You can also set <strong>-includeAuxiliary</strong> as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This <strong>-usagepackage</strong> / <strong>-usageelement</strong> argument can be used with <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">TWCloud</span> project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53949471-b76f-485d-bfd2-cb06bc32f615"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cc1b800-0b39-449e-bfe5-ba4d982c46cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -branch "MyBranch" -pversion "10" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2dfab75-3939-4e1a-a12b-b8c927f9024a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -branch "TestingLine" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/out.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "SamplePackage" -server "localhost" -servertype "twcloud" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee12cf15-5bf9-4ede-b971-8d33027950e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed35b12a-2ffc-4a6f-8a06-4c5ee2481ba6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f0dbc4-ad1d-4a8a-b341-47c56585c0bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecb64675-b957-4281-a946-f304f4ed54d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3722217c-7af2-4045-a2b2-6285ef6362d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/output.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="210bd89b-d369-4eee-9b3c-ca83ca4388f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -package "Model" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -server "localhost:1111" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -ssl "true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f0749cc-3915-449e-a973-8cf4f323074c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff858fc3-8f37-4343-9afc-b16bacf07a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If the project version specified is not the latest, the <strong>-updatemodule </strong>option would not affect the <strong>generate</strong> command.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5aaa2cc5-47bf-43a8-9869-6e1e7b233c49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1300364629</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties filename" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading generated reports to servers" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Syntax rules" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679243 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00536: Generating Code

- page_id: `169679243`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679243/Generating+Code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:41:38.627+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824354040

824354042

824354039

**Demo**

- Code Generation

824354041

You can generate code for the selected and prepared set as well as directly for model elements.

##### Code Generation for a Set

Start code generation once the set or sets are prepared. For more details about creating and editing sets, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space='MD2024xR1'].

- Choose **Generate** from the **Code Engineering Sets** item shortcut menu. It allows code generating for all created sets.
- Choose **Generate** from the selected set shortcut menu. It allows code generating only for the selected set.

The **Code Generation Options** dialog appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Generation Options dialog

The **Code Generation Options** dialog allows you to specify the way your code will be generated.

Once you specify generating options for the set, code can be generated.

| Box Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Output Directory | Type the directory where the generated files will be saved. |
|  | The Set Output Directory dialog appears. Select the directory for the generated files. |
| Set as Working Directory | The output directory is set as a working directory and files are saved to the working directory. |
| Reverse before generation | Changes your model according to changes in the existing code.Exercise caution when selecting the Reverse before generation check box. If the model differs from the code, all differences in the model will be discarded. In these cases, you will lose some of your work. |
| If element deleted from model | To influence the structure of generated code, click one of the following option buttons:Delete code. The representation of deleted entities will be deleted from the code file.Comment code. Deleted entities will be commented in the code file. |
| Use spaces in place of tabs | When selected, spaces (instead of tabs) will be written to the code file. |
| Number of spaces | Specify the number of spaces to be written. |
| OK | The Notification Window appears, displaying how code files are being generated. The Notification Window informs you about problems and errors (mainly file access and syntax errors) found in the code generation process and generation summary. You are also prompted to confirm that you wish to overwrite the file if the output directory already contains one with the same name. |
| Cancel | Closes the dialog without saving changes. |
| Help | Displays Help |

Code Generation for a Model Element

All the classes contained in the component will be written into one file. However, code for the class can be generated in a different way. Select the class to generate in the browser Data package and click Generate in the class shortcut menu. For packages and components, you can also select **Generate**, but you will not be able to specify the generation options. All the options related to that task will be set according to the default values.

If you choose framework generation for a single class or for packages, the **Code Generation Options** dialog does not appear. The code is generated according to the default values.

If no errors occurred, you can view the results with your favorite file viewer or programming environment. Look for the files in the directory you specified as your **Working directory** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space='MD2024xR1']**Round Trip Set** dialog or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options** dialog. Additional sub-directories could be created.

418416384

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678255 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00537: Generating Reports from Report Wizard

- page_id: `169678255`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678255/Generating+Reports+from+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:16:48.585+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261051786

1261051787

1261051787

In addition to default document templates, Report Wizard allows you to create customized specification document templates. Templates may contain your own custom fields not related to model elements, as well as fields that correspond to model elements. Once customized, a Report Wizard template can be used with data from any project.

You can also format your template to create the style you want, including tables of contents, headers and footers, and page numbers. You can apply most character and paragraph formatting available from your rtf editor to the rtf template or specify with html tags in the HTML template, including keywords. Once the template has been completed, you can run the Report Wizard to create a report in a format that corresponds to the template.

Report Wizard templates can be in *txt, rtf, html, odt, odf, odp, docx, xlsx, pptx*, and *xml* for DocBook or FO files. If you prefer, you can work in the Report Wizard Template Editor until you have finished the template file and use the Report Wizard template windows to save it in the template folder. The default templates are located in the *<modeling tool home>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/data* folder.

All built-in rtf report templates are combined into the RTF (deprecated) report template category.

##### Synchronizing an attached template with a server project

You can [attach a local template](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR2/Template+Management+Pane) to a server project. Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.

Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the locally attached template from a server project

1. Go to **Options > Environment** menu.

2. Select the **Report Wizard** pane.

3. In the Configure report properties table, set the **synchronizeAttachedTemplate** value to **true.**

This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.

To update the server project with a locally attached template

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2024xR1'].

2. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog.

3. Select the attached template and click **Open**.

4. Edit the attached template and click **Save**.

5. Click **Synchronize**.

6. Commit changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1261051785

**Related pages**

Templates may contain your own custom fields not related to model elements, as well as fields that correspond to model elements. Once customized, a Report Wizard template can be used with data from any project.</p><p>You can also format your template to create the style you want, including tables of contents, headers and footers, and page numbers. You can apply most character and paragraph formatting available from your rtf editor to the rtf template or specify with html tags in the HTML template, including keywords. Once the template has been completed, you can run the Report Wizard to create a report in a format that corresponds to the template.</p><p>Report Wizard templates can be in <em>txt, rtf, html, odt, odf, odp, docx, xlsx, pptx</em>, and <em>xml</em> for DocBook or FO files. If you prefer, you can work in the Report Wizard Template Editor until you have finished the template file and use the Report Wizard template windows to save it in the template folder. The default templates are located in the <em>&lt;modeling tool home&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/data</em> folder.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c192dcad-3ada-473f-9ccf-e19390e4f3b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All built-in rtf report templates are combined into the RTF (deprecated) report template category.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Synchronizing an attached template with a server project</h3><p>You can <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR2/Template+Management+Pane">attach a local template</a> to a server project. Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.</p><p>Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.</p><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-2-28_9-0-27.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To update the locally attached template from a server project</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Go to <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Options &gt; Environment</strong> menu.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Select the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> pane.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. In the Configure report properties table, set the <strong>synchronizeAttachedTemplate</strong> value to <strong>true.</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To update the server project with a locally attached template</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock an attached template for editing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Select the attached template and click <strong>Open</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">4. Edit the attached template and click <strong>Save</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">5. Click <strong>Synchronize</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">6. Commit changes.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-2-28_9-1-16.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="754c83d6-7560-4c92-945d-cafd5d27156c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1261051785</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="61c65847-44e5-4af4-9976-19d24cdef47c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

To generate reports and schedule report printing, type the command in the terminal. This feature allows you to generate reports without opening the application and using Report Wizard.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678131 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00539: Generating reports from the Containment tree

- page_id: `169678131`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678131/Generating+reports+from+the+Containment+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:59:58.432+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a Report Data in your model, you can generate a report directly from the Containment tree. You can right-click either Model or Report Data to generate it.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024xR1'] .

You must specify the template and data in the report data to generate a report from the **Report Data** in the Containment tree.

###### GenerateFromModel

To generate a report from Model in the Containment tree

1. Right-click a package in the Containment tree, select Generate Report , and then select a template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a report from Model in the Containment tree.

2. Select the save location and type the filename. A dialog will open asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer after the report generating process is complete.

3. Click either [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1'] page to learn more.

###### GenerateFromReportData

To generate a report from Report Data in the Containment tree

1. Create a project.
2. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project . The Use Project dialog will open.
3. Select the predefined path: <install.root>\profiles** and the predefined profile: Report Profile
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Finish_button.png'] . The Report Profile will be added to your project in read-only mode.
5. Right-click Model and select Create Element .
6. Select **Report Data**. **Report Data** will be created under Model. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating report data in the Model in the Containment tree.
7. Right-click the Report Data in the Containment tree, and select Specification to open its Specification dialog.
8. Select a template file, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a template file for the Report Data using the Specification dialog.
9. Right-click the Class element in the Containment tree, and select **Quick Generate Report**. The **Select Location** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Generating a report from Report Data in the Containment tree.
10. Either create a new file, or select an existing one (the content of the generated report will override that of the selected file).

- To open the Report Wizard dialog and modify the data or output properties, click the Report Wizard on the shortcut menu.
- By default, the Quick Generate Report command will select the default settings from the last changes saved to the Report Data .

#### Glossary

- Model The package that is placed at the highest level of the Containment tree.

- Report data A collection of variables in a report in Report Wizard.

- Report template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about generating reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about generating reports using Report Wizard, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="QuickGenerateReport_menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Generating a report from Report Data in the Containment tree.</h6></li><li>Either create a new file, or select an existing one (the content of the generated report will override that of the selected file).<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6a9ceee-93e3-4ff8-a55f-08611cbf23a3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and modify the data or output properties, click the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> on the shortcut menu.</li><li>By default, the <strong>Quick Generate Report</strong> command will select the default settings from the last changes saved to the <strong>Report Data</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Model</strong><br />The package that is placed at the highest level of the Containment tree.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>Report data</strong><br />A collection of variables in a report in Report Wizard.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>Report template<br /></strong>A<span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> file that serves as a starting point for a new report.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><h2>More information about generating reports in Report Wizard</h2><p>To learn more about generating reports using Report Wizard, you can visit <span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link>.<br /></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678267 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00540: Generating Use Case Description Reports

- page_id: `169678267`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678267/Generating+Use+Case+Description+Reports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:27.086+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1333739779

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1333739781

1333739777

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1333739781

#### Use Case Report Template

Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.

To generate a Use Case description document

1. Open the *Magic Library.mdzip* sample model from the directory. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.
2. Click Tools > Report Wizard to open the Report Wizard dialog.
3. Select a template, for example, **Default Template** > **Use Case (Simple),** and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. You can choose to: You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the **Report Variable** dialog.
  1. Select the built-in Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the Built-in Report Data in the Report Variable dialog by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='Variable_button.png'] or
  2. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create its report variables. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the Built-in report data. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a custom report data . [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Report Variable dialog.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The Select Element Scope option will open in the Report Wizard dialog.
6. Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip] model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.
7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The model package will be moved to the **Selected objects** tree on the right-hand side. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The output options pane will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.
9. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Browse_bigbutton.png'] to specify the location in which you want to save the report.
10. Select the file location, enter the filename, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is **.rtf*, the report file format will be **.rtf* as well.
11. Select the report image format, **.png*, **.jpg*, **.emf*, or**.wmf,*and select an image size option. TipIn some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select either the **Empty text** or **Custom text** option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.
13. Select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the report document with a default editor. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_button.png'] .

The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page [Default templates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Default+templates).

#### Glossary

- Use case A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.

- Model Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.

- Report data Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']

#### More Information on Generating Reports

You can generate different types of reports with Report Wizard. Learn more about generating the other types of reports on the following pages.

- Web Publisher 2.0 Report
- Web Portal Reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32ad4b5c-ce11-4d86-bfc4-4e301df9ea53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333739779</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dee3a14c-7536-47bf-8cfb-97117d16dc62"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333739781</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3724a5f0-beb7-4786-880c-11ac32c0945b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333739777</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a1be0ad-55da-4dc1-9289-370afccc5427"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56906b76-39b3-450b-bf12-3c318e60f690"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333739781</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use Case Report Template</span></h2><p>Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To generate a Use Case description document</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the <em>Magic Library.mdzip</em> sample model from the directory.<br /><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="magic-lib.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cafde055-4be2-48bd-9c97-db483f26c062"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard </strong>to open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Select a template, for example, <strong>Default Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Case (Simple),</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseCase_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>You can choose to:<br /><ol><li>Select the <strong>built-in</strong> Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the <strong>Built-in</strong> Report Data in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />or</li><li><p>Click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create its report variables.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Built-in Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting the Built-in Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report-wizard-dialog-two.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the Built-in report data.</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting a Custom Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting a Custom Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rep-wiz-step-two.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting a custom report data .</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="The Report Variable Dialog" ac:alt="The Report Variable Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage-report-variables.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Report Variable dialog.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3e62168-5bb4-403d-81cb-0df77361b8c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> option will open in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li>Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the <em>MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip]</em> model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.</li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The model package will be moved to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> tree on the right-hand side.</p><h6><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Scope of the Report" ac:alt="Selecting the Scope of the Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-element-scope.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The output options pane will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.<br /><br /></h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to specify the location in which you want to save the report.</li><li><p>Select the file location, enter the filename, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is <em>*.rtf</em>, the report file format will be <em>*.rtf</em> as well.</p></li><li><p>Select the report image format, <em>*.png</em>, <em>*.jpg</em>, <em>*.emf</em>, or<em> *.wmf, </em>and select an image size option.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78c70840-9f0c-4cda-8798-2fde5f502217"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The <strong>Display empty value as</strong> option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Select either the <strong>Empty text</strong> or <strong>Custom text</strong> option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.</p></li><li><p>Select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the report document with a default editor.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Default+templates">Default templates</a>.</p><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Glossary</span></h2><ul><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Use case</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.<br /></span></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Model</strong><br />Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>Report data</strong><br />Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.</li></ul><h2>Sample model</h2><p> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></p><hr /><h2>More Information on Generating Reports</h2><p>You can generate different types of reports with Report Wizard. Learn more about generating the other types of reports on the following pages.</p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Web+Publisher+2.0+Report">Web Publisher 2.0 Report</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Web+portal+reports">Web Portal Reports</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675886 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00541: Generic numbering mechanism

- page_id: `169675886`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675886/Generic+numbering+mechanism
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:21:40.002+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: ['numbering', 'numbering-scheme', 'numbering-customization', 'numbering-property', 'element-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1367060059

1367060061

1367060060

INLINE

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show an element's place in a hierarchy.

With this feature you can easily:

- Customize currently applied numbering formats.
- Create and apply your own numbering formats.
- Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.

The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with generic numbering mechanism' space='MD2024xR1'] describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.

Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Numbering customization | A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator . |
| Numbering property | Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering. |
| Numbering scheme | A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization. |
| Prefix | An affix placed before the element number. |
| Separator | A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme. |

1367060058

**Related pages**

The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:</p><ul><li>Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.</li><li>Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.</li><li>Show an element's place in a hierarchy.</li></ul><p>With this feature you can easily:</p><ul><li>Customize currently applied numbering formats.</li><li>Create and apply your own numbering formats.</li><li>Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.</li></ul><p>The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with generic numbering mechanism" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Generic numbering mechanism]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /></ac:link></span> explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.</li></ul><p>Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Concept</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><h4>Numbering customization</h4></td><td><p>A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creates the numbering customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Numbering property</h4></td><td><p>Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering.</p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Numbering scheme</h4></td><td><p>A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Prefix</h4></td><td colspan="1">An affix placed before the element number.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Separator</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca049e93-1471-430e-8310-f2452f1fa12f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1367060058</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a45e48f4-242a-47e7-a18c-67ba18058c6c" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00542: Generic table

- page_id: `169674388`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674388/Generic+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:52.456+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1198506714

1198506717

1198506711

**On this page**

42

1198506716

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Generic Table gives you the ability to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place.

##### **Purpose**

A Generic Table displays a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place. Using Generic Tables enables you to:

- Edit property values of listed model elements.
- Create selected type elements.
- Create new derived properties or custom columns.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Generic Table project sample allows you to read about the Generic Table feature and analyze examples within that project sample. 
To open the sample, do either:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples > Diagrams > Generic table .
- Go to < the modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams and open the generic table.mdzip file.

All the examples given in this section are based on the data from this sample.

##### **Specific tasks in Generic Table**

All procedures dedicated only for Generic Table are as follows:

1199743192

## PAGE 00543: Generic table tool

- page_id: `169678664`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678664/Generic+table+tool
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:28.099+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352031231

1352031232

1352031232

The Generic Table tool allows templates to:

- Access data of the Generic Table and other Table diagrams, e.g., Instance Table, Metric Table, and Glossary Table. See more samples in the tutorial.
- Use diagrams to get data from the Generic Table.
- Get the row elements.
- Get the column names.
- Get the cell values.
- Get column levels for the Generic Table tree view.
- Check for annotation values in table cells.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Dialog.

Like other Custom Tools, the Generic Table tool '*generictabletool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom Tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='MD2024xR1'].*

To import the Generic Table tool to a template, for example, type the following code in the template.

```text

```

For further information on Custom Tools and the installation, see section <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Custom tool" /></ac:link>.</em></p><p>To import the Generic Table tool to a template, for example, type the following code in the template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10aaa0ae-06ad-4d6a-be7f-6727234c6fa4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('generic', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.GenericTableTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d60ab522-2479-4e99-b11a-a3a1032a4347"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352031231</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d3e2efc6-5a8e-4bd9-8a64-a420c0fb526e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
